name: Transcontinental Race
tagline: 4,000-5,000km self-supported across Europe. The clock never stops. The ultimate test of human endurance, navigation, and self-reliance.

name: Tour Divide
location: Banff, Alberta to Antelope Wells, New Mexico
tagline: 2,745 miles from Banff to Mexico. The longest mountain bike time trial on earth. Self-supported. Unsupported. Unforgettable.
st: The Tour Divide is a self-supported off-road race from Banff, Alberta to Antelope Wells, New Mexico. Current record stands at 13 days 2 hours. Riders face significant wildlife including bears, mountain lions, grizzly bears, elk, moose, deer, coyotes, antelope, and wild horses. Wildlife encounters require constant vigilance with bears being the primary concern. Wildfire closures are increasingly common due to climate change, forcing major reroutes like the Hay Creek Fire near Whitefish Montana that blocked Red Meadow Lake Road. Smoke conditions can severely impact breathing, especially for riders with asthma. The terrain starts in remote British Columbia with familiar conditions for locals. Essential gear includes rain trousers, and riders choose between fully rigid or full suspension setups. Record attempts require riding 200 miles daily for 14 days with minimal sleep. The route covers diverse terrain from Canadian mountains to southwestern desert, with riders experiencing dramatic transitions from wilderness to civilization.

name: Unbound Gravel 200
tagline: The Super Bowl of gravel. 200 miles through Kansas Flint Hills. Where legends are made and dreams die in the dust.
st: There's a moment around mile 120 where the Flint Hills just open up in every direction and you realize there is absolutely nowhere to hide from the wind. That's when Unbound starts. I DNF'd this race in 2019. Coming back five years later and crossing that line under ten hours — that's why you keep showing up to Emporia.

name: Traka 360
tagline: Europe's Unbound. 360km through Catalonia from dawn to dusk to dawn. Where WorldTour pros meet gravel specialists.
st: Traka 360 is described as the European version of Unbound with thousands of racers. The race starts in darkness through a grass field with technical navigation over curbs and roundabouts before hitting dirt. The first major challenge comes at mile 10-12 with loose cutback descents causing crashes and field separation. The terrain features continuous real gravel that gets muddy. Riders emphasize nutrition as critical, calling it an eating competition rather than cycling race, recommending whole foods like Pringles and variety at feed zones. Successful tire setups include Thunderbird 2.2 front with Schwalbe G1 Air 50mm rear for descent control. The field strength increased when 560k riders moved down to 360k distance. The event treats men and women equally with amazing atmosphere and incredible scenery around Girona.

name: Cheaha Challenge Gran Fondo
tagline: 126 miles of Alabama savagery with 13,000ft that crushes souls twice over Cheaha.

name: Colorado Trail Race
tagline: 530 miles of high-altitude suffering across the spine of the Rockies - 75% of starters don't finish, and that's the point.
st: The Colorado Trail Race is a 530-mile journey from Durango to Denver with 72,500 feet of elevation gain. Riders describe challenging conditions including very hot weather requiring frequent water consumption and cooling off strategies. The trail features steep hike-a-bike sections that require walking bikes up and down, rocky terrain that can damage equipment, and dense vegetation described as Colorado jungle. Course challenges include significant climbing with riders gaining over 2000 feet in early sections, reaching elevations around 7500 feet. Equipment considerations include hardtail mountain bikes with front suspension working well, and low-maintenance drivetrains being advantageous. The rocky terrain is tough enough to break shifters if bikes are dropped. Winning times have been recorded under four days, with course records around 3 days 19 hours. Navigation requires GPS devices and phone apps with turn-by-turn directions. Individual time trial options exist for equipment failures, allowing starts up to 24 hours after the official race beginning.

name: Le Loop
tagline: Ride the full Tour de France route—3,429km, 172,566ft of hellish glory—one week ahead of the pros.

name: Trans Am Bike Race
tagline: 4,200 miles from the Pacific to the Atlantic. The longest road ultra-race on earth. Self-supported across 10 states. America, coast to coast.
st: The Trans Am Bike Race is a 4,200+ mile self-supported route from Astoria Oregon to Yorktown Virginia following the American Cycling Association trail. Riders mention the challenging Missouri rollers as a difficult terrain section. Weather conditions can be extreme - one rider noted unusual weather patterns lasting 40 years. Navigation challenges are common with GPS devices failing or lacking proper US maps, making paper cue sheets essential backup. Mechanical failures occur, with one rider's bike exploding into single-speed mode at 100 miles. The route involves rural areas where traffic stops for moving cattle. Safety equipment is critical including multiple high-powered lights, reflective tape, and dynamo charging systems. Riders use various directions with some going east to west to encounter other participants along the way.

name: UCI Gravel World Championships
tagline: UCI World Championship—180km elite, rainbow jersey, fastest field in gravel, rotating locations.

name: Unbound XL
tagline: 350 miles of Kansas limestone hell with zero aid stations. The ultimate test of whether you actually like riding bikes or just think you do.
st: Unbound XL in Emporia, Kansas is described as one of the toughest gravel races in the world, featuring 350 miles of brutal terrain with 14,000 feet of elevation. The course includes sharp rocks, rolling hills, and varied sections - some known for high speeds, others for constant punctures, and some for being particularly gritty and tough. Heat becomes a major challenge as the day progresses, often causing riders to crack late in the race. Early crashes around miles 7-8 are common when riders aren't expecting them. The XL distance is fully self-supported, requiring riders to stop at grocery stores and markets for supplies, while the 200-mile race has two designated sag stops. Environmental challenges include wind, rain, mud, and extreme heat. Riders emphasize the importance of mental preparation for handling multiple setbacks over such a long distance.

name: Badlands
tagline: 800km self-supported through Spanish deserts and 3,400m alpine passes. Where ultra-endurance meets extreme heat.
st: Badlands is an 800km ultra-distance race through southern Spain's diverse terrain including the Tabernas desert, Europe's only desert. The route features massive early climbing with the first 300km being essentially one big climb, starting with 1,000m elevation gain in just 28km. Key challenges include a brutal 14km climb with 1,100m gain after Tabernas at mile 217, night riding at 2,000m altitude in freezing conditions, and a 3-hour rock garden climb from the coast back to the mountains. The desert section spans 80km with temperatures reaching 46°C and technical terrain with rivets that can catch wheels. Riders emphasize the inclusive ultra community atmosphere and recommend starting conservatively, carrying 35+ hours of fuel, extra electrolytes for heat, and planning 90g carbs per hour initially. The route covers dramatic landscape variety from desert to forest to coastal roads.

name: Crusher in the Tushar
tagline: The toughest 70 miles on the planet. 10,000ft of climbing. Finish at 10,400ft elevation.
st: Crusher in the Tushar is described as packing more suffering per mile than any other event, with 69 miles and 10,000 feet of climbing featuring loose, gnarly gravel throughout. The course has three main sections: an initial climb, a middle section with downhill and flats, and a final massive climb. The valley section between towns is mentally the hardest part, described as mind-numbing with windy conditions that isolate riders. The final climb is so steep it reduces riders to granny gear, going slow enough for flies to land on them. Riders debate tire choices around 38mm width, with some preferring gum wall options. The mountaintop finish and combination of elevation gain, altitude, heat, and challenging surfaces create what organizers call a race that throws the kitchen sink at competitors. The event is popular because while brutally hard, it's finishable, giving riders a sense of accomplishment.

name: Leadville Trail 100 MTB
tagline: The Race Across the Sky. 100 miles at 10,000+ feet. The belt buckle that defines mountain biking.
st: The Leadville Trail 100 MTB is a 100-mile dirt road race done on mountain bikes at extreme altitude, ranging from 10,000 to 12,000 feet elevation with roughly 12,000 feet of climbing. The notorious Coline climb peaks at 12,500 feet and represents the race's signature challenge. Riders describe the terrain as more dirt roads than traditional mountain biking. The altitude creates massive power drops and requires different approaches to acclimatization, pacing, and nutrition compared to sea level racing. Starting in 1994, this 30th anniversary edition maintains its reputation as one of the most prestigious cross-country mountain bike events globally. Riders emphasize the need to commit fully to race decisions from the start, as the unforgiving nature means you must live with your choices. The race attracts bold people willing to put everything on the line and risk failure. Those completing the course under 12 hours receive coveted gold and silver belt buckles, representing one of cycling's most iconic achievements in the Colorado high peaks.

name: Belgian Waffle Ride
tagline: The most technical gravel race in America. 130 miles of everything—road, dirt, singletrack, sand, suffering.
st: The Belgian Waffle Ride California features a major course change with no more Double Peak finish climb, now ending with more gravel sections instead. Riders describe terrain that alternates between baby butt smooth pavement and chunky mud laden whoops that Motocross riders train on. Key challenges include the first off-road sector where positioning is critical, Lemon Twistenberg where lead groups get blown apart, and The Wash - a final deep sand section just meters from the finish that causes cramping riders to tip over. Most riders now use 40-45mm gravel-specific tires rather than the wider road tires of previous years. The course designer calls it unroad rather than gravel, with decisive sections favoring mountain bikers despite significant road portions. Race strategy has shifted since riders no longer need to save energy for the brutal Double Peak finish climb.

name: La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes
tagline: Four Tour de France cols. 17,000 feet of climbing. Finish atop Alpe d'Huez. The original French cyclosportive.

name: Mallorca 312 OK Mobility
tagline: 312 kilometers around cycling's Mediterranean paradise. Serra de Tramuntana climbs, closed roads, 8,500 riders, and a finish that makes you forget every watt of suffering.

name: Torino Nice Rally
tagline: 700km crossing the Alps from Italy to France on legendary military roads. Where bikepacking pilgrimage meets high-altitude adventure.
st: The Torino Nice Rally is a 700km route with 18,000m of climbing from Turin to Nice, featuring 50% gravel terrain that becomes increasingly technical with big rocks rather than just gravel. The route traverses high Alpine military roads mostly over 2000m elevation through national parks between Italy and France. The notorious Colombardo climb is the first major challenge, taking up to 8 hours and forcing many riders to walk sections due to its extreme steepness and altitude. Other key climbs include Col de la Finestra and the dangerous Death Road, closed due to rockfall risk. Technical descents require intense concentration at high speeds. The rally format allows riders to choose their own route and pace, taking 5-10 days to complete. Accommodations vary from mountain refuges to wild camping. Riders emphasize constant eating and frequent stops at restaurants. The remote, lonely sections through beautiful Alpine scenery provide a meditative experience above 2000m elevation.

name: Big Sugar Gravel
tagline: The Life Time Grand Prix finale—where the season ends and titles are decided on Ozark gravel.
st: Big Sugar Gravel in Bentonville, Arkansas is known for its challenging terrain featuring category 2-3 gravel with particularly sharp rocks that frequently cause flat tires. The course typically covers 100 miles with 9,500 feet of elevation, though weather conditions can force race organizers to shorten it to 50 miles for safety. The shorter course is notably smoother than the full 100-mile route. Pros recommend endurance-oriented tire casings to handle the sharp rock sections. The race serves as the finale of the Lifetime Grand Prix series, attracting 2,500 riders and featuring $300,000 in prize money. Weather conditions including rain and potential severe storms significantly impact race day decisions. The course is described as punchy with climbs where competitors attack frequently. Nutritionally, top riders consume up to 150g of carbs per hour to maintain energy levels throughout the demanding course.

name: GFNY Lourdes Tourmalet
tagline: 157km of Pyrenean hell: 4,064m up Tourmalet and Aspin—pure climber's rapture.

name: Gran Fondo Maryland
tagline: 100 miles of savage Maryland climbs crushing souls with 10,000ft through Catoctin wilds.

name: Gravel Earth Series
tagline: Europe's premier gravel circuit. From Girona to Kenya to Iceland, the alternative to Life Time's American hegemony.
st: Riders describe extremely challenging terrain conditions with chunky gravel featuring massive rocks that cause destructive impacts over 200-mile distances. The North Course is particularly punchy and chunky, creating high speeds and chaos in the field. Weather conditions significantly impact racing with muddy terrain from winter rain and wind, temperatures around 8-14 Celsius, and riders getting covered in mud. The races feature rocks flying everywhere, frequent crashes, and high mechanical risk from flat tires and equipment failures. Competition levels are described as through the roof with fields of 20+ potential winners and over 100 strong riders including Olympians, pro triathletes, and world cup winners. Racing is very aggressive, dynamic, and technical. The sport has evolved from casual friends riding together to highly professional events with team tactics, sponsors demanding results, and significant preparation required. Early season races in February present unique challenges as all riders are fresh and injury-free, making competition particularly intense.

name: Haute Route Alps
tagline: Seven stages. 800 km. 19,000 meters of climbing. The highest and toughest amateur stage race in the world crosses the Alps from city to city.

name: Mount Baker Hill Climb
tagline: Brutal 22-mile, 4,462ft beast with jaw-dropping alpine views that demands your soul.

name: Paris-Brest-Paris Randonneur
location: Rambouillet to Brest and back, France
tagline: 1,200km from Paris to the Atlantic and back. The oldest cycling event on earth (1891). 90 hours. No rankings. Just you, the road, and 130 years of history.

name: Marcha Cicloturista Quebrantahuesos
tagline: Spain's legendary bone breaker. 198 km through the Pyrenees. Four mountain passes. Two countries. Named after the bearded vulture that rules these skies.

name: SBT GRVL
tagline: Champagne gravel at altitude - where the pros come to play and the views will make you forget about the suffering
st: SBT Gravel in Steamboat Springs features the famous champagne gravel - fine pebbles that create an extremely fast racing surface. The course becomes intensely dusty immediately after the first gravel section, covering riders and bikes completely. Key challenges include a grueling five-mile, 1,000-foot climb that tests even elite gravel climbers, technical single track sections resembling cyclocross, and scary washboard downhills where excited riders frequently pass. The altitude makes racing feel insanely fast and presents a significant challenge. The event spans four days with expo activities, multiple course distances from 37 to 100+ miles, and attracts 1,800+ riders from all backgrounds. Riders emphasize pacing strategy over group riding, recommending starting slow and checking in with yourself throughout the long distance. The course plays to different strengths with 90% favoring certain rider types, but steep climbs require conservative pacing and altitude management.

name: We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo
tagline: The cobblestones. The bergs. The beer. Ride the greatest one-day classic the day before the pros.

name: Gravel Worlds
tagline: 150 miles of relentless Nebraska rollers. $100K purse. Pirate swords for winners. The grassroots championship.
st: Gravel Worlds in Lincoln, Nebraska features 150 miles with about 11,000 feet of climbing across 87 hills. Riders describe the terrain as more relentless than Unbound, with constant roller coaster hills that cause speed loss and leg fatigue. The course has no flat roads and includes challenging sections of deep gravel requiring relaxed bike handling. Long straight roads create crosswind challenges and echelon formations. The race requires complete self-sufficiency with no outside support for mechanicals or nutrition. Riders must carry all supplies or stop at 2-3 official water oasises and checkpoints. The $100,000 prize purse attracts a highly competitive field. Road conditions vary from primo gravel to technical deep sections that can put riders into difficulty. Weather is typically dusty with potential for strong winds.

name: L'Etape du Tour de France
location: Route changes annually (2026: Bourg d'Oisans to Alpe d'Huez, French Alps)
tagline: One stage. The real Tour de France route. 15,000 amateurs on closed roads, one week before the pros.

name: Dirty Reiver
tagline: The UK's biggest gravel race. 200km through Kielder Forest. Cold, wet, and utterly British.
st: Dirty Reiver at Kielder Forest is described as one of the biggest gravel events in the UK, offering 200km, 130km, and 65km route options. Located on the England-Scotland border, the course features 3,500m of elevation gain over 200km with immediate challenging terrain after a neutralized start. Riders describe the gravel as rougher and more technical than some European events, but still flowy rather than super technical. The event starts with pretty hilly terrain early on and what riders call a spicy start straight onto rough stuff. While officially not a race but a timed challenge, competitive riders do race at the front. The atmosphere is praised for great energy and friendly vibes, with emphasis on helping other riders and finishing safely. Preparation includes significant carb loading the night before, with riders consuming rice, wraps, and peanut butter sandwiches. Even experienced riders note that 200km is a massive distance requiring proper preparation and early rest.

name: Gravel Locos
tagline: Fast Texas gravel. River crossings. Scorching heat. The Unbound tune-up that breaks people.
st: Gravel Locos features a challenging early river crossing at mile 10 where riders must walk bikes across using ropes, with moving rocks and strong current making it treacherous especially in road shoes. The course includes extremely fast gravel roads where pathfinder tires excel due to road-like center tread. Key challenges include a steep short climb immediately after the river crossing and high-speed downhills that string out the field. Mountain bike riders struggle with spinning out and aerodynamics on the fast flats. The race starts neutralized through the river crossing for safety. Pros like Dylan Johnson, Colin Strickland, Ted King, and Peter Stetina participate, with the field easing into race pace before ramping up intensity. Aid stations provide Gatorade, water, and candy for refueling. Technical skills matter for cornering and handling potholes on dirt sections.

name: Haute Route Dolomites
tagline: 450 kilometers and 14,500 meters of climbing across 5 stages through the Italian Dolomites and Alps — Stelvio, Gavia, Pordoi, and the jagged spires of UNESCO World Heritage mountains. Professional stage-race format for amateur cyclists who want to suffer like the pros.

name: L'Ardéchoise Cyclosportive
tagline: Europe's largest mountain cycling gathering. 15,000 riders. 160 decorated villages. The Ardèche department becomes one giant cycling festival.

name: The Mid South
tagline: Oklahoma's weather lottery with a $100K purse and a guaranteed Bobby hug.
st: The Mid South features over 6000-7000 feet of climbing across 100+ miles of Oklahoma red dirt roads. Key challenges include County Road 70 with old bridges and double track on the west side of I-35, and the back 50 miles which are more hilly than previous years with continuous climbing. The course runs through Stillwater to Mulhall aid station at mile 28, then becomes significantly more challenging. Terrain includes chunky red dirt that becomes very problematic when wet, requiring big tires for clearance. East-west roads don't get sunlight and can have mud and ice. The Triple XXX section is fully tree-covered requiring lights. Riders emphasize drinking water constantly due to heat, using descent momentum for climbs, and warn that single speed attempts are extremely challenging. The first 28 miles are shared between 50 and 100 mile courses with 1500 riders creating fast early pace.

name: Midnight Sun Randonnée
location: Brännland Inn near Umeå, Sweden to Scandic Plaza hotel in Umeå (loop into Norway to Mo i Rana and Norwegian Sea fjords)
tagline: Epic 1,215 km randonnée across Scandinavia under 24-hour midnight sun, crossing the Arctic Circle with 14,296 m elevation gain.

name: Taiwan KOM Challenge — The Ascent to the Clouds
tagline: 105 kilometers from the Pacific Ocean to 3,275 meters. Through Taroko Gorge's marble cathedrals to the roof of Taiwan. Asia's most iconic hill climb — where WorldTour pros and amateur dreamers start together at sea level and finish above the clouds.

name: White Rose Classic
tagline: Yorkshire's must-do sportive: 125 miles, 11,000ft of Dales brutality.

name: Belgian Waffle Ride Cedar City
tagline: Half the climbing of San Diego. All the suffering. Plus miles of playground sand.
st: Belgian Waffle Ride Cedar City features 127 miles with 6500 feet of elevation and significant technical challenges. The race starts cold at 40 degrees with a critical tunnel pinch point at mile 4 that creates crashes and dust visibility issues. Riders need to be in the top 50 to avoid problems. The first major climb at mile 19 is decisive, requiring 8+ minutes at high watts. A technical single track section at mile 115 advantages mountain bikers. Gravel feels much rougher at race pace (25-30 mph) than pre-riding speeds. Course has sections with rocks and holes hit at high speed with little warning. Recommended setup includes 40-42mm tires, tire inserts for flat protection, and lower pressures (27-29 psi). Race goes from no warm-up to full gas immediately, making positioning crucial early.

name: Squadron Energy Grafton to Inverell Cycle Classic
location: Grafton to Inverell, New South Wales, Australia
tagline: 228 km point-to-point from the Clarence Valley to the New England Tablelands. 3,382 metres of climbing including the 16.3 km ascent of Gibraltar Range through World Heritage rainforest. Australia's toughest one-day road race since 1961.

name: Haute Route Pyrenees
location: French and Spanish Pyrenees (route varies annually)
tagline: Five stages. 580 km. 14,000 meters of climbing. The Haute Route brand crosses the Pyrenees from Mediterranean to Atlantic across the most legendary cols in Tour de France history.

name: Jeroboam
tagline: The world's toughest gravel ride where wine bottle sizes determine your suffering quota and 7,000 meters of climbing is just the appetizer.
st: The Jeroboam 300 is a 300-kilometer gravel challenge based in Erbusco near Milan, heading up into the Alps. Riders report the course was surprisingly 90% on road with less gravel than expected, making it more of a low drag experience. Key challenges include pretty gnarly gravel sections in the Alpine portions and an absolutely ridiculous descent called Stradivari with insane steep gradients and massive cobbles bigger than your head. Weather can be brutal with immediate soaking rain and hailstones requiring rain jackets from the start. The big mountain pass is typically hit around midnight to 1 AM for those riding through the night. Riders used 42mm 650b tires but recommend pumping them out more for the massive cobble descents. Many riders take shelter at natural stations during severe weather. The event has a bike packing flavor and offers checkpoints with stamps along the route.

name: L'Eroica
tagline: 209 kilometers, 3,863 meters of climbing, and 112 kilometers of strade bianche on vintage steel bikes through Tuscany's Chianti, Crete Senesi, and Val d'Orcia. 8,000 riders in wool jerseys and toe clips reliving the golden age of cycling since 1997.

name: Paris-Roubaix Challenge
location: Busigny to Roubaix Velodrome, Nord, France
tagline: The Hell of the North. 30 cobbled sectors. The Arenberg trench. Finish on the Roubaix Velodrome. The day before the pros.

name: Gran Fondo Strade Bianche
tagline: Ride the white roads of Tuscany. 138km through the iconic sectors. Finish in Siena's Piazza del Campo.
st: The Gran Fondo Strade Bianche features 16 gravel sectors totaling 82km off road over 213km total distance with elevation of a mountain stage. The race starts chaotically on a descent with 6,000 riders, creating dangerous conditions before hitting relentless lumpy terrain with proper steep sections. Key challenges include the technical Monte Santi media section where crashes occur and historical attacks at 80km to go. The gravel sectors are described as fast and furious, requiring thick tires for improved handling and lower puncture risk over weight savings. Riders recommend getting quickly to upward gradients after the chaotic start to avoid crashes. The course features traffic jams even on closed roads and requires threading through hundreds of riders on gravel climbs through Tuscany's challenging terrain.

name: Tour of Nilgiris (TFN)
tagline: Asia's premier multi-stage endurance cycling expedition through the Western Ghats

name: Best Buddies Challenge: Hyannis Port
location: Boston to Hyannis Port, Massachusetts, USA
tagline: 100-mile charity rocket from Boston to Cape Cod with pros, celebs, and lobster-fueled glory.

name: Granfondo La Fausto Coppi
tagline: The Campionissimo's mountains. Cuneo to the summit of Colle Fauniera at 2,481 meters. Three Piedmont cols. Since 1987.

name: Gran Fondo Suisse
tagline: 150km of Vaudois Alps brutality with 3,700m+ that crushes souls like a Grand Tour stage.

name: La Stelvio Santini Granfondo
tagline: Alpine legend: conquer the Mortirolo and Passo dello Stelvio in Italy's most celebrated granfondo

name: Maratona dles Dolomites - Enel
location: La Villa / Corvara, Alta Badia, South Tyrol, Italy
tagline: Seven Dolomite passes. 9,000 riders chosen by lottery from 31,000 applicants. The most oversubscribed cycling event on Earth.

name: Rebecca's Private Idaho
tagline: Idaho's most beautiful gravel race. 100 miles through the Pioneer Mountains. Not made to be easy.
st: Rebecca's Private Idaho features Trail Creek Summit as its crux climb, a 20-30 minute ascent that's half paved, half dirt with six percent average grade. Riders emphasize the critical importance of staying with a good group over this summit, as the following sections are fast rolling terrain where dropped riders face long chases. The 22-mile Copper Basin Loop presents rough, washboard roads that get hot and dusty, described as a sufferfest with rolling terrain. The final 10-mile return to Trail Creek is considered the most mentally challenging section, featuring straight roads with slight uphills and brutal headwinds. Course surfaces are rocky and bumpy throughout, with washboards from vehicle traffic, but riders report 32-35mm tires are sufficient. The event includes registration and expo areas in Ketchum, with aid stations available though early ones may be skippable.

name: Rift Valley Odyssey (RVO)
location: Kiambu County to Maasai Mara, Kenya
tagline: The only mountain bike race crossing Kenya's Great Rift Valley to Maasai Mara

name: Schleck Gran Fondo
tagline: 159km of Luxembourg Ardennes brutality on Fränk Schleck's savage training roads—UCI qualifier that sells out faster than a Tour de France sprint.

name: Sea Otter Classic Gravel
tagline: Festival racing on California's Central Coast. Where gravel meets expo madness.
st: Sea Otter Classic has transformed from a mountain bike race to a gravel race for 2025, creating new challenges for riders. The course starts at Laguna Seca Raceway with a huge fast descent that makes riders nervous, followed by short intense climbs throughout. Key sections include Lookout Ridge, a steep 2.7-mile climb with technical single track that represents the most challenging part of the course. The race finishes with a critical 180-degree turn just 200 meters from the line, where positioning is crucial for victory. Located in Northern California right on the ocean, the race features cool cloudy conditions that may warm up as fog burns off. Riders face uncertainty with the new gravel format, requiring strategic decisions about wheel, tire, and gearing options. The course demands tactical thinking about pinch points and where attacks might happen, as no one has raced this particular gravel route before.

name: Unbound Gravel 100
tagline: The little sibling that still kicks your ass. 100 miles of Flint Hills gravel—all the suffering, half the existential crisis.
st: Unbound Gravel 200 in Emporia, Kansas features 200 miles through the Flint Hills with significant elevation and challenging terrain. The North Course is described as punchier and chunkier with massive rocks that create a destructive racing surface. Heat is a major factor that wreaks havoc on power output late in the race, often determining who cracks or breaks into top positions. The course includes sections famous for high speeds, frequent punctures, and tough gritty terrain with rocks flying everywhere. Riders report it takes 10.5 hours of intense focus with crashes and mechanicals being unpredictable race factors. The event attracts 4,000 riders from 50 states and 40 countries, making it the most competitive gravel field. Training requires completely different mentality and preparation compared to other races. Pro riders recommend road bike gearing for high speeds and chain waxing for the conditions.

name: Belgian Waffle Ride San Diego
tagline: The OG American Spring Classic. 130 miles of kasseien-crushing brutality where 1 in 3 riders DNF, and the survivors earn their waffles.
st: Belgian Waffle Ride San Diego features a major course change with no Double Peak finish climb, allowing riders to be more aggressive earlier in the race. The 131-mile route includes substantial rough off-road sections that favor mountain bikers and gravel-specific equipment. Key challenges include the first off-road sector requiring good positioning to avoid 20+ minutes of chasing, the Lemon Twistenberg dirt section that shreds lead groups, a boulder garden dismount, and a brutal 20-percent sandy trail climb that forces walking. The route starts with a big climb followed by dirt multi-use trail and fast downhill to thin packs before single track. Most riders now use 40-45mm gravel tires instead of road tires. Double Peak still features as an unrelenting 1,400-foot climb but repositioned earlier in the race. Strong headwinds and technical terrain create opportunities for selective racing throughout the event.

name: L'Etape du Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift
location: Route changes annually (2026: Vaison-la-Romaine to Mont Ventoux, Provence)
tagline: The official amateur stage of the Tour de France Femmes. 7,000 riders on Mont Ventoux, one day before the pros, on fully closed roads through Provence.

name: Liège-Bastogne-Liège Challenge
tagline: La Doyenne's amateur sportive — 257km through the Belgian Ardennes, 4,500m of climbing, and every legendary côte that has defined Monument cycling since 1892.

name: Race Across America (RAAM)
location: Oceanside, CA to Atlantic City, NJ
tagline: 3,000 miles. Coast to coast. 12 days. The most extreme endurance cycling race on Earth. Oceanside to Atlantic City, nonstop since 1982.

name: Little Sugar MTB
tagline: New LTGP MTB race—30 miles of technical Bentonville singletrack, pairs with Big Sugar Gravel, pro field guaranteed.
st: Little Sugar MTB in Bentonville, Arkansas is described as the most technical race on the Lifetime Grand Prix calendar. The 30-mile course features 3500 feet of climbing in a saw tooth profile with constant ups and downs. Key challenges include Technical Difficulty with steep technical switchbacks, and The Ledges - a narrow section high above ravines where riders expect the most carnage. The course starts at Coler Mountain Bike Park, heads through Down Under loop, Tunnel Vision for 12 miles of single track, then Technical Difficulty before descending to Windsor Dam crossing and the only aid station at mile 30. The second half climbs through Back 40 to The Ledges, then Mighty Pine Trail to the Bentonville finish. Riders emphasize this is a proper mountain bike race requiring full suspension bikes, technical cornering skills, and durable tires due to Bentonville's reputation for being hard on equipment.

name: Raid Pyreneen Randonneur
tagline: Atlantic to Mediterranean. 18 cols. 720 km. 100 hours. The Pyrenees from ocean to ocean since 1950.

name: Seven
tagline: Australia's crown jewel gravel race. 125km through Western Australia's forests. Host of 2026 UCI Gravel World Championships.
st: Seven gravel race in Nannup, Western Australia features 125km with 3200m of climbing and 12 categorized climbs, making it the biggest gravel race in the Southern Hemisphere. The course starts with a chaotic first gravel sector where the surface is uneven and riders get pushed off lines. Key challenges include loose, sketchy gravel on descents due to dry conditions, and the brutal Brockman climb with 22% gradients where some riders must walk. The first major descent stretches the peloton based on confidence levels. Riders emphasize staying in the front 30 wheels during early gravel sections and picking lines carefully on loose surfaces. The race offers stunning scenery through valleys with epic views, though cameras don't capture the full beauty. Over 1600 people registered, with perfect weather conditions. Course logistics include food drop bags at the halfway stop, and accommodation books out quickly requiring early registration.

name: BWR Utah (Belgian Waffle Ride Utah)
tagline: Utah red rock adventure—135km of high-desert gravel, 3,000m elevation, May heat, waffles.
st: BWR Utah in Cedar City features 127 miles with technical single track that riders describe as the most technical in the BWR series. Key challenges include a potential pinch point at the tunnel at mile 4, and the first major off-road section at mile 19 with zigzags and climbing. The course includes 12 miles of flat to rolling hills and ends with a super steep final climb. Riders emphasize the technical single track gets pretty sketchy and rowdy, requiring mountain bike skills and careful gear selection. The race typically takes over 6 hours but the added single track increases completion time. Tire selection is critical with 40-42mm widths recommended. The course features eight aid stations and requires strategic positioning at four key points throughout the race. Technical terrain demands features like suspension, chain guards, and mountain bike pedals for easier clip-in/out on sketchy sections.

name: Iron Horse Bicycle Classic
tagline: Race the train. 47 miles. Two 10,000-foot passes. Durango to Silverton since 1972.
st: The Iron Horse Bicycle Classic road race covers 47 miles with 5,700 feet of climbing from Durango to Silverton over two 10,000-foot passes. Coal Bank Pass (10,640 ft) and Molas Pass (10,910 ft) are the decisive climbs where attacks happen continuously and selections are made. The course runs on closed US Highway 550 with big wide roads where groups move faster than solo riders. Riders report the first climb makes it nearly impossible to maintain conservative power targets due to race dynamics. The event has run since 1972 as Colorado's oldest cycling event, attracting a diverse field from 43 states and five countries. Nutrition strategy involves consuming 650-850 calories during the race at 150 calories every 30 minutes. There is a mile of false flat through Silverton to the actual finish line. Race tactics emphasize finding wheels early and staying consistent rather than chasing attacks.

st: You're riding through the Mara and there's zebras crossing right in front of you. You just have to stop and take it in because nothing in cycling prepares you for this. Day three broke me. The heat, the dust, the altitude — everything compounds. But crossing that finish line knowing you survived all four stages is something else entirely.

name: The Rift
tagline: 200km across Iceland's volcanic highlands. River crossings, lava fields, and the tectonic rift. Gravel's most otherworldly course.
st: The Rift in Iceland presents logistical challenges for traveling riders, with flight disruptions and limited preparation time being common issues. Race morning conditions included rain, requiring strategic clothing choices. Riders report that temperatures drop significantly once entering the highlands portion of the course. The race took place during a period of volcanic activity in southwest Iceland, adding to travel complications. Accommodation near the start line is available and considered advantageous. Local food shopping can be confusing with significant price variations on basic items like avocados. The race attracts riders seeking both performance and the experience of racing through Iceland's dramatic landscape, with some finding meaning beyond competitive results in the natural beauty of the course setting.

name: Tour de Mátra Gran Fondo
tagline: Hungary's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier in the Mátra Mountains, climbing to Kékestető - the nation's highest peak

name: Triple Bypass — Colorado's Three-Pass Challenge
tagline: 3 mountain passes. 118 miles. Nearly 11,000 feet of climbing across the Continental Divide. Colorado's defining cycling challenge since 1988.

name: Wicklow 200
tagline: Ireland's premier cycling sportive through the breathtaking Wicklow Mountains

name: Belgian Waffle Ride Asheville
tagline: Blue Ridge brutality—129 miles, 10,000 feet, Belgian-style mixed terrain in Asheville humidity.
st: Belgian Waffle Ride Asheville is described as an extremely demanding 98-mile course with 10,000 feet of climbing, split 50 percent gravel and 50 percent road. Almost all climbing occurs on gravel surfaces. The race presents continuous difficulty with no easy roll-out or cruise sections, keeping riders in the hurt locker from mile 5 to 95. Key challenges include very steep late-race climbs where riders stand in third gear moving only two miles per hour, and technical downhill turns where tires can slide out. The course was shortened twice from an original 140 miles due to flooding. Riders recommend 35mm tires and suggest upgrading to an 11-36 cassette for adequate gearing. The inaugural Asheville edition was delayed from 2020 due to COVID and moved to August 2021. Race conditions included some rain during the final sections.

name: BWR Montana (Belgian Waffle Ride Montana)
tagline: Montana Rockies adventure—140km of mountain gravel, 2,800m elevation, Missoula scenery, waffles.
st: BWR Montana serves as the final stop in the Belgian Waffle Ride Triple Crown series, taking place in Big Sky Country around Bozeman, Montana. Riders describe the course as encompassing everything gravel racing should be, featuring significant single track sections that create technical challenges, especially when riders hit them 100 miles into the race while fatigued. The terrain includes challenging unroad sectors that pop up unexpectedly throughout the course. Most competitive riders opt for 45mm tires to handle the varied terrain. The race typically breaks down into small groups throughout the day, with expectations that only three or four riders will remain together heading into the technical single track sections. The event attracts strong competition as riders vie for both individual race wins and overall series standings.

name: Grinduro
tagline: Where gravel racing meets enduro format - timed segments, untimed vibes, and a festival that actually delivers
st: Grinduro features multiple challenging sections including the notorious Mud Canyon that causes issues for many riders, the Cow Path of Death described as the most spectacular spot, and an insane 45 percent grade hike-a-bike section. The race starts fast with 1000 riders in four waves fighting for position before single track. Terrain includes very bumpy gravel sections and single track segments in woods. The format uses timed stages within longer loops - four different stage types including hill climbs, downhill sections, and technical single track. Riders recommend wide-range gearing like 11-52 cassettes, 42mm tires, suspension forks with 30mm travel, and dropper posts. Pacing strategy is crucial for the 60+ mile distance. The event combines serious racing with an epic party atmosphere and camping, creating a bucket list experience in locations like Tuscany and Scotland.

name: Haute Route Ventoux
tagline: 3 stages, 3 summits of Mont Ventoux, 270 kilometers, and 7,500 meters of climbing through Provence. Every stage finishes atop the Giant of Provence — from a different side each day.

name: Giro Sardegna Gravel
tagline: Sardinian mountains—130km of island gravel, 2,600m elevation, Mediterranean views, May warmth.
st: The Sardinian coast offers stunning scenery with crystal blue water and beautiful beaches, but riders should expect much lumpier terrain than route profiles suggest. The East Coast section appears flat on maps but features constant up and down elevation changes that can be challenging. Old mountain roads have very little traffic and provide amazing views. Heat and strong headwinds are significant factors during midday hours. Riders recommend starting very early (around 4am) or riding into the night to avoid the heat. Local knowledge is valuable - connecting with Sardinian cycling tour guides can provide route suggestions for the most scenic roads. The hospitality of Sardinia is notable, with tight-knit communities where everyone knows each other.

name: Granfondo Tre Valli Varesine

name: Grinduro Germany
tagline: Enduro-style gravel festival in Germany's Eifel with timed descents, untimed climbs, and party vibes.
st: Grinduro Germany in Hellenthal featured a unique format with four timed segments on a long course rather than a traditional A-to-B race, including a challenging 1K hill climb prologue. Race day conditions were extreme with heavy rain and strong winds throughout the first several hours, creating muddy terrain that riders described as making it feel like the longest 50k ever. Multiple riders experienced punctures, with tubeless tires being essential equipment. The event attracted both professional and amateur riders, with some switching categories day-of. Despite challenging weather conditions with lots of mud, riders maintained good grip and made the best of the wet terrain. The race format included multiple attempts at the prologue time trial. Riders emphasized the importance of pacing for long distance events and being prepared for variable weather conditions in the Eifel region.

name: L'Etape Costa Rica by Tour de France
tagline: 130km of volcanic fury near Irazú—Tour de France spirit ignites Central America's savage climbs!

name: L'Étape Slovakia by Tour de France

name: Mammoth Tuff
tagline: UCI Gravel World Series qualifier at 8,000 feet—High Sierra alpine gravel where altitude humbles everyone.
st: Mammoth Tuff is a challenging high-altitude gravel race in California's Eastern Sierra starting at 8,500 feet with 8,000 feet of climbing over 80+ miles. The course features unique pumice terrain from volcanic activity that feels light and airy under tires, requiring specific riding techniques and tire selection. Key sections include beautiful Chadago Canyon with red rock formations and challenging descents. Riders encounter sand, chunky terrain, and white pacey sand that remains playful rather than soft at elevation. The race demands altitude acclimatization and conservative pacing at endurance levels to manage the thin air. Recommended gear includes 50mm tires for the conditions and extra layers for temperature changes. The event serves as a destination weekend in Mammoth Lakes village, offering 360-degree mountain views and a strong community atmosphere for both first-time gravel racers and experienced riders.

name: Nordic Chase Gravel Edition
tagline: Multi-day Scandinavian adventure—Copenhagen to Oslo, 600km over 4 stages, supported with hotels and meals.
st: The Nordic Chase Gravel Edition runs from Copenhagen to Oslo covering 373 miles with 26,247 feet of elevation. The route features over 85% perfect gravel roads with excellent surface conditions and very light traffic. Riders face an immediate 800-meter climb from the starting point that brings them onto rolling Alpine plains, then flattens out with spectacular views for the next 200+ kilometers. The course offers beautiful views of mountain ranges including Hemal and Yiman National Park. Every 40-50 km there are small cafes and kiosks for resupply, though riders should check opening times as they sometimes close early. Water is readily available from fast flowing streams. Weather can be unpredictable with cold breezes even on sunny days, so extra clothing is essential. The race emphasizes community and adventure, taking riders through three countries with varied landscapes.

name: Oregon Trail Gravel Grinder
tagline: 5 days. 5 stages. The Grand Tour of American Gravel.
st: Based on rider experiences, gravel terrain varies significantly by region. Northern Vancouver Island features predominantly graded forest service roads with golf ball-sized gravel sections that remain fully rideable. Southern Spain presents more challenging conditions with steep, crumbly tracks requiring frequent hike-a-bike sections. Riders recommend minimum 2-inch tires for Spain's difficult terrain and 45mm tires for Vancouver Island routes, though hardtails aren't overkill. Weather changes rapidly on coastal routes, requiring preparation for varying conditions. The Jones H-bar system offers multiple hand positions for different terrain, allowing riders to shift weight back on steep descents and forward on climbs. Camping is relatively easy in southern Spain with cheap food options, while Vancouver Island offers oceanside camping opportunities near towering coastal mountains and deep fjords.

name: TORTOUR
tagline: 1,000 km. Multiple Alpine passes. Two days. Non-stop. The world's largest multi-day ultra-endurance cycling event — solo or in teams, day and night, across Switzerland.

name: Tour du Mont-Blanc Cyclo
tagline: Circumnavigate Western Europe's highest peak through three countries. 330 km. 8,000 meters of climbing. France, Switzerland, Italy, and back.

name: Tour of the Gila
location: Silver City, Grant County, New Mexico, USA
tagline: America's only UCI stage race — open to amateurs. 5 stages. 7,400 feet of summit finish. The Gila Monster awaits in the New Mexico wilderness.

name: Whiskey Off-Road
tagline: The OG Southwest MTB festival. Skull Valley will break you. Whiskey Row will rebuild you.
st: The Whiskey Off-Road 50-mile race in Prescott, Arizona features just shy of 7000 feet of climbing, starting at 5300 feet elevation and reaching 7000 feet at the highest point. The course begins on historic Whiskey Row and includes challenging terrain ranging from buff riding to very technical, loose, steep, and rocky sections. Key challenges include the brutal Skull Valley climb, described as about an hour of sustained climbing after the turnaround point. Early in the race, riders face a route choice between Moby and 48 sections. The course offers limited opportunities for nutrition due to technical terrain that makes reaching bottles difficult. Riders note hidden rocks, narrow rocky trails, and the need for careful line selection on downhills with two-way traffic. The elevation significantly impacts performance, particularly in the first two hours. The race finishes with new single track sections and is known for its welcoming community atmosphere in the cowboy town of Prescott.

name: Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo
location: Hazza bin Zayed Stadium, Al Ain to Sheikh Zayed Festival, Al Wathba, Abu Dhabi, UAE
tagline: Desert gran fondo linking Abu Dhabi's cities with massive prizes and community spirit

name: Chequamegon MTB
tagline: The Cheq—40 years of mass start MTB chaos, 3,000+ riders, and Life Time Grand Prix glory in Wisconsin's Chequamegon National Forest.
st: The Chequamegon 40 is a 39-mile point-to-point race through Wisconsin's North Woods following parts of the famous Berky cross country ski trail. Riders describe it as a 2-hour long short track race that's like a big washing machine with constant position changes. The race starts with a full-gas climb up a grassy hill that has only one good racing line despite being wide. Fire Tower Hill is the highest point and main selective climb where attacks happen and the race breaks up, followed by the punchy Seven Sisters climb. The course combines mountain bike single track with gravel sections and finishes with wide open power line undulations. Known as the drag race from Hayward to Cable, it's considered one of the hardest Grand Prix races to win due to pinch points and aggressive pack racing. The 42-year-old event brings together a strong cycling community in the small town of Cable, Wisconsin.

name: Gunni Grinder
tagline: High-altitude Colorado mountain gravel with 10,000 feet of climbing and thin air at 10,000 ft.
st: The Gunni Grinder takes place in Gunnison County, Colorado, which contains 35 percent of all gravel roads in Colorado and is 93% public land surrounded by BLM and forest service areas. Riders face significant altitude challenges, with the elevation requiring several days of acclimatization for those coming from lower elevations. The course features dust and technical sections throughout, with very varied terrain that progresses from sagebrush areas with sprawling vistas and distant mountains to riding directly in the mountains themselves. The route offers a great mix of road surfaces across this varied terrain. Gunnison County provides 800 miles of single track and millions of acres of public land access, making it an ideal location for challenging gravel racing with dramatic elevation changes and diverse landscape features.

name: Cycle to the Sun — Haleakala
tagline: Sea level to 10,023 feet. 36 miles of continuous climbing up a Hawaiian volcano. The House of the Sun awaits.

name: Lauf True Grit Gravel Epic
tagline: Where the Mojave, Great Basin, and Colorado Plateau collide to humble your gravel dreams
st: The Lauf True Grit Gravel Epic is an 84-mile, 9000-foot elevation gain race through Santa Clara, Utah's challenging terrain. The course travels through the Beaver Dam Wash National Conservation Area, featuring technical sections so difficult that finishing is considered an accomplishment. Key challenges include the technical descent to Zen where races can be won or lost, deep river crossings that catch riders off guard, and terrain accessible only by bike. Weather can transform the course into muddy, cyclocross-like conditions with hypothermia risks, tons of puddles, and cold descents followed by hopeful road sections. Riders recommend carrying tubes for common flats, considering larger 235mm tires for better traction, and preparing for extreme weather. The race combines competitive racing with family-friendly atmosphere, taking place annually in March through some of Utah's most beautiful and remote scenery.

name: Le Grand du Nord
tagline: UCI Gravel World Series race through Minnesota's stunning North Shore at Lake Superior.
st: Le Grand du Nord features challenging climbs in Minnesota's Sawtooth Range with three major ascents including a 20% grade section after the opening 6-7 mile gradual climb. The 110-mile course starts at Grand Marais YMCA with checkpoints positioned at hill bottoms requiring climbs back out. Riders describe fast gravel conditions with some chunky B sections that remain manageable for various bike types including fat bikes. The remote course winds through wooded areas with no gas stations or stores, offering Lake Superior views and potential wildlife sightings including moose and wolves. Race starts are staggered with 110-mile at 8:15am and shorter distances at 9:15am. Four bottles recommended for hydration. The scenic Grand Marais finish area provides excellent spectator viewing as riders return to town.

name: L'Etape Taiwan by Tour de France
location: Sun Moon Lake, Nantou County, Taiwan
tagline: 104km of Taiwanese lake paradise with 1,500m pain—Tour de France vibes in Asia!

tagline: Five Pyrenean passes. A double Tourmalet. 5,700 meters of climbing. Summit finish. The Alps' little sister bites harder than you think.

name: Gran Fondo Milano-Sanremo
tagline: La Classicissima. 296 km from Milan to the Ligurian Riviera. The Turchino, the Capi, Cipressa, Poggio. The longest Monument sportive in the world.

name: Monaco Gravel Race
tagline: Monaco/French Alps—110km of steep climbs, 2,500m elevation, iconic location, 20-25% DNF.
st: The Monaco Gravel Race features 120km of racing with approximately 2600-2700m of climbing through challenging terrain characterized by big stones and long climbs. The course includes a decisive long climb in the middle section where many riders struggle to maintain pace, followed by a critical final 7km downhill where mechanical issues can end podium chances. Weather conditions can turn the course wet and muddy when rain occurs overnight. The finish line is located in the middle of the forest rather than in town. Riders report the course feels fast rolling like a road bike on gravel despite technical sections. Tire pressure management and puncture preparation are crucial as multiple flats are common. The terrain includes beautiful landscape but demands climbing legs and technical skills through stony sections.

name: Niseko Classic
tagline: Asia's premier mountain gran fondo qualifier in Hokkaido's legendary ski terrain

name: Nova Eroica
tagline: Tuscan strade bianche—135km of iconic white roads, 2,800m elevation, medieval town charm, Italian gravel paradise.
st: Nova Eroica in Buonconvento, Tuscany attracts riders with diverse bike setups ranging from vintage steel road bikes to modern gravel machines. The event showcases a mix of mechanical groupsets including Apex, GRX, and Rival, with two-by drivetrains being more common than in US gravel races. Tire choices vary dramatically from very narrow road tires to massive 2.2-inch mountain bike rubber. Popular bike brands include Italian steel frames, Canyon, Cannondale, Scott, and vintage Bianchi models. Riders emphasize the importance of enjoying the Tuscan roads, local food, and overall experience rather than focusing solely on performance. The event maintains a relaxed, celebratory atmosphere where participants are encouraged to savor the journey through beautiful Italian countryside and return annually for the unique cultural experience.

name: Omloop Het Nieuwsblad Cyclo
tagline: Ride the Flemish classics cobbles and bergs the day after the pros

name: Ouray Gravel Grinder
tagline: Where gravel meets the sky - high-altitude suffering in Colorado's Switzerland
st: The transcripts provided do not contain information about the Ouray Gravel Grinder race in Colorado. Instead, they feature product reviews and bike builds for Parley bicycles, including the Parley TA and Parley Aura models. The videos discuss bike specifications, construction methods, and component details but do not mention the actual Ouray race event, course conditions, terrain challenges, or rider experiences from this specific gravel race. No race-specific intelligence about course difficulty, elevation challenges, surface conditions, or racing strategies for the Ouray Gravel Grinder can be extracted from these transcripts as they focus entirely on bicycle equipment reviews rather than race coverage or rider testimonials.

name: The Assault on Mt. Mitchell — Beast of the East

tagline: 102.7 miles from Spartanburg to the highest peak east of the Mississippi. 11,000 feet of climbing. The Southeast's most iconic cycling challenge since 1975.

name: Big Horn Gravel
tagline: High, rough, and lonesome. Colorado's rowdiest gravel race.
st: Big Horn Gravel in Gypsum, Colorado features 10,000 feet of climbing over 85 miles at elevation ranging from 6,000 to 10,000 feet. The course was rerouted due to snow at the high point but still delivered significant elevation gain. Riders describe losing top-end power at altitude with fewer matches for recovery, making pacing critical. The terrain consists mainly of county roads and rugged jeep roads, with some sections rotted out from rain and vehicle traffic. Key challenges include an early climb that strings out the field, a walking section near the summit where grades become unrideable, a technical two-mile downhill, and swoopy bermed single track toward the finish. Fast riders used straight gravel bikes while others benefited from hardtails with suspension forks and wider tires. The race attracts strong competition including Lifetime Grand Prix athletes preparing for Leadville. The event provides on-site camping and runs on a Sunday schedule with Saturday expo activities.

name: GFNY Bogota
tagline: The world's highest gran fondo starts inside a salt cathedral 200 meters underground, then climbs into the Colombian Andes above 3,000 meters. Race where Egan Bernal learned to pedal, where oxygen is rationed, and where cycling is a national religion.

name: UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake
location: Qiandao Lake, Chun’an County, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China
tagline: China's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series on the scenic 2022 Asian Games course at Thousand Island Lake

name: Granfondo Gottardo
tagline: 115 km. 3,248 meters of climbing. Three HC Alpine passes — Gotthard, Furka, Nufenen — on cobblestones dating to 1832. Switzerland's most iconic loop.

name: Grasshopper Adventure Series
tagline: The OG of gravel racing - 25+ years of Northern California dirt, redwoods, and pure cycling soul.
st: The Grasshopper Adventure Series in Northern California features some of the gnarliest gravel riding in California according to experienced riders. The course starts in Duncan Mills and rolls through wine country around Healdsburg before climbing into deep redwoods on Pine Flat and heading to the coast. Key challenges include Coleman Valley with spectacular views at the top, and Joy climb with brutal 16-17% gradient kickers. The terrain combines chunky pavement with dirt sections across rugged geography formed by tectonic plate meetings. Riders encounter everything from rolling wine country to coastal views, with famous stops like the watering hole in Casadero. The route showcases Northern California's diverse landscape with unexpected terrain changes that make it challenging and rewarding. Good aid stations including bakeries provide refueling opportunities during the long efforts required on this demanding course.

name: Hotter'N Hell Hundred
tagline: 100 miles. 100 degrees. Since 1982. The largest sanctioned century ride in America, where 10,000+ riders battle the brutal Texas heat in Wichita Falls every August. Birthplace of CamelBak. Where hydration is survival.

name: La Leggendaria Charly Gaul
tagline: 141 kilometers and 4,000 meters of climbing from Trento's Cathedral Square to the summit of Monte Bondone — the same finish where Charly Gaul collapsed, frozen and victorious, in the 1956 Giro d'Italia. UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier since 2012.

name: L'Etape Poland by Tour de France
tagline: 84km of Tatra brutality with 1,547m that punches above its Tour de France weight.

name: Pisgah Monster Cross
tagline: Southeast gravel classic—50 miles, 8,000 feet of technical Appalachian terrain in Pisgah National Forest.
st: Pisgah Monster Cross features challenging terrain made rougher by recent hurricane damage. The race starts with a 20+ minute climb up Joel Branch including steep switchback single track where riders hit threshold power. The course combines chunky gravel roads, double track ATV trails, and technical single track with roots. Key challenges include the opening climb, water puddles and brake bumps on the fish hatchery descent, and rutted gravel sections requiring careful line choice. Many riders bring full mountain bikes with 120mm travel, giving significant advantage over gravel bikes with 40mm tires on technical sections. The 50-mile course with 8000 feet of elevation takes around 5 hours to complete. Race atmosphere includes several hundred participants and no outside aid is permitted at aid stations.

name: Race Across Benelux
location: Amsterdam to Arlon via Netherlands/Belgium/Luxembourg, with loops around Arlon

name: RAGBRAI (Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa)
location: Across Iowa, USA (Missouri River to Mississippi River, west to east)
tagline: America's oldest, largest, and longest multi-day recreational bicycle ride. 10,000+ riders. 7 days. ~400 miles across Iowa from the Missouri River to the Mississippi. Running since 1973. Where an entire state becomes your aid station.

name: Rasputitsa
tagline: Vermont's early season mud fest—46 miles of Class 4 roads, self-supported suffering, and guaranteed mud.
st: Rasputitsa in Burke, Vermont is a 46-57 mile gravel race with 5000-7000 feet of climbing that riders describe as relentlessly difficult. The course features hard-packed Vermont gravel that feels like a dirt highway, with constant ups and downs throughout. Key challenges include the Notorious BIG Hill around mile 30 with 3800 feet of climbing already completed, and a rough section called Siberia. Riders emphasize this requires constant maximum effort and heart rate. The race has grown from 50 to 500 participants and features excellent community support with people lining the entire course. Aid stations offer whiskey shots, cookies, and hot dogs. Weather can vary dramatically - past years have been freezing cold while recent editions have been sunny and warm. The terrain can be muddy the day before but dry up for race day. The event supports the Halo Foundation which helps families dealing with cancer costs in Orleans County Vermont.

name: Styrkeprøven
tagline: 540 km from Trondheim to Oslo. Over Dovrefjell in the midsummer night. Norway's ultimate test of strength since 1967.

name: The Rad Dirt Fest
tagline: High-desert climbing in southern Colorado. Spanish Peaks backdrop, 10,000+ feet of vertical.
st: The Rad Dirt Fest in Trinidad, Colorado features 113 miles with over 10,000 feet of elevation gain. Riders describe fast gravel conditions suitable for slick tires or narrow 38mm options. The course includes varied terrain from smooth pavement to rough two-track with rocks, sand, and mud sections. Key challenges include an early choke point at mile 3.8 where only single riders can pass between fences, creating aggressive positioning battles. The route heads through Trinidad Lake State Park's rowdy Jeep tracks, then climbs gradually at 4-6% grades that are draftable, topping out at 9000 feet around mile 65. A steep paved climb hits riders at 10 miles out and again with 10 miles to go. Descents are mostly smooth but have surprise sharp corners requiring attention. The 9am start time provides better logistics than typical 4am race starts. Roads remain open to traffic requiring riders to stay right on blind corners.

name: Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo
UCI Gran Fondo World Championships
location: Venue rotates annually (2026: Niseko, Hokkaido, Japan)
tagline: The only world championship in amateur road cycling. Qualify through 36 global events, race for the rainbow jersey against 2,000 age-group riders from 58 countries. Venue rotates annually.

name: 947 Ride Joburg
tagline: Own the streets of Joburg. 97km through Africa's City of Gold on fully closed roads. The world's second-largest timed cycle race.

name: AIDS/LifeCycle
location: San Francisco to Los Angeles, California, USA
tagline: 545 miles. 7 days. 1 mission. The most emotionally powerful cycling event ever created. San Francisco to Los Angeles. $300 million raised. Where the cycling community rides to end AIDS.

name: Amashova National Classic
location: Pietermaritzburg to Durban, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa

name: Belgian Waffle Ride North Carolina
tagline: The Hell of the East - where your gravel bike wishes it was a mountain bike and your legs wish they were someone else's.
st: The Belgian Waffle Ride North Carolina is a 130-mile course with 14,000 feet of climbing that riders describe as arguably the hardest BWR course. The route is 60 percent paved but includes some of the roughest gravel descents in the area and four significant single track sections. Key challenges include the steepest climb at mile 78 - a 1.6-mile double track hitting 29 percent gradient where podium fights begin, and the final climb at mile 118. The course features sustained pitches well over 20 percent and private property sections like Falling Creek with steep single track that riders cannot pre-ride. The terrain is described as chunky with ridiculously steep climbs. BWR races are known for encouraging under-biking due to their mixed terrain design. Riders recommend wider tires for the technical sections despite the majority pavement, compact gearing for steep climbs, and timing moves on technical terrain rather than paved sections where the race typically isn't won.

name: Cape Town Cycle Tour
location: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
tagline: 35,000 riders. Chapman's Peak. The entire Cape Peninsula in a single day. The world's largest individually timed cycling event.

name: Death Ride — Tour of the California Alps
tagline: 5 mountain passes. 14,000 feet of climbing. The Sierra Nevada's ultimate single-day climbing challenge since 1978.

name: Dragon Ride L'Etape Wales
location: Margam Park, Port Talbot, South Wales, UK
tagline: Britain's hardest sportive. 230 km. 3,500 m of climbing. Brecon Beacons mountain roads. Devil's Elbow at 10.3%. Twenty-two years of slaying dragons in South Wales.

name: In Flanders Fields Cyclo - Wevelgem
tagline: Ride the legendary Flemish classic's course through war history and cobbled climbs

name: GFNY Aguascalientes
tagline: BE A PRO FOR A DAY in Mexico's cycling hotbed with views of Cerro del Muerto

name: GFNY Punta del Este
location: Punta del Este, Maldonado Department, Uruguay
tagline: 146km of beach glamour meets brutal hills in Uruguay's sexiest sufferfest.

name: UCI Gran Fondo World Series Antalya
tagline: Fast Turkish Riviera qualifier with luxury resort vibes

name: Gravel Brazil
tagline: Brazilian coastal mountains—100km, 2,000m elevation, steep technical terrain in beach town paradise.

name: Heck of the North
tagline: Technical snowmobile-trail gravel through Minnesota's North Shore at peak fall foliage.
st: Heck of the North features challenging terrain including rough ATV trails that make riders wish they brought mountain bikes instead of gravel bikes. The course includes technical snowmobile trail sections with very wet, muddy conditions requiring riders to walk through multiple cold ponds barefoot. Bridge crossings have slats that can catch 38mm tires. The race takes place in late September when northern Minnesota foliage is changing colors. Riders report the ATV sections are bumpy enough that it's difficult to see rocks clearly. Course scouting is recommended to identify navigation points and problem areas. Early pacing is critical as riders report being exhausted by mile 40 when going out too hard with fast groups. Weather can be cold requiring winter layers that need to be shed as the sun comes out.

name: Istria Granfondo
tagline: UCI qualifier crams 112km and 1745m into Istria's savage coastal climbs—pure sufferfest gold.

name: L'Etape Norway by Tour de France
tagline: Norway's inaugural 130km Tour de France experience through Arctic fjords and Nordic climbing

name: L'Étape Slovenia by Tour de France
tagline: Official Tour de France amateur cycling event in Slovenia's scenic Gorenjska region with pro-level closed-road routes.

name: Mt. Washington Auto Road Bicycle Hillclimb (MWARBH)
tagline: 7.6 miles. 4,678 feet. Average grade 12%. The highest peak in the Northeast, home to the worst weather in America. The toughest hill climb in the United States since 1973.

name: Gran Fondo Nove Colli
tagline: The world's oldest gran fondo. Nine hills. 200 km. Pantani's hometown. Since 1971.

name: Rad Dirt Fest
tagline: Colorado Rockies adventure—100km of high-altitude gravel, 2,500m elevation, Salida scenery.
st: Rad Dirt Fest in Trinidad, Colorado features challenging terrain that transitions from flat plains to mountainous climbing toward the Spanish Peaks. The race starts with a punchy 20+ percent grade climb that creates early selection and visibility for the field. After 113 miles of racing, riders face a final left turn onto Main Street to reach the finish. The course includes difficult sections of deep rock gravel that riders find challenging to ride on for extended distances. Weather can be a factor with sudden storms making clay-like road surfaces sticky and slick. One particularly tough section involves highway riding with 65 mph traffic and no shoulder. The race has tactical elements beyond pure fitness, with riders playing chess match games especially at the bottom of climbs. Trinidad has developed gravel cycling tourism with curated routes for visitors. The community strongly supports the event with local businesses and volunteers partnering to make the race possible.

name: Southern Cross Gravel
tagline: Punchy Georgia mountain gravel with a winery finish and the best post-race party in the Southeast.
st: The transcript discusses southeastern US gravel events but does not specifically focus on Southern Cross Gravel in Dahlonega, Georgia. However, it mentions similar regional events featuring challenging terrain with clay roads, quiet farm roads, and pleasant scenery typical of Georgia gravel racing. The events described showcase mixed surface riding with routes crossing state borders, offering multiple distance options typically ranging from 40 to 200 miles. The terrain is described as challenging regardless of distance chosen, with longer routes featuring clay roads and scenic countryside. These grassroots events emphasize community support and affordable entry fees, representing the authentic gravel cycling culture of the southeastern United States region where Southern Cross Gravel takes place.

name: Tour Aotearoa
location: Cape Reinga to Bluff, New Zealand

name: Vermont Overland
tagline: OG gravel since 2009—54 miles of Vermont pavé, 7,000 feet of climbing, and Paris-Roubaix dirt roads.
st: Vermont Overland is a 53-mile gravel race that's 92% dirt featuring 77 sections of class four road - ancient unmaintained Vermont roads. The course includes major climbs like Wade's Field Gap starting at 33 miles and 3,000 feet with eight to nine percent grades, More Town Gap, Roxbury Gap with heavy car traffic, and the notorious Braintree Gap described as bone jarring with real big dirt and rocks that riders avoid frequently. The race starts and finishes in downtown Woodstock village with Central Street closed for the event. The finish features a narrow footbridge section followed by the brutal Doubleday Hill climb at eighteen to twenty percent grade with pavement transitioning to grass and dirt. Race organizers recommend faster riders line up front at the 9 AM start, with positioning becoming critical on the technical finishing circuit through village streets and the final challenging climb.

name: Almanzo 100
tagline: The Granddaddy of All Gravel Races - where the movement was born, and where your legs go to die on Oriole Road.
st: Only 105 out of 333 starters finished. The mud was so thick your wheels would just stop turning. This is what Almanzo is — you against the roads, no support, no excuses.

name: Arlberg Giro
location: St. Anton am Arlberg, Tyrol, Austria
tagline: 150 kilometers and 2,500 meters of Alpine climbing through Tyrol and Vorarlberg. Two major passes, one legendary Austrian village, and the kind of scenery that makes you forget the suffering — until the next switchback.

name: Bootlegger 100
tagline: Southeast gravel staple—100 miles, 10,000 feet of North Georgia mountains, technical descents, Dahlonega charm.
st: Riders describe this as an extremely challenging race with gravel that is way tougher and harder than most places, featuring rocky and chunky terrain that delivers relentless pounding over 4+ hours. The course causes many race-ending mechanical failures, particularly wheel breakages from big hits on the harsh surface. Key challenges include managing pacing from a cold start, as riders must line up in age pens 45 minutes before the start with no warm-up opportunity. The rocky conditions tempt riders to increase tire pressures, but experienced riders recommend sticking to lower pressures for better suspension and comfort. Equipment durability is crucial, with strong wheels being essential to survive the chunky terrain that ends many riders' races through mechanical failures.

name: Copper Triangle — Colorado's Three-Pass Alpine Loop
tagline: 3 mountain passes. 79 miles. 6,500 feet of climbing — entirely above 9,000 feet. Colorado's definitive high-altitude loop since 2006.

name: CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica
location: Jacó, Garabito, Puntarenas Province, Costa Rica
tagline: Costa Rica's UCI Gran Fondo qualifier on the stunning Pacific coast

name: Cycle Oregon — Rally
location: Southern Oregon — Grants Pass and Jacksonville basecamps (2026). Route and region change annually.
tagline: Oregon's premier multi-day bicycle touring event. Since 1988. Five days through Oregon's most spectacular landscapes. Fully supported. Route changes every year.

name: GFNY Bali
tagline: A GFNY World series gran fondo that climbs from Bali's black-sand coast to the crater rim of Kintamani volcano. Tropical heat, 2,230 meters of climbing through rice terraces and temple villages, and the Island of the Gods as your backdrop.

name: GFNY Bento Gonçalves
location: Bento Gonçalves, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil

name: Gran Fondo Il Lombardia
tagline: The Classic of the Falling Leaves. One Monument. Two legendary climbs. Lake Como in autumn. Ride the roads that close cycling's greatest season.

name: BGY Airport Granfondo (Granfondo Felice Gimondi)
tagline: 30 years in the saddle of a legend. Six valleys. Selvino's 19 hairpins. Bergamo's cycling soul, named for the man who won all three Grand Tours.

name: UCI Gran Fondo Brasil – Pomerode
tagline: South America's Premier UCI Gran Fondo Qualifier in Germany-Heritage Santa Catarina

name: Gravel Burn
tagline: South African highveld classic—100km of red dirt roads, 2,500m elevation, stunning Mpumalanga scenery.
st: Gravel Burn is a seven-stage, 786-800km gravel race across South Africa featuring extreme terrain variations and challenging conditions. Key obstacles include the brutal Shellot climb with rocks, stones and loose terrain that forces riders to dismount, and the 14km Prince Alfred's Pass climb reaching over 1,000m elevation. Stage one presents muddy, slippery forest sections in wet conditions cold enough to destroy brake pads. The race traverses from coastal forests through the Karu Desert to wildlife reserves, with stages including 80km tarmac sections. Riders face extreme weather from freezing nighttime temperatures that freeze tents and gear to desert heat. The event combines professional racing with a $150,000 prize purse and burn camps featuring communal dining and campfires. Terrain constantly changes making flat stages potentially the hardest, requiring strategic pacing across the week-long effort.

name: Grinduro California
tagline: OG gravel party—timed enduro descents, untimed climbs, legendary Quincy vibes since 2015.
st: Grinduro California features four distinct timed segments that challenge any bike choice: a road time trial, gravel descent, gravel climb, and single track section around Lake Siskiyou. Riders report the course is notoriously dusty with challenging conditions including 90-degree temperatures. At 30 miles riders hit 3000 feet of elevation gain halfway through. The dirt descent segment is described as longer than expected and tiring. The road time trial segment is particularly dreaded, with riders struggling to maintain pace lines in the heat. The event provides full support with breakfast, lunch, dinner, bike support, and aid stations with food and drinks, though navigation can be confusing. Single track sections around Lake Siskiyou offer perfect trail conditions. Riders emphasize pacing strategy to avoid depletion before final segments.

name: L'Étape Mexico City
tagline: Experience the Tour de France in the heart of Mexico City

name: Maraton Franja BTC City
tagline: Europe's oldest gran fondo. 43 editions through the Slovenian heartland since 1982, named after a secret WWII partisan hospital hidden in a mountain gorge.

name: Paris to Ancaster
tagline: Canada's Spring Classic. 100km of mud chutes, rail trails, and chaos. The race that eats bikes.
st: Paris to Ancaster is described as the OG of gravel racing, existing since 1994 as a tribute to Paris-Roubaix. The 100km course features a mixture of road, gravel and technical sections including farm lanes, trails and tracks. Key challenges include a chaotic race start with nearly 3,000 riders creating bottlenecks and stones flying everywhere on the opening gravel sections. Notable course features mentioned by riders include the epic Powerline Mudslide section and the Martin Road climb. The race moves from fast gravel starts to wide road sections where positioning becomes crucial. Conditions can be muddy, creating what riders call a mudfest experience. The event uniquely starts male and female riders together and attracts both professional and amateur cyclists. Riders emphasize getting to the front early to avoid trouble in the technical sections after the fast opening.

name: Peaks Challenge Falls Creek
tagline: Three alpine summits. 235 km. 4,000 meters of climbing. Australia's definitive mountain sportive.

name: Rad am Ring
tagline: Race the Green Hell. 12,000 cyclists on the world's most famous motorsport circuit — the Nurburgring Nordschleife.

name: Rule of Three
location: Emporia, Kansas (same roads as Unbound Gravel)
tagline: 300 miles on Unbound roads—Kansas Flint Hills ultra distance, self-supported, 30-40% DNF, 15-25 hours of suffering.
