st: Rule of Three in Emporia, Kansas uses the same roads as Unbound Gravel, featuring the challenging Flint Hills terrain. Riders describe the North Course as particularly punchy and chunky with massive rocks that create a relentless beatdown over the 300-mile distance. The gravel feels like massive rocks that pound riders' bodies throughout the race. Key challenges include managing the rocky, chunky terrain that destroys equipment, with many races ending from flat tires, mechanicals, and broken wheels. The course demands significant preparation and respect, with high speeds and chaos in the field. Riders emphasize not starting too fast, especially from cold starts, and sticking to familiar tire pressures rather than going higher to avoid punctures. The terrain is described as way tougher than other gravel areas, requiring strong wheels and careful pacing. The race is highly unpredictable due to mechanical issues, and perfect scenarios don't exist in this 10.5-hour suffer-fest through the Flint Hills.

name: 3RIDES Gran Fondo Südliche Weinstrasse
location: Porte des Vins Allemands (German Wine Gate), Schweigen-Rechtenbach, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany
tagline: UCI Gran Fondo qualifier through German vineyards and into France

name: Les Cinglés du Mont Ventoux
tagline: Three ascents. Three faces. 4,400 meters of climbing. One day. The mountain they call the Beast of Provence — climbed from every side, because once is never enough.

name: Collegiate Gravel Nationals
tagline: USA Cycling National Championship—100km of Texas Panhandle gravel, collegiate field, wind, red dirt.
st: The Collegiate Gravel Nationals in Turkey, Texas featured demanding racing conditions with riders pushing exceptional power numbers throughout the 62-mile course. Power data shows normalized power outputs of 370-380 watts for 5+ hours, with climbs requiring 430-500+ watts repeatedly. The course proved punchy with multiple selection points on climbs, where riders averaged over 450 watts each time. Weather conditions were described as cooler than previous years, with mostly dry course conditions except for a few wet sections in tree-covered areas. The terrain allowed for relatively fast racing at around 38 km/hour average speeds during flatter sections, though constant elevation changes and lack of drafting opportunities made for extremely high energy demands. Multiple climbs featured 6-9 minute sustained efforts at 430+ watts, creating significant selections in the field throughout the race.

name: GFNY Dominican Republic
tagline: Be a Pro For A Day in Paradise

name: GFNY New York
location: Fort Lee, New Jersey / Hudson Valley, New York, USA
tagline: The original Gran Fondo New York. 5,000 riders from 90+ countries start on the closed George Washington Bridge and race 100 miles through the Hudson Valley to Bear Mountain and back.

name: GFNY Pittsburgh
tagline: The hardest GFNY in America: 70 miles and 7,756 feet of climbing through Pennsylvania's relentless hills

name: Giro d'Italia Ride Like a Pro USA
tagline: 85 flat miles of Italian flair in sunny Miami—pure pro vibes for amateurs.

name: UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki
tagline: Greece's inaugural UCI Gran Fondo qualifier combining Mediterranean coastal riding with mountain climbing on the Isthmus of Corinth

name: Gran Fondo Alberto Contador
tagline: Ride with a legend. 146 km through the Costa Blanca mountains, alongside the man who won the Tour, the Giro, and the Vuelta — and still shows up to pedal with the field.

name: Granfondo Eddy Merckx
tagline: The Cannibal's roads. 162 km through Belgium's Ardennes heartland. 18 savage hills. 2,644 meters of climbing. Ride the terrain that forged the greatest cyclist who ever lived.

name: Gran Fondo Siete Lagos
location: San Martín de los Andes, Neuquén, Argentina

name: Highlands Gravel Classic
tagline: The only US qualifier for UCI Gravel World Championships. 67 miles of Ozark smashfest.
st: The Highlands Gravel Classic is a 68-mile rolling terrain race in northwest Arkansas featuring treacherous gravel roads known for causing flat tires. The course includes a key climb that hits 20-25 minutes into the race after a fast descent section, with multiple pitches where the second pitch is particularly challenging and can split the field. Riders recommend mountain bike tires like 2.2 Conti Race Kings for the rough conditions. The race is part of the UCI Gravel World Series, one of only two such races in the US, where finishing in the top 25% of your category automatically qualifies you for the UCI Gravel World Championships. With smaller field sizes, start position matters less since the first real effort comes well into the race.

name: Granfondo Sestriere — Colle delle Finestre
tagline: The only granfondo in the world with the Colle delle Finestre. 7.8 km of legendary Giro d'Italia dirt at 2,178 meters. Summit finish at Sestriere.

name: Rock Cobbler
tagline: California's early season classic—100 miles of Central Valley rocks, dirt, and February weather roulette.
st: Rock Cobbler features 1000 riders in four waves of 250 each, starting with fast road sections before hitting challenging gravel terrain. Key obstacles include Mud Canyon causing issues for many riders, the spectacular Cow Path of Death, and a brutal 45% grade hike-a-bike section. The course includes 9300 feet of climbing with super punchy steep sections, a 4-mile Breen Ridge Road climb, and the difficult Ranchlands region. Riders recommend pacing strategy over aggressive early racing, suggest 42mm tires with suspension forks and dropper posts, and note the importance of staying connected with groups before technical sections. The course features minimal actual roads, momentum-sucking short climbs, and extends up to snow level with significant private property sections.

name: The Gralloch
tagline: Scottish Highlands ultra—200km of remote terrain, 3,500m elevation, brutal weather, 25-35% DNF.
st: The Gralloch features a brutal 6K opening climb requiring 8+ minutes at over 300 watts, followed by technical corners that catch riders off-guard. The chunky gravel drains well but is too rough to ride through punctures. Feed stations are self-service requiring full stops, with tech boxes every 5-10K. The ultra version covers 330km with a 25-hour time limit, though fastest finishers complete it in 12-13 hours. Set in Galloway Forest Park, riders experience stunning scenery with woodland sections, open areas, and mountain views. The dark sky designation means excellent stargazing for night riding. Weather can be challenging but the well-draining gravel surfaces stay rideable even after heavy rain.

name: Tour de Big Bear
tagline: 100 miles and 8,500 feet of climbing at 7,000-foot altitude through the San Bernardino Mountains. Onyx Summit at 8,443 feet is the highest paved road in Southern California.

name: BWR Arizona
tagline: Hell of the North Desert—100+ miles of sand, rock, and technical singletrack through Arizona's high desert.

name: Cyclassics Hamburg
tagline: Germany's only WorldTour race. 22,000 riders on closed roads. The Waseberg at 16%. Europe's biggest cycling festival — and registration is free.

name: Sea Otter Fuego XL
tagline: Where the major series begins and Leadville dreams are made in California sand
st: The Sea Otter Fuego XL is a roughly 34-mile loop course featuring fast single track and Fire Road sections with loose over hard pack terrain conditions. Riders describe the course as not very technical with just a couple rocks here and there, but extremely fast-paced. The race starts explosively with the first two minutes requiring around 600 watts of power output. A pre-ride lap takes about two and a half hours, but race pace cuts that time roughly in half. Key strategic sections include the first major climb where groups get established, and the first bit of single track on lap two where groups typically split up as riders begin to fade. The event is part of the Lifetime Grand Prix series with application-only entry requiring significant racing credentials. The race takes place during the larger Sea Otter Classic expo with major cycling brands present throughout the week.

name: Gran Fondo Hincapie
tagline: George Hincapie's Blue Ridge playground. 83 miles. 8,000 feet. Three timed climbs. Skyuka Mountain at 8.7%. The Southeast's premier gran fondo, born from 17 Tours de France.
st: The Gran Fondo Hincapie takes place in Greenville, South Carolina, utilizing the area's cycling infrastructure including the Swamp Rabbit Trail, a rails-to-trails conversion that connects Greenville to Travelers Rest. The route features Paris Mountain, described as a legendary climb with significant history from hosting National Road Race Championships for four to five years. Riders mention the beautiful downtown area with waterfalls and cafes accessible via bike paths. The Swamp Rabbit Trail allows car-free travel from downtown to accommodations like Embassy Suites. Local cycling amenities include numerous shops and cafes along the path, with popular spots being Tandem Crepery, Methodical Coffee, and the Commons. The area offers excellent cycling infrastructure with dedicated bike paths that avoid car traffic, making it rider-friendly for both the event and general cycling activities.

name: Gran Fondo Marco Pantani
tagline: Il Pirata's training roads. His family's event. Cesenatico to the Romagnol hills and back. Since 2005.

name: Granfondo Belgium
location: Les Lacs de l'Eau d'Heure, Hainaut, Belgium
tagline: 138km of Ardennes fury with 2100m punch—car-free UCI qualifier that bites back.

name: Gravel and Tar Classic
tagline: New Zealand countryside—160km of farmland gravel, 2,000m elevation, February warmth, 15-20% DNF.
st: The Gravel and Tar Classic in New Zealand's Manawatu region is described as one of the hardest one-day races in the world, testing riders both physically and technically. The 135km course features demanding gravel climbs and sectors that split the peloton, with Junction Road being the first major challenge featuring corrugations that cause crashes. Tire choice is critical, with smart riders opting for 28mm over 23mm tires. Dust kicked up on gravel sections makes breathing difficult and gets in riders' eyes. The race starts in Fielding and finishes in Palmerston North, taking in farmland, gravel watersheds, and river plains. Hot conditions over 30 degrees add to the challenge. Positioning is crucial as riders fight to get on gravel sections first. The technical demands require following track lines on corrugated roads while managing nutrition and hydration over the demanding terrain.

name: Gravel Roubaix
tagline: The Hell of the East - 8,000 feet of Pocono punishment that'll make you question every life decision

name: Grinduro Canada
tagline: Race inside a 450-million-year-old meteorite crater where the party is as legendary as the singletrack

name: Pan-Mass Challenge (PMC)
location: Worcester to Provincetown, Massachusetts, USA (multiple routes across Massachusetts)
tagline: The world's most successful athletic fundraiser. 6,500 riders. Up to 177 miles across Massachusetts. $1.125 billion raised for Dana-Farber Cancer Institute — and 100% of every rider-raised dollar goes to cancer research.

name: Dekom System Sudety Tour
tagline: A challenging cross-border gran fondo through the Sudetes connecting Czechia and Poland

name: Tour de l'Île de Montréal
tagline: 25,000 riders. Car-free Montreal. The island circumnavigation that once held the Guinness World Record as the largest gathering of cyclists on the planet.

name: Tour de Yorkshire Sportive
tagline: Up to 140 km on the same roads as the pros. Yorkshire's brutal climbs, Tour de France heritage, and 2.6 million screaming roadside fans — all cancelled since 2019.

name: Alpe d'HuZes
tagline: Twenty-one hairpins. Six ascents. One day. Cycling's greatest charity climb — EUR 100 million raised and counting.

name: Bend Dirt Fest
tagline: Multi-discipline high desert festival in Bend—gravel, MTB, and trail run in one weekend.
st: Riders describe Bend area gravel routes that utilize old logging railroad grades, maintaining gentle gradients that rarely exceed 2 percent. The terrain takes advantage of extensive logging roads on public lands throughout Oregon. A typical route starts from Shevlin Park area, climbing through upper Shevlin to connect with BS logging road. The warm-up section is described as uphill the whole way with one steep section at the beginning. Riders mention a steep downhill portion during the main route. The logging railroad foundation makes for very even terrain throughout most of the ride. One rider successfully used 40c Schwalbe G1R tires for the conditions. The area offers scenic riding with sunset views, and riders note this type of terrain showcases why Oregon's logging road network creates excellent gravel riding opportunities on public lands.

name: Engadin Radmarathon
tagline: Three days. Five passes. 221 km. 4,000 meters of climbing. Swiss Alpine precision through the Engadin — where glaciers meet UNESCO heritage and every pass tops 2,000 meters.

name: Ford RideLondon
tagline: Olympic legacy. 25,000 riders. 100 miles of closed London and Essex roads. The world's greatest festival of cycling — now on indefinite pause after 10 editions and £85 million raised for charity.

name: Lost River Classic
tagline: West Virginia's Appalachian gravel grinder: 8,500 feet of relentless climbing through humid forest roads where heat management matters more than fitness.
st: Based on rider accounts, this race features significant altitude challenges at around 10,000-12,000 feet that severely impact non-acclimated riders. One experienced rider was four minutes behind his personal record on the Columbine climb due to altitude sickness, experiencing headaches and difficulty breathing. The course includes major climbs like Sugarloaf and Columbine, described as brutal and requiring significant preparation. Riders report needing more than seven bottles for hydration. There are technical sections including Powerline descent where multiple riders experienced flats - six flats were observed in one section. The terrain includes chunky fire road climbs and swoopy dirt descents. Feed zone chaos is mentioned as a logistical challenge. Riders emphasize the importance of proper acclimatization and pacing strategy, with some having to drop back through multiple groups as the race progresses.

name: Munsterland Giro
tagline: Germany's biggest Jedermann cycling event. Up to 7,500 riders roll through the Munsterland on October 3rd, finishing at the Schlossplatz in Munster — Germany's bicycle capital — while UCI pros battle on the same roads in the afternoon.

name: Prosecco Cycling (Gran Fondo Prosecco)
tagline: 100 kilometers through UNESCO vineyards, Prosecco at the aid stations, Nessun Dorma at the start line. The only gran fondo where the feed zone serves shrimp scampi and sparkling wine.

name: RADL GRVL
tagline: Adelaide Hills adventure—130km of rolling Australian bush, 2,500m elevation, South Australia gravel.
st: RADL GRVL in Adelaide Hills features a brutal opening climb that riders describe as an absolute wall requiring 20 minutes of maximum effort. The 108km course includes over 2,000m of elevation with mixed terrain of gravel tracks, big climbs, and road sections. Riders emphasize the importance of starting position due to the immediate steep climb where people walk in their easiest gears. The race attracts professional cyclists including national champions, creating a competitive atmosphere. Equipment choices include gravel bikes with suspension forks and fenders. Race preparation involves strategic fueling with taped gels and route loading. The event is held in association with Tour Down Under, marking it as a significant gravel racing debut in South Australia's challenging terrain.

name: Redlands Bicycle Classic — Legends' Fondo
location: Redlands, San Bernardino County, California, USA
tagline: The longest-running professional stage race in the US — now ride the same courses. 40 years of where legends are born.

name: Tour de Okinawa
tagline: Japan's premier citizen road race. Subtropical island. Yanbaru forest climbs. 35 years of shut-down roads, sea-level starts, and the strongest amateur field in Asia.

name: Adelaide Epic Ride (Santos Tour Down Under)
tagline: 136 km through the Adelaide Hills on the same roads as the UCI WorldTour pros. Chandlers Hill, Wickham Hill, Mount Barker Summit, and 1,800 metres of climbing under the January sun. The community ride of Australia's biggest professional cycling race.

name: Crystal Bear
location: Laona, WI to Crystal Falls, MI
tagline: 192-mile overnight bikepacking odyssey from Wisconsin to Michigan's Upper Peninsula where darkness, cold, and solitude break you down and build you back.
st: The Crystal Bear race starts in town at a local bar on a flat, easy ATV/UTV trail with bad blacktop sections. The course features hard packed dirt roads with thick tree cover throughout, including beautiful sections through pine and birch forests that riders call some of the most scenic parts. Rain the night before the race made roads wet and sticky with numerous potholes and puddles that required careful navigation. The race typically starts relaxed with riders rolling out at 15-16 mph before the pace picks up. Hill sections through forested areas tend to split groups as stronger climbers gap others. Small groups form and work together for much of the race, rotating and drafting on flat sections. The northern Wisconsin setting provides a cool atmosphere with beautiful forest roads, though road conditions can be challenging after wet weather with riders needing to avoid obstacles.

name: Dirty French
tagline: Ohio's Appalachian suffer fest - 9,000 feet of vertical brutality in the heartland

name: GFNY Bahamas
location: West Bay Street near Goodman's Bay Park, Nassau, New Providence Island, The Bahamas
tagline: Ride through paradise on a flat and fast Caribbean course.

name: Gran Fondo Florida
tagline: Rolling hills and quiet country roads through Central Florida's horse farm country

name: Helsinki Gran Fondo
tagline: Scenic paved routes from city to countryside, non-competitive gran fondo for all levels

name: Le Race
location: Christchurch to Akaroa, Canterbury, New Zealand
tagline: 100 km. Cathedral Square to Akaroa. Across an extinct volcano, over the Port Hills, and down into a French harbour town. New Zealand's toughest one-day road classic since 1999.

name: Levi's GranFondo
location: Santa Rosa, Sonoma County, California, USA
tagline: King Ridge through the redwoods. 8,500 feet of Sonoma County climbing. Wine country meets Pacific Coast. Northern California's signature gran fondo.

name: Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo
tagline: 90 miles through Salinas Valley wine country with 5,500 feet of climbing, capped by the world's premier cycling festival.

name: Tour de Brisbane
tagline: Australia's ultimate closed-road gran fondo through a vibrant city on fully shut streets

name: Vätternrundan
tagline: 315 km around Sweden's second-largest lake. Through the midsummer night. 22,000 riders. The world's largest recreational cycling event.

name: United Energy Around the Bay
tagline: 210 km around Port Phillip Bay. Ferry crossing, Beach Road, 10,000 riders, and 30 years of Melbourne cycling tradition. Australia's biggest one-day ride, and it is not even close.

name: Atlas Mountain Race
tagline: Moroccan Atlas Mountains ultra-endurance gravel race.
st: The Atlas Mountain Race features immediate brutal climbing from Marrakech starting with the lung-busting 117km Tizi-n-Tichka Pass with 1800m elevation gain. The descent is an obscure rocky mule path littered with stones where most riders walk rather than ride. After 300km, the landscape transforms from snow-capped peaks and almond trees to lunar terrain with only rock dust and sagebrush. Day one includes a savage 2000m+ pass with snow and no clear path, taking riders all night to complete in pitch darkness. Navigation is extremely difficult in these conditions. The route alternates between tarmac and gravel surfaces. Race starts at 6pm with mandatory bike checks and tracking devices. Riders experience severe sleep deprivation, living on 2-3 hours sleep nightly while wearing the same kit for days. Checkpoint one is mandatory to continue and finish on time.

name: Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride
tagline: Australia's Tour de France winner. The Great Ocean Road. Challambra Crescent at 10%. Ride the same roads as the pros.

name: Dirty 30
tagline: Early season Michigan gravel with cold starts, mud risk, and MGRS community.
st: The Dirty 30 in Saranac, Michigan features hard packed gravel conditions despite recent wet weather. Riders report multiple sandy spots throughout the course that reduce bike control, even on gravel-specific setups. The course contains numerous potholes that require careful navigation. Race day temperatures can vary dramatically year to year, from 23 degrees in previous years to 34 degrees. The event offers multiple distances including 30-mile and 130-mile options, with longer distance riders starting earlier in the day. The terrain provides good riding conditions when dry and hard packed, though the sandy sections present ongoing technical challenges for bike handling and control throughout the race.

name: Etape Loch Ness
tagline: Scotland's largest closed-road sportive. 66 miles around the world's most famous loch. 900 metres of climbing. The King of the Mountain at 12%. Dawn start in the Highland capital.

name: Gran Fondo Firenze
tagline: Over the Ponte Vecchio. Through the Renaissance. Up the World Championship wall. Florence gives you the keys to the city — and then sends you into the Tuscan hills to earn them.

name: Gran Fondo Jasper
tagline: Canada's ONLY fondo inside a national park. 165 km through the Canadian Rockies. Marmot Basin — Alberta's #1 categorized climb. The Icefields Parkway. Jasper National Park, UNESCO World Heritage Site. Running since 2014.

name: Granfondo Pinarello
tagline: 155 km through Pinarello's backyard. The Muro di Ca' del Poggio. The Prosecco Hills. The brand that built Indurain's and Froome's bikes invites you to ride their roads.

name: GranFondo San Sebastián
tagline: 170 km on the roads of the Clásica San Sebastián. Jaizkibel above the Bay of Biscay. The amateur edition of a WorldTour monument.

name: Iowa Wind and Rock
tagline: 340 miles of relentless Iowa gravel where the wind never stops, the terrain never flattens, and 40 hours of continuous riding reveals who you really are.
st: This Iowa gravel ride features challenging terrain including a particularly difficult five-mile section of white rock surface that riders describe as horrific. The route passes through small Iowa towns like Exira and includes stops at local attractions such as a tree growing in the middle of a road intersection and Albert the Bull statue. Riders encounter typical Iowa gravel conditions with rocky surfaces that can be punishing on equipment. The area features rural gravel roads connecting small communities, with the terrain offering both scenic rural views and technical challenges from varied surface conditions.

name: Lake Taupō Cycle Challenge
tagline: 7,000 riders. One volcanic lake. 160 km around the largest caldera in the Southern Hemisphere. New Zealand's biggest cycling event since 1977.
st: The available transcript content for the Lake Taupo Cycle Challenge consists primarily of music or promotional content rather than substantive rider commentary about the race experience. No specific information about course features, terrain conditions, key challenges, or race atmosphere could be extracted from the provided transcript. Riders seeking intelligence about this 100-mile New Zealand event with 4000 feet of elevation gain would need to reference additional sources for actionable race preparation insights.

name: L'Etape Brasil by Tour de France
location: Campos do Jordao, Sao Paulo State, Brazil (flagship stage; series also visits Cunha and Rio de Janeiro)
tagline: ASO's Tour de France franchise brings closed-road mountain racing to the Serra da Mantiqueira -- Latin America's biggest amateur cycling event, 10+ years deep.

name: L'Etape Chile by Tour de France
tagline: 110km of Chilean coastal fury with 1,200m punch—Tour de France soul hits the Southern Hemisphere!

name: L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France
tagline: 50 km of Alpine charm through Italy's Maritime Alps with Tour de France flair and zero pretension

name: L'Étape Romania
tagline: Experience the Tour de France in the heart of Bucharest's historic streets

name: London to Brighton Bike Ride
tagline: 54 miles. 15,000+ riders. Clapham Common to Brighton seafront. The ride that invented mass-participation charity cycling. Since 1976. Ditchling Beacon awaits.

name: Ned Gravel
tagline: High-altitude Front Range gravel with ghost towns and 8,000-foot starts.
st: Ned Gravel in Nederland, Colorado features significant elevation challenges with multiple steep climbing sections that force many riders to walk. The course includes what riders describe as a never-ending descent to checkpoint one, followed by beautiful gradual grades when riding in groups, but also steeper climbs later in the route. Weather plays a major factor with overnight and morning rain creating muddy conditions. The 6000 feet of elevation gain pushes heart rates high, with one rider averaging 150 bpm throughout. Riders describe really good descents and beautiful views, but note the climbing makes for a rough day. Weather can change quickly during the event, with some riders finishing just before rain hits while others get caught in heavy downpours. Despite the challenging elevation profile, riders recommend the route for its fun descents and scenic Colorado mountain views.

name: Gran Fondo Pekan / Pekan Classic
location: Royal Town of Pekan, Pahang, Malaysia

name: Pelotonia
tagline: 17 to 165 miles across Central Ohio. $339 million raised. 100% of every rider-raised dollar to cancer research. This isn't a bike race — it's a movement on two wheels.

name: USA Cycling Gravel Nationals
location: La Crescent, Minnesota (2025-26); previously Gering, Nebraska (2023-24)
tagline: Stars and stripes on gravel—the national championship with open entry and a UCI Worlds pathway.
st: USA Cycling Gravel Nationals moved from Nebraska to La Crescent, Minnesota in the driftless region. The 110-mile course features 7,500 feet of elevation gain with nine distinct climbs roughly one mile long and 500 feet high. All courses finish on the chicken ridge gravel climb through an apple orchard, ending with a final one-mile climb averaging 8 percent grade. The terrain features bluffs with flat ridges and 400-500 foot descents into valleys. Riders emphasize pre-riding is essential due to limited visibility, tight turns, and technical descents. The gravel surface is typically good unless freshly graded. The course is described as windy, tight, and hilly like a mountain bike race. Racing starts at 7 AM on Main Street during Apple Fest weekend, drawing 766 riders competing for national championship jerseys across three distance options.

name: Vapor Trail 125
tagline: 125-mile high-altitude Colorado mountain gravel in Leadville.
st: The provided video transcripts do not contain any content related to the Vapor Trail 125 gravel race in Leadville, Colorado. The transcripts cover unrelated cycling content including mountain bike crashes, XC marathon racing in Switzerland, and general cycling incidents. No actionable intelligence about the Vapor Trail 125 race course, terrain, conditions, gear recommendations, or race strategies can be extracted from these materials.

name: Boulder Roubaix Road Race
location: Boulder Reservoir, Boulder County, Colorado, USA
tagline: America's spring classic. 61% dirt, 39% pavement, 100% suffering. The original Paris-Roubaix tribute race on Colorado's Front Range farm roads.

name: Cape Rouleur
tagline: 600 km. 6,000 meters of climbing. Five stages through the Cape Winelands on closed roads with pro-level support. South Africa's most luxurious cycling experience.

name: Cohutta Gravel Grinder
tagline: 100-mile Georgia mountain gravel through Cohutta Wilderness.

name: Finnish Gravel
tagline: The happiest gravel in the world. F1 glamour meets midnight sun in a UNESCO Geopark. Fast, flat, and fckng Finnish.
st: The Finnish Gravel race starts in downtown Lahti harbor and finishes at the Lahti Sports Complex, known for its world famous ski jumps that serve as a beacon for riders approaching the finish line. The race takes place in June when Finland experiences extended daylight, with sunset not occurring until 10:49 PM on race day, giving riders plenty of natural light for the 110-mile course. The finish area hosts a weekend expo at the Sports Complex. Post-race festivities extend late into the evening and include music, awards ceremony, food and beer. The race attracts hundreds of international riders who gather in the harbor area for the start.

name: TD Five Boro Bike Tour
tagline: 32,000 riders. 40 miles. 5 boroughs. Zero cars. The largest cycling event in the United States — a rolling block party through the greatest city on earth.

name: GFNY Bremen
tagline: Flat, fast, and windy: Be a Pro for a Day on North German roads

name: L'Etape Denmark by Tour de France
location: Various start points across Jutland, Denmark — all routes finish in Viborg
tagline: ASO's Tour de France franchise on Denmark's ancient Viking road. 7,000 riders. Five routes up to 300 km. TdF jerseys. All roads lead to Viborg.

name: Muju Gran Fondo
location: Muju County, North Jeolla Province, South Korea
tagline: South Korea's largest road cycling race in scenic Muju County[6].

name: Neusiedlersee Radmarathon
tagline: A 127 km flat-and-fast UCI Gran Fondo through Austria's easternmost wine country, where wind tactics trump climbing prowess.

name: Ring of Kerry Charity Cycle
tagline: 170 km around Ireland's Iveragh Peninsula. 4,000+ riders. 1,200 volunteers. EUR 22 million raised since 1982. The Atlantic coast is the scenery. Charity is the point.

name: STP — Seattle to Portland
tagline: 206 miles from Seattle to Portland in one or two days. 6,000+ riders, 47 years of tradition, and the Pacific Northwest's defining cycling event. City-to-city on country roads through farmland, small towns, and the green corridor of western Washington and Oregon.

name: Spinneys Dubai 92 Cycle Challenge
location: Expo City Dubai, Al Forsan Park
tagline: Middle East's premier mass-participation cycling event and UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier

name: Transcordilleras
tagline: Colombian Andes mountain gravel adventure.
st: Transcordilleras is a multi-stage Colombian Andes race covering 1055 kilometers over eight stages with 28,500 meters of elevation gain. Riders describe it as crossing three cordilleras through jungle terrain with steep climbs at high altitude and humid conditions. The ride to Puerto Berrio stands out as particularly challenging due to extreme heat requiring riders to seek relief in rivers and creeks. Logistics prove as demanding as the riding itself - daily tasks include securing food, cleaning bikes, and preparing for early starts. The non-stop category can be completed in 4.5 to 5 days with minimal sleep. Colombian riders are noted for their climbing speed and altitude adaptation. The race attracts international professionals who experience genuine Colombian hospitality. Riders emphasize the need for both mental and physical preparation as the race demands every ounce of energy over the multi-day format.

name: RBC GranFondo Whistler
location: Vancouver to Whistler, British Columbia, Canada
tagline: 122 km from Vancouver to Whistler on a fully closed Sea-to-Sky Highway. North America's largest gran fondo — 5,000+ riders crossing the Lions Gate Bridge at sunrise and climbing through the Coast Mountains on the one day a year this highway belongs to bikes.

name: WYO 131
tagline: Central Wyoming ultra-distance gravel through mountain and high desert.
st: The WYO 131 in Lander, Wyoming is described by organizers as a beast that's absolutely not for the faint-hearted, requiring previous gravel experience for riders attempting the full 131-mile distance. The event has expanded from only offering the intimidating full distance to now providing three options: 40 miles, 70 miles, and the full 131 miles, tripling registration numbers. The race welcomes all bike types from mountain bikes to expensive gravel bikes, emphasizing accessibility across the gravel community. The 70-mile option is still considered a big challenge. Hosted by the non-profit Lander Cycling Club, the event supports local trail work and building safer pathways to schools while fostering community involvement in cycling.

name: 66 Degrés Sud – La Cyclo
tagline: A UCI Gran Fondo World Series event through the Pyrenees-Orientales wine country and mountain terrain

name: America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride
tagline: 72 miles around Lake Tahoe at 6,300 feet. Emerald waters, Sierra Nevada granite, and one of the most audacious event names in cycling — except it might actually be true.

name: Bighorn Gravel
tagline: Colorado mountain gravel in Gypsum near Vail with high altitude climbing.
st: Bighorn Gravel in Gypsum, Colorado is a challenging 62-mile race with 6000 feet of elevation gain that tests riders at altitude from 6000 to 10000 feet. The course features a brutal first climb where many riders lose contact with the field, followed by rolling terrain along creek valleys with pitches that go up and level out repeatedly. Technical sections include swoopy bermed single track and a challenging two-mile downhill. Terrain varies from county roads to rugged Jeep roads that can be rotted out with ruts from weather damage. The elevation significantly impacts power output and recovery, requiring careful pacing strategies. Some sections near the summit are too steep to ride, forcing walking. The race attracts strong competition including Leadville preparation riders, and offers on-site camping with a Sunday morning start schedule.

name: Granfondo Colnago
tagline: 140 km through Lake Garda's morainic hills. Colnago's name on the bib. Vineyards, olive groves, and the climb to Coste where the mountains begin. The legendary frame builder invites you to ride their festival.

name: Granfondo Via del Sale
tagline: The Salt Road. From Cervia's ancient salt pans to the Romagna hills. Pantani showed up at the first one.

name: L'Etape Australia by Tour de France
location: Wollongong to Kiama, New South Wales, Australia
tagline: ASO's official Tour de France amateur experience on fully closed Australian roads -- 143 km, 2,701 m climbing, yellow jersey classification.

name: L'Etape Czech Republic by Tour de France
location: Prague and Central Bohemia Region, Czech Republic
tagline: ASO's Tour de France franchise in Central Europe -- 3,000 riders on fully closed roads through Bohemian hills, starting and finishing in Prague.

name: Rasputitsa Spring Classic
tagline: Vermont spring mud season mountain gravel in Burke.
st: The Rasputitsa Spring Classic features 5,700 feet of climbing over approximately 45-60 miles through Vermont's Burke Mountain area. Riders describe roads as pretty smooth with gorgeous scenery and horses along the course. The major challenge is the Notorious BIG Hill around mile 30, where riders face sustained climbing with grades that make 5% feel mellow. Aid stations around mile 26 offer whiskey and cookies, with hot dogs available later in the race. Weather can be brutal - riders report racing in 3-4 degree Celsius temperatures with rain turning to snow. In wet conditions, mud becomes so deep that riders sink into it and lose traction. Cramping becomes an issue on climbs steeper than 5% grade. The course attracts strong community support with people lining the entire route and helpful volunteers.

name: GranFondo Banff
tagline: 142 km through Banff National Park on closed roads with 1,600 meters of climbing. Canada's only gran fondo in a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Discontinued after 2016 -- succeeded by Gran Fondo Jasper.

name: Sagan Fondo
tagline: California Sierra Nevada/Tahoe gravel in Truckee.
st: The Sagan Fondo in Truckee, California features 68-78 miles with 8000 feet of elevation gain. The course includes a notorious section called The Boneyard around mile 40, which riders describe as legitimately like rock crawling for 20 minutes that destroys your back and legs. Sharp rocks throughout the course cause frequent punctures. With 500-600 participants, dust becomes a major issue, causing chains to run dry around 40 miles in. The terrain is technical enough to break bikes and demands high volume tires. Riders report the event being feisty and more race-like than a typical gran fondo, with age group categories and podium positions. The course serves as good preparation for other technical gravel races.

name: THE EQUALIZER - Missouri State Championship
tagline: Missouri State Gravel Championship through wine country in brutal July heat.
st: The St Genevieve Gravel Classic takes place in St. Genevieve, Missouri, the oldest French settlement west of the Mississippi River. The race offers multiple distance options including 18, 44, and 78-81 mile routes. A key challenge is Thunder Ridge, a levy section that's mostly grass with some gravel, where riders face about five miles of direct headwind. At mile 21, there's a significant fork where longer and shorter routes split. The terrain features beautiful countryside gravel roads through old farms and houses. Race organizers paint directional arrows on roads for navigation, which riders find more reliable than signs. The event is hosted by Trail Et with smooth check-in processes and good facilities. Weather can impact longer routes, with severe storm warnings potentially canceling extended distances for safety. The downtown area is blocked off for the event with plenty of porta-potties and organized packet pickup.

name: Vuelta Altas Cumbres
tagline: Argentine Andes mountain gravel challenge.
st: The available transcripts for the Vuelta Altas Cumbres race in Córdoba, Argentina contain minimal coherent content, consisting primarily of music, applause, and fragmented words. No specific information about the 100-mile course with 12,000 feet of elevation gain could be extracted from these transcripts. Riders looking for detailed race intelligence about terrain conditions, gear recommendations, pacing strategies, or specific course challenges will need to seek additional sources, as these particular video transcripts do not provide actionable racing insights or detailed course descriptions.

name: Wasatch All-Road
tagline: Northern Utah mountain gravel in Wasatch Range near Park City.
st: Riders describe gravel cycling as a broad term meaning riding off-road, with UK conditions typically involving walking tracks, bridal ways, mountain bike trails, and packed gravel paths. Terrain varies from hard-packed gravel suitable for slick tires to muddy tracks requiring knobby tires. Tire choice is crucial - slick tires work for hard surfaces and the gravel World Champs was won on 34mm slicks, while muddy conditions need knobblies with bigger, spread-out knobs for grip despite slower rolling. Modern gravel bikes are highly capable, more so than 1990s mountain bikes according to experienced riders. The key is matching tire choice to expected surface conditions, as this can make or break the ride experience. Events may be more technical than expected, so riders should be prepared to walk sections if needed.

name: Superior Morgul Road Race
tagline: The Wall. The Coors Classic. LeMond. Hinault. Phinney. Colorado's most storied road racing circuit, resurrected for a new generation on the legendary Morgul-Bismarck loop.

name: Croatan Buck Fifty
location: New Bern, North Carolina (Croatan National Forest)
tagline: East Coast ultra—150 miles of flat coastal gravel through Croatan National Forest, sandy terrain, 10-15 hours.
st: The Croatan Buck Fifty features a 99% gravel course through North Carolina's Croatan National Forest with over 560 participants. Riders describe fast sandy gravel roads dominated by large potholes that create constant hazards, especially when riding in groups where visibility is limited. The terrain is notably bumpy with more potholes than previous years, causing riders to hit rim bottoms on impact. Savage Road stands out as a technical section requiring careful line selection. The mass start with 550+ people creates hectic conditions requiring extra caution. Riders mention tire pressures around 38 PSI with some considering lower pressures. Despite challenges, participants praise the inclusive atmosphere, great food, and fun gravel roads. The course conditions are described as being in good shape overall, though riders consistently underestimate the difficulty level.

name: Crooked Gravel
tagline: Colorado mountain gravel in Winter Park with high altitude climbing.
st: The available transcript from Crooked Gravel in Winter Park, Colorado contains only fragmented audio with music and unclear dialogue. No specific information about the 62-mile course, terrain conditions, elevation challenges, or race experience could be extracted from the provided transcript. The video appears to contain primarily background music and incomplete phrases that do not provide actionable intelligence about course features, gear recommendations, pacing strategies, or race day logistics for this Colorado gravel event.

name: Granfondo Campagnolo Roma
tagline: Start at the Colosseum. Climb the Castelli Romani. Finish at Caracalla. Campagnolo's Roman holiday on two wheels.

name: L'Etape Cancun by Tour de France
tagline: Blaze 63 flat miles of Caribbean speed on closed roads, Tour de France style.

name: L'Etape Greece by Tour de France
location: Sparta, Laconia, Peloponnese, Greece (2026). Location rotates annually within Greece.
tagline: ASO's Tour de France franchise meets ancient Greek myth -- 140 km through Sparta, Mystras, and the wild passes of Mount Taygetus.

name: MAAP Beechworth Granite Classic
tagline: Victorian high country gravel in historic gold rush territory.
st: The MAAP Beechworth Granite Classic features challenging technical terrain through Victorian farmlands with significant sandy sections that can be treacherous. Riders report skatey, squirrly conditions when dry, with full sandy bogs that have caused crashes, including one rider crashing in sand up to their shins before the last rest stop. Tree roots present additional hazards throughout the course. The race starts with 2-minute intervals in groups of 50 through the town. Course reconnaissance is highly recommended to identify climbs and technical sections. Riders suggest arriving early to finalize tire pressure and bike setup. Nutritionally, 60-80 grams of carbohydrates per hour are recommended, with one hydration bottle plus shot blocks. Feed station locations should be scouted if relying on event nutrition. The course winds through rural farmlands with various technical challenges but is described as totally manageable and fun despite the technical difficulties.

name: Marji Gesick
tagline: 100-mile rugged Upper Peninsula mountain bike/gravel endurance.
st: The Marji Gesick 100 in Marquette, Michigan is described by riders as one of the most challenging endurance bike races in the Midwest. The course features 85-90% single track with 12,000 feet of climbing through rocks, roots, jump lines and constant hills across mining country trails. Riders emphasize the mental challenge, saying visibility is limited to 100-foot spurts with no rhythm possible. The terrain is bone-jarring and unforgiving, requiring complete focus even on descents. Key challenges include significant hike-a-bike sections especially toward Ishpeming and mental deterioration after 6-10 hours of technical riding. The race has no official aid stations, only Trail Angels providing limited support. What started as an event organizers thought might attract 12-15 people now draws over 600 participants who come to test their spirit and determination on this purposefully designed sadistic course.

name: Pony Xpress
tagline: Remote southern Colorado gravel through Sangre de Cristo mining country.

name: Red Granite Grinder
tagline: Wisconsin fall gravel—150/100/50 miles of rolling farmland, fall colors, and ultra-distance option.
st: The Red Granite Grinder in Athens, Wisconsin features challenging hill sections that push riders to their gear limits, with one particularly difficult climb identified as 'the worst of it' by riders using climb tracking technology. The 150-mile course includes road biking segments through remote middle-of-nowhere areas. Riders describe it as an enjoyable event with good community atmosphere and people. The terrain includes significant climbs followed by downhill recovery sections. Some participants noted they didn't train adequately for the event's demands. The race takes place in a scenic rural setting with venues like Running Horse Farm along the route. Despite the physical challenges, riders expressed willingness to participate again, praising both the course design and the overall community feel of the event.

name: Viana Granfondo
location: Viana do Castelo, Alto Minho, Portugal

name: Bike MS: New York City
location: Manhattan, New York City, New York, USA
tagline: The flagship edition of the world's largest cycling fundraising series. 30 or 50 traffic-free miles around Manhattan — through Battery Park Tunnel, past the Brooklyn Bridge, across the George Washington Bridge — raising money to end multiple sclerosis.

name: Bowral Classic
tagline: Australia's premier cycling gran fondo through the rolling hills and heritage towns of the Southern Highlands -- 150 km, 2,393 m climbing, spring in Don Bradman's country.

name: El Tour de Tucson
tagline: 102 miles through the Sonoran Desert. Saguaro cacti, four mountain ranges, and the best November cycling weather in America. The season closer.

name: Iceman Cometh
tagline: 30-mile Michigan mountain bike race with 4000+ riders in November.

name: aQuelle Tour Durban
tagline: Durban's coastal classic. 110km on the M4 from Moses Mabhida Stadium to the KwaZulu-Natal coast and back. Every cent to charity.

name: TransRockies Gravel Royale
tagline: British Columbia multi-stage gravel adventure.
st: The TransRockies Gravel Royale features challenging terrain with forest sections that are notably slow, mixed with gravel roads and champagne gravel surfaces. Riders encounter tough climbs requiring wide-range gearing like 10-52 tooth cassettes, plus challenging descents where suspension helps smooth rough edges. The course demands 47mm tires at 30 PSI for optimal performance. Forest sections significantly slow pace, making it a demanding day for riders. Electronic shifting proves beneficial on longer stages. Navigation requires GPS with preloaded routes. The race tests equipment durability with potential for damage. Riders emphasize enjoying the experience over placement given the technical challenges. Mixed terrain includes everything from smooth gravel to rough forest trails requiring versatile bike setups.

name: Amy's Gran Fondo
location: Lorne, Great Ocean Road, Victoria, Australia
tagline: 103 km along Australia's Great Ocean Road. A ride built on memory — honoring Amy Gillett, the cyclist killed in 2005 whose legacy reshaped cycling safety in Australia.

name: Belgian Waffle Ride Kansas
tagline: The Hell of the (Mid) West—BWR's fall Midwest edition through the Flint Hills with 89% unroad.
st: Belgian Waffle Ride Kansas features over 7,000 feet of climbing with grades up to 18%, making it extremely challenging for riders used to flatter terrain. The 123-mile full route includes technical sections like a cyclocross dismount-and-run portion and single track trails originally designed for hiking rather than biking. Riders describe washboard descents that make control difficult and steep climbs that demand careful pacing. Weather conditions included 55-degree temperatures with northwest winds up to 20 mph, requiring cold weather gear for many riders. The course features route changes and potential rerouting around bridge closures that GPS may not reflect. Aid stations are well-stocked with friendly volunteers, though the drink mix is potato and rice-based rather than sugar-based, and much of the food contains caffeine. Mechanical issues are common on the technical terrain. Riders consistently describe this as one of the most challenging gravel events, calling it insane but awesome.

name: D2R2
tagline: 180km Massachusetts rolling hills gravel in Deerfield.
st: D2R2 in Deerfield, Massachusetts features extremely challenging terrain with 16 climbs over 112 miles and 7000-13000 feet of elevation. Riders describe multiple 15% grade climbs on slippery, rocky dirt roads that test mental fortitude and require low gearing and wider tires. The course includes very rugged sections where some riders must zigzag up steep climbs. Support is limited so riders need to plan hydration carefully. The event provides three full meals including continental breakfast, lunch along Green River, and barbecue dinner, plus multiple aid stations with pickles, cheese, and Cliff bars. Registration costs $150 and includes commemorative pint glass with beer. Rest stops are typically positioned before climbs. The scenery is described as beautiful with changing fall foliage, but the technical rocky terrain requires specific gear preparation including wider tires and very low gearing ratios.

name: Etape Caledonia
tagline: Scotland's premier closed-road sportive. 80 miles through Highland Perthshire in the shadow of Schiehallion — the fairy hill where they first weighed the Earth.

name: Gravel Worlds Amateur
tagline: 150-mile Nebraska gravel challenge in Lincoln.
st: Gravel Worlds in Lincoln, Nebraska features a challenging 150-mile course with 11,000 feet of climbing and 87 hills throughout. Riders describe it as harder than Unbound despite being 50 miles shorter, with relentless roller coaster terrain and no flat roads. The course is 95% gravel with sections of deep gravel requiring riders to stay relaxed and let the bike float. Key logistics include no outside support - riders must be self-sufficient for mechanicals and nutrition, with only 2-3 water oases and two official checkpoints for refueling. The race attracts top talent due to a $100,000 prize purse and Graler series status. Long straight roads can create challenges with crosswinds and echelons. Towns along the route include Tui, Valparaiso, and Malcolm. Weather was favorable with low winds and humidity, though dust was present. The terrain constantly zaps riders' legs with up-and-down sections that cause speed loss over climbs followed by acceleration on descents.

name: Haute Route Gravel
tagline: European Alpine multi-stage gravel event.
st: Haute Route events are professionally supported multi-day stage races featuring extreme climbs across European mountain ranges. Riders face challenges like the 19km Jebel Akhdar climb in Oman with 8% average gradient and 20% ramps, and high-altitude Alpine climbs reaching 2200 meters where reduced oxygen significantly impacts power output. The Alps route covers 896 kilometers with over 22,000 meters of elevation gain across multiple stages. Each stage includes timed climbing sections with mandatory time limits riders must meet to continue. Gear recommendations include 50/34 chainrings with 11-28 or 11-30 cassettes for steep gradients. Nutrition becomes critical with larger riders needing 800-1300 grams of carbs daily. Events provide full support including marshals, neutral service, medical support, and rider briefings covering weather, climb details, and safety information. Mental challenges are common especially for first-time participants, but strong community support among riders helps people through difficult moments on climbs.

name: La Resistance
tagline: Route de la Soif — serious Alpine gravel with brooding mountain presence in the French Alps.
st: La Resistance features the Monte du Plato de Glier, a very steep six kilometer categorized climb that leads onto gravel roads across the plateau. The gravel section is described as strada bianchi style unmade roads, providing a different challenge for riders. The course holds deep historical significance as a memorial to 121 French resistance fighters who died on this plateau in 1944. Teams typically ride tactically on the steep climb, maintaining modest pacing to keep riders fresh despite the peloton getting thinned and stretched out. There are logistical pinch points at the top where riders collect bottles from support before transitioning to the gravel section. The plateau offers stunning scenery and riders often acknowledge the emotional importance of the location during the race.

name: L'Etape Argentina by Tour de France
location: Cordoba, Cordoba Province, Argentina (Estadio Mario Alberto Kempes start/finish)
tagline: ASO's Tour de France franchise returns to Argentina after an 11-year absence -- 134 km through the Sierras de Cordoba with the Argentine National Road Championship running alongside.

name: L'Etape Portugal
location: Viana do Castelo, Alto Minho, Northern Portugal
tagline: The Tour de France comes to the Atlantic. ASO's franchise sportive in Portugal's green north. Rolling Alto Minho roads. Three jersey classifications. Your gateway to L'Etape du Tour.

name: Noosa Gravel Enduro
tagline: Queensland hinterland enduro with technical terrain and tourist town base.
st: The Noosa Gravel Enduro starts at Tawanton with flat double track and single track sections before heading into the Noosa Hinterland. The course features significant climbing in the mid-section and additional climbing toward the end. Key challenging sections include the Tuchekoi Forest around Mount Karora with short sharp climbs, though riders warn of even bigger climbs afterward. The terrain includes swooping multi-use trails and old forest roads through the trees, but climbs are steep enough that even front-runners need to walk sections. The course passes through Pomona to the first checkpoint, then through various townships in the Noosa Hinterland with scenic views to the ocean. The final 20k returns through the extensive Noosa trail network connecting hinterland townships. Riders emphasize the importance of pacing strategy, maintaining distance from other riders on single track, and proper hydration and nutrition timing.

name: RAIN - Ride Across Indiana
location: Terre Haute to Richmond, Indiana, USA
tagline: 160 miles in one day across the entire state of Indiana. One road, one direction, dawn to dusk. America's most honest century-plus ride.

name: Sasquatch Duro
tagline: Western Oregon Coast Range mountain gravel in wine country.
st: Sasquatch Duro features wet Oregon fire roads with significant elevation changes and sheltered, tree-covered sections that make it difficult to judge gaps between riders. The course has a high point at 4,400 feet and includes strategic climbs early in the race for breaking up groups. A key feature is the Sasquatch Monster Split at mile 31, described by race promoters as where the race gets interesting. The terrain allows for fast riding on fire roads with Kenda Alluvium Pro 35mm tires proving effective. The final downhill section enables rapid passing of other riders. Race conditions often include Oregon rain, adding to the challenge. With 260 riders registered, the event draws significant participation. The course rewards tactical climbing and gap management skills due to limited visibility between competitors.

name: Spotted Horse Ultra
tagline: Iowa prairie ultra-endurance gravel challenge.
st: Spotted Horse Ultra in Iowa is known for extreme challenge and variable weather conditions. Riders describe encountering dusty B-roads throughout the 150-mile course, with significant elevation changes requiring some riders to walk steep climbs. Wind is a major factor with 15-35 mph conditions creating both beneficial tailwind sections early in the race and punishing headwind stretches in the second half. The course features a mix of gravel B-roads and some pavement, finishing with the final 3/4 mile on pavement. Water resupply is available around mile 35-37. Race conditions can vary dramatically year to year, from extreme mud and cold requiring survival tactics to pleasant 70-degree weather with favorable winds. The event is held in October and organized by Sarah Cooper and Steve Fuller, with a reputation for being one of the most challenging gravel races.

name: Tour de Nebraska
tagline: Five days of supported gravel cruising through Nebraska's rolling farmland - more family reunion than race, and that's exactly the point
st: No race-specific content found in provided transcript. The transcript appears to be from a historical documentary about Boulder Dam construction rather than gravel cycling race coverage or rider experiences from Tour de Nebraska. Unable to extract cycling-related intelligence, course conditions, terrain descriptions, or rider advice from this material.

name: Tour of Cambridgeshire
location: East of England Showground, Peterborough, Cambridgeshire, England
tagline: The UK's only UCI-sanctioned closed-road gran fondo. 7,000 riders across the Cambridgeshire fenlands where wind, not elevation, is the enemy.

name: Trans Iowa
tagline: 300+ mile self-supported Iowa gravel ultra-distance challenge.
st: The transcripts cover gravel racing experiences from Kyrgyzstan's Silk Road Mountain Race and Texas's Gravel Locos race. Riders describe challenging terrain including steep mountain passes reaching 3,100 meters elevation with altitude sickness concerns. The Texas race featured technical river crossings that required walking bikes across with rope assistance due to deep water and slippery rocks. Gravel roads gradually increased in difficulty and speed throughout events. Equipment recommendations include 42mm tires, frame bags for nutrition storage, and dual bottle setups with backup hydration. Race tactics mentioned include neutralized starts for safety, gradual pacing by professional riders, and strategic refueling at aid stations. Course conditions varied from paved tarmac roads to technical gravel sections with punchy climbs requiring power output.

name: Battenkill
tagline: New York rolling hills gravel in Cambridge.
st: Riders describe Battenkill as featuring compact gravel and sand sections that favor wider, slicker tires over narrower knobby ones. The course includes bumpy grassier sections that send vibrations through hands and arms, as well as fast fire roads where flared handlebars provide comfort. Key challenges include the technical difficulty of eating and drinking while hanging onto wheels over seriously bumpy terrain when heart rate is sky high. Riders recommend 30 psi tire pressure or slightly lower for comfort, wider slicker tires for the surface conditions, and higher handlebars with shorter stems compared to road bike setup for better handling and comfort. The race requires significant research beforehand since gear changes aren't possible once racing starts. Nutrition becomes particularly challenging when racing hard over rough surfaces, making practice during training essential for success in this demanding gravel event.

name: Coast to Coast
tagline: Michigan ultra-distance crossing from Lake Huron to Lake Michigan.
st: The Coast to Coast Gravel Grinder is a 204-mile point-to-point race from Au Gres to Ludington, Michigan, with both solo and relay options. The race splits into four legs of roughly 50 miles each. The course features generally flat gravel terrain with significant headwinds that riders describe as nasty. Key challenges include tight sections at the start requiring a neutral rollout, and an 11-mile straight section that riders find mind-numbingly boring. The route passes through Gladwin and includes areas where dropped bottles accumulate from the challenging headwind conditions. Riders report burning around 2,000 calories by mid-morning and recommend pacing around 12-13 mph for sustainable effort. The flat gravel sections allow for drafting opportunities where riders can team up for aerodynamic advantages.

name: Cowichan Crusher Gravel Fondo
tagline: Vancouver Island forest roads around Lake Cowichan.
st: The Cowichan Crusher is held in Lake Cowichan, British Columbia, featuring four different distance options from 25km to 160km. The course has been modified due to construction on the rail trail. Riders experience the beautiful Cowichan Valley Rail Trail with spectacular trestle bridges and phenomenal scenery. The terrain includes hard-packed trail sections with no motorized vehicle access, creating smooth riding conditions without washboard surfaces. Some sections become more mountain bike-like and rougher but remain rideable. The event attracts diverse participation with over 30% female riders overall and nearly 50% in shorter distances. The race moved from Duncan to Lake Cowichan for a more remote setting while maintaining access to excellent gravel riding. Conditions can vary from fast, smooth trail sections to more technical mountain bike-style terrain, requiring riders to adapt their pacing accordingly.

name: Devils Cardigan
tagline: Tasmanian mountain gravel adventure.
st: The Devils Cardigan features extremely challenging terrain including the notorious Snake Pit section, described as a river bed with soft gravel, soft mud and water where bikes get stuck. The course runs from Arthur's river to Warar, with particularly gnarly rough terrain from Warar to Delerain that requires walking and features steep climbs so difficult riders say they wouldn't attempt them on mountain bikes. Weather conditions can change dramatically, with temperatures dropping and rain starting around 4 a.m. Equipment reliability is crucial as bike lights can fail unexpectedly. The course was reportedly much harder than previous years. Despite the brutal conditions, riders emphasize the strong community spirit and camaraderie, with fellow competitors providing encouragement during difficult climbs. The race leaves participants feeling isolated in remote wilderness sections but also creates lasting bonds through shared suffering.

name: Edition Zero
tagline: New Zealand South Island gravel adventure.
st: Edition Zero takes place in Waimate, South Island New Zealand, about 20 minutes from Timaru. The 140k entree distance takes around 6 hours. Key challenges include deep gravel sections starting around 6k that cause crashes, and a unique velodrome finish requiring two laps around the historic 1891 Waimate velodrome. The terrain features long straight roads with rolling hills, varying from thick to thin gravel where riders constantly search for smooth lines. The relentless straight sections and rough surface cause significant back fatigue without suspension. Riders report being able to pass others who slow down after the turnaround point, suggesting conservative pacing is effective. Hydration planning is critical with riders finishing water supplies at the halfway point. The event is praised for strong organization and community focus.

name: Filthy 50
tagline: Minnesota fall gravel—50 miles of rolling terrain, 1,000 riders, and peak autumn colors.
st: The Filthy 50 in Northern Minnesota features challenging conditions that vary significantly with weather. Riders report velcro-like road surfaces after rain that require substantial extra power to navigate, with soft gravel that creates leg fatigue. Headwinds are a major factor, with one rider reporting 220 watts needed to maintain just 11 mph speeds. The route travels through farmlands near the Minnesota-Iowa border around Blue Earth, Minnesota. Post-rain conditions eliminate dust but create sticky, power-sapping surfaces. Wind direction can dramatically affect ride difficulty, with tailwinds providing relief on return portions. Some riders cut routes short to conserve energy due to challenging headwind conditions, suggesting pacing strategy is crucial for completing the full distance.

name: GFNY Cozumel
location: San Miguel de Cozumel, Quintana Roo, Mexico
tagline: Two flat laps around a Caribbean island where the wind does the climbing. Latin America's largest cycling event wraps a GFNY World series qualifier in tropical heat, trade winds, and turquoise ocean views.

name: Gravel Bogotá
tagline: Colombian Bogotá mountain gravel challenge.

name: Karukinka
tagline: Chilean Tierra del Fuego gravel adventure.

name: L'Etape Thailand by Tour de France
location: Rotating: Phang Nga (2018-2022), Phitsanulok (2022, 2025), Udon Thani (2024), Thailand
tagline: ASO's Tour de France franchise brings closed-road racing to Southeast Asia -- rotating between Thai provinces with tropical heat, hilly terrain, and the only TdF-branded event in ASEAN.

name: NYC Century Bike Tour
location: New York City, New York, USA — Meatpacking District start/finish, four-borough route through Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, and the Bronx
tagline: America's only all-urban century ride. 100 miles through four NYC boroughs. Born in 1990 as an act of cycling protest. 35 years of staking cyclists' claim to New York City streets.

name: Oregon Coast Gravel Epic
tagline: Central Oregon coast gravel with coastal and mountain terrain.
st: The Oregon Coast Gravel Epic in Waldport features two major climbs that define the race experience. The first climb spans 5.5 miles with over 1,200 feet of elevation gain, described by riders as a grinder that strings out the 250+ participant field. After descending nearly to sea level, riders face a second climb gaining 1,400 feet in just 5 miles. Key logistical challenges include a flagged intersection at mile 3.1 where riders leave pavement, requiring complete stops and slow navigation. Logging trucks share the roads during the event. The course includes smooth pavement sections on A River Road that provide relief from gravel surfaces. This is a timed event using electronic sensors rather than traditional finish line placement, with the short course taking experienced riders approximately 3.5 hours to complete 38 miles.

name: Paris Mountain Gravel Grinder
tagline: South Carolina Paris Mountain gravel in Travelers Rest.
st: Riders describe challenging climbs including Santa Rosa Creek, which features extremely steep sections requiring full out-of-saddle efforts with some riders walking portions. The area offers multiple climbing options including Old Creek and Santa Rita road. Santa Rita road is highlighted as gravel that's smooth enough for road bikes, described as quiet and beautiful with scenic views. The terrain around Santa Margarita offers varied surfaces from steep paved climbs to manageable gravel roads. Riders recommend tubeless tire setups and specifically mention IRC X Guards for reliability. The riding area features multiple santa-named roads with different character - some extremely challenging climbs and others more accessible gravel suitable for various bike types.

name: Pony Xpress Gravel 160
tagline: Colorado ultra gravel challenge.
st: The Pony Express Gravel features smooth roads early with tailwinds, transitioning to challenging conditions with high 90s to 100 degree temperatures both days. Course includes lots of minimum maintenance roads, stream crossings, and steep climbs. Glass hazards present - one rider suffered tire damage from glass shard around mile 40. Route passes through small towns with gas stations for water and supply resupply. Early morning conditions are pleasant before heat and headwinds develop, creating suffer fest conditions later. Strong community spirit among riders helping each other with mechanical issues. Beautiful rural churches and scenery along the route. Two-day bikepacking option available with camping midway through.

name: River City Gravel Fondo
tagline: Vancouver Island gravel through forest roads and coastal terrain.
st: The River City Gravel Fondo is the first event in the Vancouver Island Gravel series, part of the BC Gravel event series sponsored by Trek. The race offers three distance options: Experto Long Course, Intermedio, and Impulso. The Impulso short course covers just under 40km in an out-and-back format to a turnaround point. The course runs primarily on logging roads that have been recently redone and described as smooth, though they become very dusty during the event. Riders face significant climbing sections that cause glasses to fog up from exertion. The turnaround point is marked with cones and signs, featuring an aid station where riders can replenish supplies. Race registration includes lunch ticket, water bottle, and t-shirt. This second annual event has grown significantly in participation numbers and takes place on a beautiful non-rainy day with spring foliage.

name: Ruby Roubaix
tagline: Northeast Nevada gravel in remote Ruby Mountains near Elko.
st: The available transcripts for Ruby Roubaix provide limited race-specific information. One experienced rider mentions this was their third time racing the event and achieved second place, describing the experience as fantastic. The rider notes being able to keep up with the lead group and pushing hard throughout the race. The event appears to attract riders who return multiple years, suggesting a positive race experience. However, the transcripts do not contain detailed information about course features, terrain conditions, specific challenges, or technical aspects that would typically help riders prepare for this 62-mile, 5000-foot elevation gravel race in Lamoille, Nevada.

name: The Range
tagline: Alberta mountain gravel challenge.
st: The Range rustler is an 86km gravel race in Alberta featuring mostly public gravel roads in perfect condition with quiet traffic. Key features include a decision junction where riders choose between 60km Rodeo or full rustler distance, and exclusive access to Burke Creek Ranch via private road with fast downhill section. Race starts at 8am in 20C temperatures before afternoon heat reaches mid-30s. Course has one aid station at turnaround point. Terrain feels ancient and fossil-rich through Alberta coulees. Early pace goes really fast requiring pack riding skills. Riders should come self-sufficient with food and water and plan around hot afternoon conditions.

name: Tour de Cure
location: Multiple locations nationwide, USA (Arizona, Northern California, Chicagoland, New England, Greater New York, Mid-Atlantic, Texas, and more)
tagline: America's premier diabetes cycling fundraiser. Multiple editions nationwide. 5 to 100 miles. Red Riders leading the charge. 30+ years of riding to stop diabetes — one pedal stroke at a time.

name: Tour de Palm Springs
tagline: Desert charity century through the Coachella Valley. 28 years. 10,000 riders. 20 bands. $5 million raised. Wind, sun, and a post-ride party that rivals the ride itself.

name: 3 State 3 Mountain Challenge
tagline: Three-state mountain gravel crossing Alabama, Georgia, and Tennessee.
st: The provided transcripts do not contain information about the 3 State 3 Mountain Challenge race in Mentone, Alabama. Instead, the transcripts discuss bike sizing considerations, a gravel race in Vermont called the Raspatootsa, and the Belgian Waffle Ride. No specific course details, terrain conditions, challenges, or race-day advice related to the 65-mile, 6000-foot elevation 3 State 3 Mountain Challenge could be extracted from these video transcripts.

name: Barry-Roubaix Killer Gravel Road Race
tagline: 100+ mile Michigan gravel with 1000+ riders in Hastings.
st: The provided transcript from the Barry-Roubaix Killer Gravel Road Race appears to be primarily music and audio fragments without substantive race content. No specific course features, terrain conditions, or rider experiences are described in the available transcript. The 100-mile gravel race in Hastings, Michigan with 3500 feet of elevation gain requires additional video content to provide meaningful race intelligence for prospective participants.

name: Carpathian Gravel
tagline: Romanian Carpathian mountainous gravel.
st: The Carpathian Gravel route crosses the Carpathian Mountains with challenging terrain in the first half featuring lots of ups and downs, followed by more gradual descents following a river. Riders encounter military checkpoints requiring passport verification when crossing regional boundaries (Oblast). The route includes extremely steep and fast descents that require constant braking at speeds around 10 km/h, followed by long climbs back out of valleys. Road quality varies significantly, often poor in certain areas, but the surroundings are described as stunning and gorgeous. Weather conditions include occasional downpours and mountains covered in mist. The elevation profile drops from around 900 meters to 160 meters over the latter portion. Local communities are described as very nice and welcoming despite economic challenges in the region.

name: Dirty Hector - New York State Championship
tagline: New York State Championship gravel in Finger Lakes wine country.

name: Fools Gold Gravel Grinder
tagline: North Georgia mountain gravel in Dahlonega.
st: Fools Gold is described by riders as normally being a mountain bike race with 38-mile and 54-mile options starting at Montaluce Winery. The course begins with asphalt riding and a fun downhill where groups stay tight, then crosses a bridge into country roads with mountain climbs and descents, followed by fast fun single track sections. However, riders warn about the rain route - when weather conditions are poor, the Department of Forestry can shut down off-road sections, converting the entire race to all gravel instead of the normal mountain bike terrain. This can catch riders off guard who come prepared for mountain biking but find themselves racing an entirely different gravel course due to weather restrictions.

name: Gran Fondo Ozarks
tagline: Arkansas Ozark Mountains gravel in Eureka Springs.

name: Gravel del Fuego
tagline: Chilean Patagonia gravel adventure.
st: Gravel del Fuego takes place in Patagonia, Chile, in the far southern region near Antarctica. The race location is characterized by extreme wind conditions that require riders to prepare for harsh weather exposure. The area covers the southern regions of both Argentina and Chile, with riders describing it as one of the most remote locations they have ever been to for cycling. The wind conditions are particularly notable, with experienced guides recommending head protection even during warmer periods due to the constant ripping winds throughout the day. The race represents an opportunity to experience one of the most geographically isolated and challenging environments in gravel cycling.

name: Gravel Medellín
tagline: Colombian Medellín mountain gravel challenge.
st: The Transcordilleras gravel race in Colombia crosses three mountain ranges from north to south, with elevations ranging from 200m in valleys to 3,900m in the paramo where it's cold and hard to breathe. Colombian roads come in two flavors according to riders: steep or crazy steep. The terrain features extremely rough descents that are back-pinching and arm-shaking, making riders happy when climbs begin. Key challenges include a six-mile 10% grade climb from guatape and racing through active construction zones. Riders recommend switching to larger chainrings like 44t and using 51t cassettes for the steep terrain. The race features F1-style quick stops at tiendas where locals help riders. For gear, experienced riders suggest minimal kit - one bib and jersey washed daily for multi-day events, plus essential weather protection including windbreakers and gore-tex jackets. The community aspect is strong with local support and experienced riders setting pace for groups.

name: Gravel Suisse
tagline: Wild Alpine gravel from the World Cycling Center in Aigle to the heights of Villars.
st: Gravel Suisse features a brutal climbing profile with the main challenge being a long first climb containing multiple pitches of 25% grade that riders describe as really grim. From mile 30 to 60, the course is basically uphill through horse territory with single trail sections and never-ending steep climbs. The race starts from the UCI Cycling Center in the medieval town of Aigle. Riders recommend conservative pacing strategy, letting others go hard early then catching groups on the climbs. Technical descents after major climbs require local knowledge and confidence. Successful riders used 40-52 gearing for the steep pitches and maintained high cadence. The terrain includes single trail sections through horse territory with very steep, sustained climbing efforts that accumulated significant elevation gain.

name: Grinduro Italy
tagline: Italian coastal mountain gravel in Finale Ligure.
st: No race-specific information about Grinduro Italy at Finale Ligure is available in the provided transcripts. The transcripts discuss unrelated cycling topics including mountain biking in Val Argentina, general bike trail discussions, and EWS bike touring experiences, but contain no content about the Grinduro Italy race, its 60-mile course, 5500-foot elevation profile, terrain conditions, or rider experiences specific to this event.

name: Kettle Mettle
tagline: British Columbia mountain gravel challenge.
st: The Kettle Mettle in British Columbia features deceptive terrain that starts with calm wide gravel flats before hitting challenging climbs on Forest Service roads behind Princeton. Riders report the gravel gets looser and climbs punchier than expected, with long exposed stretches testing legs and mindset. Only a small portion uses the anticipated KVR trail, surprising many participants. The pristine gravel roads offer spectacular views of Princeton and surrounding area. The course features varied terrain that challenges riders more than expected. Rock hazards pose flatting risks for riders following too closely. The grassroots event attracts diverse age groups including many riders in their 50s and 60s, emphasizing personal achievement over speed. The race philosophy focuses on showing up and getting a good workout rather than competitive pressure, making it appealing for those transitioning from other disciplines like triathlon to gravel riding.

name: King Price Race to Sun
tagline: South African mountain gravel stage race.
st: The King Price Race to Sun is a 165km gravel race in South Africa's Northwest Province, starting at U Resort on Hartebeestpoort Dam and finishing at Sun City. Riders describe it as the original South African gravel race that introduced the gravel scene to the country. The course features hard and fast dirt roads along the Brits canal irrigation system with 1000m of elevation gain over 100 miles. Terrain is notably rough, described as the roughest it's ever been, with constant movement required on gravel bikes. The race typically sees high early pace around 38k/hour average to the second water point. Key tactical sections include pinch points where position is critical and a small ditch/climb around 21km used for attacks. Recent route changes eliminated problematic corrugated roads and added a compact gravel section through Sadiba quail adventure center. The course is backloaded with new features in the second half. Weather conditions are typically perfect with wide roads allowing safe overtaking.

name: Ozark Gravel
tagline: Arkansas Ozark hills gravel in Rogers.
st: The Ozark gravel scene in Arkansas offers challenging terrain around Rogers and Fayetteville.
