Riders describe very chunky jeep trails with loose rocks, steep barely rideable climbs, and sections requiring walking. The area features over 300 miles of routes with close to 30,000 feet of climbing for ultra-distance events. Team formats require staying together as a group. The terrain includes creek crossings that provide cooling opportunities during summer rides. Fayetteville serves as a vibrant base town with restaurants and downtown amenities. Disc brakes and comfortable gravel bikes are recommended for the rough surfaces. Riders mention 13-mile loop options and technical sections. The rural Arkansas setting offers authentic working-class farm country atmosphere. Power meters help with pacing on the varied terrain that ranges from easy spinning to maximum efforts on steep climbs.

name: Tour de Pologne Amatorów
location: Southern Poland (route varies — typically Krakow area / Tatra Mountain foothills)
tagline: Ride the roads of Poland's WorldTour stage race. Southern Poland's Tatra foothills, historic cities, and the most affordable serious sportive in Europe.

name: Tour of the Battenkill — Gran Fondo
location: Cambridge, Washington County, New York, USA
tagline: 64 miles through Washington County's farm roads. Covered bridges, stone walls, and dirt sectors that predate the gravel movement by decades.

name: Wörthersee Gravel Race
tagline: Austrian Alps gravel challenge.
st: The available transcript from the Wörthersee Gravel Race contains primarily fragmented audio with limited coherent race intelligence. The video appears to capture race atmosphere with background music and crowd interactions, including encouraging words like 'Have fun. Enjoy your race' to participants. Some references to heat conditions are mentioned multiple times throughout the footage. However, the transcript lacks detailed course descriptions, specific terrain analysis, gear recommendations, or strategic racing advice that would typically be valuable for race preparation. The content suggests this is documentation of the race experience rather than instructional or analytical coverage of the 75-mile Austrian gravel event.

name: 3Rides Aachen
tagline: German mountain gravel in Winterberg/Aachen.
st: The available transcript from the 3Rides Aachen race in Winterberg/Aachen, Germany consists primarily of music lyrics and motivational content rather than race-specific commentary. No detailed information about the 65-mile course, its 4500 feet of elevation gain, terrain conditions, or specific challenges is provided in the available video content. Riders seeking actionable intelligence about course features, gear recommendations, pacing strategies, or race logistics would need additional video sources with more detailed race commentary to make informed decisions about participating in this Tier 3 gravel event.

name: 6 Hours of Syllamo
tagline: 6-hour Arkansas endurance on Syllamo Trail in Mountain View.

name: 66° South Pyrénées
tagline: French Pyrénées mountain gravel.
st: The Pyrenees crossing presents diverse terrain that changes daily as riders move from the Mediterranean toward the Atlantic. Key challenges include the Port Caboose climb serving as the gateway back to Spain from Andorra, and sudden thunderstorms that can trap riders on exposed mountaintops requiring emergency shelter. The terrain includes rough hike-a-bike sections where shortcuts become challenging portages. Weather is highly variable with frequent rain jacket changes needed throughout the journey. Livestock including horses and cows frequently occupy roads, particularly on descents, requiring constant vigilance. The route covers approximately 1,000 kilometers over two weeks with nearly 25,000 meters of elevation gain. Camping spots near mountain refuges may be shared with curious horses that can disturb sleep. The diverse landscape and rapidly changing conditions make this a challenging multi-day adventure requiring weather preparedness and flexibility.

name: Ardenne Gravel Stages
tagline: Belgian Ardennes multi-stage gravel in Vielsalm/Engreux.

name: Berryman Epic
tagline: Missouri Berryman Trail mixed surfaces in Potosi.
st: The Berryman Trail in Missouri is described by riders as one of the premier trails in the state, featuring challenging rocky terrain that demands proper preparation. The trail includes a 16-mile section from Berryman campground to Brazil Creek that typically takes longer than expected, with riders noting the need for extra time planning. Key challenges include brutal climbs even on road shortcuts, potentially high creek crossings, and technical rocky surfaces that affect gear choices like gloves. The terrain is described as really rocky and demanding. Riders recommend strategic refueling at Brazil Creek campground around the 16-mile mark. The trail offers beautiful fall riding conditions and scenic descents, though technical skill is required for safe navigation. Training for longer distances on this trail is noted as particularly tough and demanding.

name: Black Forest Gravel
tagline: German Black Forest mountainous gravel in Freiburg.
st: The Black Forest offers challenging gravel riding with long, dirty roads and very steep climbing that riders consistently emphasize is much harder than other regions. Key challenges include navigating tight corners that become slippery in dark forest conditions. The terrain features extensive climbs where riders need to gather all their strength, with some routes reaching near the highest mountain in the Black Forest. Riders access the area via train from Freiburg, with routes including loops around Lake Titisee. The forest environment creates mysterious, dark conditions with challenging visibility. Riders strongly recommend being conservative with climbing plans and paying extra attention to corners. The area offers beautiful lake stops for swimming and connects via the Tisam river back to Freiburg. Multiple riders describe finishing completely exhausted after 4-5 hours of riding due to the demanding steep terrain throughout the Black Forest hills.

name: Cheaha Challenge
tagline: Alabama Cheaha Mountain gravel in Jacksonville.
st: The Cheaha Challenge in Jacksonville, Alabama is a 65-mile gravel race with 6000 feet of elevation gain that has become widely known in the cycling community and brings positive economic impact to the county. According to race coverage, the event attracts long-distance cycling enthusiasts to the area. However, safety concerns have been documented, with at least one incident involving a cyclist being shot with a pellet rifle approximately seven miles from the finish line during a race. Despite the injury requiring medical attention, the affected rider completed the race. Law enforcement investigated the incident, which was linked to similar shootings in the White Plains community area. The race takes place in rural Alabama terrain where cyclists may encounter various road users and conditions typical of the region.

name: GRUSK
tagline: West Virginia mountain gravel in Circleville.

name: Hot Springs Gravel Gauntlet
tagline: Appalachian gravel in remote western North Carolina mountains.
st: The Hot Springs Gravel Gauntlet features challenging terrain with significant climbing despite shorter distance options. The course includes a first gravel section with potholes, followed by an immediate climb after a right turn. A key descent has loose gravel with left-right turns before hitting pavement halfway down. Hurricane Gap provides a long descent with potential car traffic and scenic tower views at the top. The brutal final climb is described as the steepest ever ridden, forcing many riders to hike-a-bike. Route 25 features an all-downhill finish where riders exceed 30 mph, requiring caution with car traffic. Course conditions include super loose sections without grooves. Race morning temperatures were 34-36 degrees with snow visible on higher mountain elevations. The event was first held in 2021 after delays since 2020, with both short course and longer options available.

name: Iron Cross
tagline: Pennsylvania gravel/cyclocross with technical elements.
st: Iron Cross in Pennsylvania is a 56-mile gravel race with significant elevation and technical challenges. The course features mixed terrain including road, gravel, soft pack, and technical single track sections. Multiple hike-a-bike sections with steep rocky climbs are a defining feature, with some climbs feeling twice as long as they actually are. Riders report needing granny gears for extended climbing sections, though single speed completion is possible. The constant elevation changes mean no drafting or pack riding - success depends entirely on individual fitness. Technical rock features require bike handling skills. The terrain switches frequently, mixing riders of different abilities throughout the race. Despite the difficulty, riders praise the supportive community atmosphere and beautiful fall scenery in Central Pennsylvania. Many first-time participants find it challenging enough that they don't return, though repeat racers appreciate both the tough reputation and positive vibe.

name: Little Apple 100 - Illinois State Championship
tagline: Illinois State Championship 100-mile gravel through farmland.

name: Lost and Found Gravel
tagline: California Sierra Nevada gravel in Portola.
st: Lost and Found Gravel in Portola, California features 96-97 miles with 6,000-8,000 feet of climbing. The race starts with brutal early climbing including 16,000 feet of elevation in the first 5 miles, with steep sections hitting 12% grades before mile 9. Key challenges include a hidden left turn at mile 8.4 miles where the course transitions to dirt, and climb six around mile 33 - a 2.7-mile brutal climb with 650 feet of gain on loose, chunky gravel. Riders face a big empty valley around mile 30 with brutal headwinds that continue to the finish. The course features extremely dusty conditions that affect chain lubrication and visibility, mixed with water crossings. Terrain includes primo gravel sections, washboards, and loose gravel on climbs. Veterans recommend starting conservatively due to early climbing, positioning well in the pack for dust visibility, and bringing chain lube due to harsh conditions.

name: Niseko Gravel
tagline: Japanese Hokkaido mountain gravel.

name: Ouachita Challenge
tagline: Arkansas Ouachita Mountains gravel in Mount Ida.
st: The Ouachita Challenge starts with 3 miles of pavement before hitting double track gravel where heart rate effort significantly increases. The first major challenge comes at miles 6-7 with around 1000 feet of elevation gain on a jeep road, getting steep before the single track. At mile 9, riders hit the Watchtower trail, the first single track section known for being extremely rocky with large rocks that force riders to dismount and walk. The course features beautiful pine trees and gravel roads through southwestern Arkansas. Riders describe the terrain as super gnarly and rocky with awesome trails. The rocky sections humble even experienced mountain bikers, with the large rocks creating significant technical challenges that separate the field early in this 60-mile race.

name: Pirate Cycling League Gravel
tagline: California coastal mountains gravel in Ojai.

name: Race to Valhalla
tagline: South Carolina mountain gravel in Walhalla.
st: Race to Valhalla is a roughly 70-mile gravel race in Walhalla, South Carolina that takes experienced riders around 3 hours and 46 minutes to complete. The defining feature is the Winding Stair climb, a four-mile ascent taking over 20 minutes that riders describe as the pivotal moment breaking up the race where winners rise to the top. The climb begins with a left turn across a bridge where the action intensifies and riders start attacking. Smart pacing strategy is crucial - riders who go out too hard early get caught by those maintaining steady power. The climb allows stronger climbers to separate from the field and form elite groups for podium contention.

name: RAW — Ride Across Wisconsin
tagline: 175 miles of paved Wisconsin backroads in a single day. Rolling farmland, river valleys, and the Chippewa Valley — the Wisconsin Bike Fed's flagship fundraiser and the state's defining one-day cycling event. ~1,000 riders, 11 years of tradition, and rest stops stocked with Midwestern hospitality every 20 miles.

name: Santa Fe Century Gravelón
tagline: New Mexico mountain gravel challenge.

name: Sea Otter Europe Gravel
tagline: Spanish Girona gravel challenge.
st: Sea Otter Europe Gravel in Girona features a mix of nice fast gravel roads with good surface quality combined with technical sections. Riders highlight the technical descents with twisty corners as a key challenge requiring skill and bike handling. The course offers variety between fast gravel sections and more demanding technical terrain. Riders describe the gravel quality as impressive and recommend the area as an excellent destination for cycling. The technical nature of the descents with twisty corners stands out as a defining characteristic that riders need to prepare for.

name: Trans-Sylvania Epic
tagline: Pennsylvania multi-stage mountain gravel.
st: Trans-Sylvania Epic is a multi-day stage race in Pennsylvania's national forests featuring 47-mile opener stages and longer 60-65 mile days with significant climbing. Day four includes 61 miles with 6000 feet of elevation gain. The course features very rocky steep climbs that riders describe as nasty, including a key 2.5 mile climb out of Woodward. Roads are generally in good condition according to experienced riders. The race includes run sections that can be challenging with road cleats. Riders describe the Pennsylvania national forests as super green, lush and beautiful. Four-wheelers on course present some safety concerns. The multi-day format creates an immersion gravel experience through scenic wooded terrain with substantial daily elevation gains and technical climbing sections.

name: Uwharrie Forest Gravel Grinder
tagline: Forest gravel in central North Carolina's Uwharrie National Forest.
st: The Uwharrie Forest trail system features the Wood Run trail with deep historical significance, following the old Fayetteville to Morganton Plank Road through the defunct town of Lawrenceville. Riders encounter Wood Run Creek crossings and multiple power line clearings between wooded sections. Fall conditions create significant leaf surfing opportunities with deep leaf coverage affecting trail surface. Recent controlled burns have left remnants throughout the forest. Key logistical notes include a rerouted trail entrance that now passes Escape rather than the old route. Hunters in orange are common during fall season. The trail connects to other Uwharrie trails including access to Kiwi Trails and connects to the broader trail network. Historical features include visible courthouse foundation remnants from the old Montgomery County seat before it was moved to Troy.

name: Waucheesi Bike Race
tagline: Appalachian gravel in remote southeast Tennessee mountains.
st: The Waucheesi Bike Race in Tellico Plains, Tennessee features substantial climbing with riders reporting approximately 7,000 feet of elevation gain by the halfway point of the 75-mile course. Race conditions can vary significantly with temperatures ranging from pleasant 70-degree weather during the race to much cooler 42-degree temperatures post-race. The course is challenging enough that riders feel accomplished completing it, with some successfully finishing on fat bikes and placing well in their categories. The mountainous Tennessee terrain provides beautiful scenery throughout the event, making it both a physical and visual experience for participants.

name: Balkan Gravel
tagline: Balkan mountainous gravel in Slovenia/Croatia.
st: The Balkan Gravel race takes riders through challenging terrain across Slovenia and Croatia, featuring demanding and dynamic rolling gravel with some asphalt sections. Riders describe the gravel as very rough and the conditions as very hot. Key challenges include a frightening tunnel section that riders warn about, and mountain terrain where mechanical issues can separate groups and cause significant delays. The northern Slovenia sections feature hard packed gravel roads and tarmac, while southern areas contain unexpected challenging terrain. The route climbs out of the Bay of Kotor and passes through dramatically changing landscapes from bustling areas to remote farmland. Weather conditions have been described as perfect despite expectations of horrible weather, though the heat presents a significant challenge for riders tackling this demanding course through the breathtaking but unforgiving Balkan mountains.

name: Grasslands 100
tagline: 100-mile Texas grasslands gravel in Decatur.
st: The available transcript for the Grasslands 100 gravel race in Decatur, Texas does not contain coherent rider commentary or race intelligence. The transcript appears to be fragmented music and partial phrases without substantive information about the course, conditions, challenges, or rider experiences during this 100-mile race with 3,500 feet of elevation gain.

name: Gravel Battle of Sumter Forest
tagline: South Carolina Sumter National Forest gravel in Whitmire.
st: The Gravel Battle of Sumter Forest features a 50% gravel, 50% road mix with varied terrain conditions. Riders describe the course as having chunky, blown-out sections especially before the I-26 underpass, while other gravel portions are smoother. Even the pavement sections are notably rough, making gravel bikes essential. The first major challenge comes at mile 7 with the first dirt section where the large mass start creates hectic conditions and the field begins splitting up. Early race pace is fast with attacks starting within the first two miles. The course features quiet back roads with minimal car traffic, crossing under I-26 twice and including sections on the Palmetto Trail. Riders recommend 38mm slick tires around 50-52 PSI for the mixed surface conditions. The race atmosphere is described as packed and exciting, representing a return to racing post-pandemic with strong community spirit typical of gravel events.

name: Greek Gravel
tagline: Greek mountainous gravel in Peloponnese.
st: Riders describe the Greek Gravel race in the Peloponnese as having significant climbing challenges, particularly an 11 km berg and a queen stage with 3,000 meters of elevation over 40K. The route starts with a difficult exit from Athens involving big highways and heavy traffic for about an hour before reaching quieter, more scenic windy roads. Terrain improves away from civilization with lovely rolling hills and descents. Riders encountered heat, wildfire conditions, and haze during their trips. The second tallest hill in Paras presents a notable challenge when heading towards the port areas. Nutrition planning is crucial as riders experienced issues with inadequate breakfast and lunch on longer riding days. The coastal sections offer relief opportunities for cooling off. Local accommodation requires flexible planning due to the tourist destination nature of Greece.

name: Hellhole Gravel Grind Stage Race
tagline: Multi-day South Carolina gravel in Jamestown.

name: Hungry Bear 100
tagline: Northern Wisconsin century in the Chequamegon area.
st: The Hungry Bear 100 in Cable, Wisconsin features challenging sandy terrain throughout the course, with rocks scattered across the route requiring careful cornering. The most significant challenge comes around mile 80 with a 6.8-mile section called sand dispatch that riders describe as unbelievable and time-consuming to navigate. The actual distance was 91 miles with about 7000 feet of climbing rather than the expected 10,000 feet. The sandy, grovely surface demands technical handling skills. Despite the challenges, riders praise the beautiful landscape that feels like being out west in Montana, with stunning trees and scenery. The course offers a mix of steep hills and technical terrain, making it a memorable and recommended gravel race experience in Wisconsin's northwoods.

name: L'Etape Dubai by Tour de France
location: Dubai Design District (D3) to Expo Village Park, Dubai, UAE
tagline: ASO's Tour de France franchise hits the Arabian desert -- 112 km of flat, fast highway riding through Dubai's skyline with classification jerseys and celebrity pros.

name: Natchez Trace Gran Fondo
tagline: Tennessee Natchez Trace Parkway cycling in Nashville.
st: The Natchez Trace features 444 miles of continuous pavement from Natchez, Mississippi to Nashville, Tennessee. Riders describe it as a smooth country road winding through forest, farmland, and historical areas with no traffic lights or stop signs. The route is noted as safer for cyclists because it's closed to commercial traffic with vehicle speed limits of 50 mph. The scenic byway traverses homelands of American Indian nations and Civil War battle sites. One rider specifically chose a road bike over their usual mountain bike for the speedier pavement conditions. The route offers opportunities to experience Southern culture and history while traveling through beautiful countryside at a cycling pace.

name: Noosa Classic
tagline: 160km through the Noosa Hinterland. Queensland's finest gran fondo. Tewantin to Kandanga and back through rolling valleys, historic villages, and subtropical bushland. Currently on hiatus — but the roads remember.

name: Reliance Deep Woods
tagline: Unmarked Cherokee National Forest gravel — just you, your GPS, and Tennessee mountains.
st: The transcript mentions the Reliance Deep Woods as part of a broader discussion of Southeast gravel events. The race is described as featuring red clay canopy roads that provide challenging riding conditions. Riders are advised to bring climbing gear as the terrain is not flat. The event appears to be community-focused, with proceeds benefiting local programs. The race takes place in an area known for scenic riding with significant climbing challenges. The terrain includes red clay surfaces under tree canopy that riders will encounter throughout the 65-mile course with 4500 feet of elevation gain.

name: Reliance Tennessee Gravel
tagline: Appalachian foothills gravel in remote southeast Tennessee.
st: The Reliance Tennessee gravel race features challenging terrain including dangerous bridge sections where crashes resulting in concussions and shoulder injuries have occurred. The course includes sustained climbs with 7% grades where riders slow to 7 miles per hour. Surface conditions can be hazardous, particularly tar sections that become slick when wet. The Web Brothers store serves as a key landmark and appears to be the only store in the area, making it an important logistical point. Riders are using Continental tubeless tires in 28mm widths for the varied terrain. The scenic route passes through beautiful areas that are popular with motorcycles and connects to areas like Cage Cove. The challenging nature of the course requires careful attention to technical descents and sustained climbing efforts.

name: Rooted Vermont
tagline: Vermont rolling to mountainous gravel in Morrisville.
st: Rooted Vermont features significant climbing with over 20,000 feet of elevation in the first 18 miles according to riders. The course includes challenging technical sections called class four sectors that create bottlenecks and separate the field, particularly around miles 9 and 13. Riders describe absolute mayhem in these sections with common mechanical issues including seat post failures. The race offers beautiful views at aid stations with pickle shooters available. Camping options range from basic setups to elaborate overland vehicles and rooftop tents. Strategy involves starting near the front to avoid bottlenecks during the neutral start, then working up through the field while always having wheels to follow. The terrain alternates between serious climbing sections and nice flat recovery areas, making it ideal for riders who enjoy significant elevation gain.

name: Safari Gravel Race
tagline: Kenyan safari gravel adventure.
st: The Safari Gravel Race in Kenya's Hell's Gate National Park presents unique challenges for gravel riders. The course runs through a game reserve where wild animals like zebras, impala, giraffe and buffalo cross the road unexpectedly. Riders must navigate rough, bumpy terrain with big holes, gullies and ruts on descents, plus livestock including cows on the road. The race starts cool in the morning but becomes very hot by midday, with altitude adding difficulty. Water stops are supplemented by small shops along the course. Traffic and animals frequently travel on the wrong side of the road around blind corners. The bumpy conditions can cause mechanical issues with electronic shifting systems. Despite technical challenges, riders praise the professional race organization and vibrant local cycling community. The course features steep climbs that some riders walk, followed by long descents to the finish. Dust becomes an issue around 30km into the race. The setting in Hell's Gate National Park, where Lion King was based, provides stunning Great Rift Valley scenery.

name: Salida 76
tagline: Salida 76. 76 miles of gravel through Salida.
st: Salida 76 is a challenging 76-mile gravel race in Salida, Colorado at 7,000 feet elevation. The course features a brutal 14-15 mile opening climb gaining 4,000 feet to over 10,000 feet elevation, requiring extended time in granny gears. Riders then descend into South Park for 40-50 miles of rollers before tackling the backside of the same climb around mile 65. The finish line is positioned at the crest to avoid a sketchy 3200-foot descent back to town that's open to traffic. Weather can be variable with chilly morning starts in the 40s and potential mud conditions from overnight precipitation. Riders recommend wider tires (40-45mm preferred over 35mm) and lower gearing for the sustained climbing. The race has been running for about 5 years in this historic mining town known as Colorado's banana belt for its mild climate.

name: Spirit World 100
tagline: Arizona desert gravel 100-miler.
st: The Spirit World 100 features a challenging 17.5-mile climb to Ducane Pass before descending into Low Kill, Arizona, showcasing the entire course's variety in one section. Riders face 7000 feet of climbing with constant ups and downs throughout the 100-mile route. The course includes technical challenges like deep sand pits where riders fishtail at 25 mph, creek crossings that splash riders, rocky shelves, and hot sun-exposed descents. Wind can be a significant factor, blowing consistently and making the ride substantially more difficult. The race takes place in early November and is part of a three-day festival featuring music, cycling, and community celebration. The course utilizes hundreds of miles of ranch roads around Patagonia, Arizona, located 18 miles north of the Mexican border. The terrain offers access to 90,000 acres of open space in the Sonoita Valley.

name: UCI Gravel Dustman
tagline: UCI Gravel World Series stop at a Khmer Empire outpost—jungle gravel in Kanchanaburi, Thailand.
st: The UCI Gravel Dustman in Kanchanaburi, Thailand presents extreme muddy conditions due to heavy rainfall, earning it the nickname 'mud man' from participants. The 81-mile course features more than 10 river crossings combined with extensive muddy terrain that forces riders to dismount and push their bikes, particularly in the final 4 kilometers. One experienced rider described it as the hardest gravel race they had participated in. The thick mud becomes especially challenging after 85 kilometers when riders report their legs and arms being completely exhausted from the conditions. The race takes place at the Eva Hotel with registration expo the day before. Despite the brutal conditions that leave riders physically drained, participants describe the experience as very fun and rewarding, though the muddy terrain dominates the racing experience throughout the course.

name: Walburg Dirty 30
tagline: Thirty miles of pure Texas Hill Country grit - where limestone legends are made

name: Wild Horse Gravel
tagline: Western Colorado high desert gravel in De Beque.
st: Wild Horse Gravel is an inaugural event in De Beque, Colorado featuring two course options - a longer Alton back course with two timed segments and a shorter course with one timed segment. The event follows a grinder format where it's more of a ride than a race, though some riders take the timed segments seriously. The course features loose and rutted conditions requiring careful line choice, with the first timed segment ending in a very steep climb followed by another steep hill to the neutral water station. There's a cut-off time in De Beque for the long course. Riders encounter high desert scenery, wildlife including cows and wild horses, and several flatter open areas with expansive views. Weather starts cool requiring arm warmers but warms to low 70s Fahrenheit by late morning. The format emphasizes scenery and photography over pure racing, making it relaxing compared to traditional timed events.

name: Wish One Millau
tagline: French plateau gravel in Millau Grands Causses.
st: Based on available race footage from the Wish One Millau gravel race in France's Millau Grands Causses region, riders specifically mention heat as a notable factor during the 70-mile event. The race covers 4500 feet of elevation gain through the French countryside. While detailed course information is limited in available transcripts, the emphasis on heat conditions suggests riders should prepare for warm weather racing in this region of France. The race appears to be part of organized gravel racing events in the area, though specific terrain details, technical sections, and gear recommendations are not detailed in the current race footage transcripts.

name: Dead Swede Gravel
tagline: Northern Wyoming high plains gravel in Sheridan ranch country.
st: Dead Swede Gravel in Sheridan, Wyoming features challenging sustained climbs that riders describe as difficult grinding sections. The race offers multiple distance options including 20, 40, and 100-mile routes. Weather conditions during race day were ideal at 75 degrees with light winds. The 30-mile aid station serves as a key checkpoint providing bacon and refreshments in a well-organized setup that riders praise as race MVPs. The course includes significant elevation with big climbs that test riders' pacing strategies. The 20-mile option alone contains substantial uphill sections that are described as no joke for the distance. Riders emphasize the importance of proper pacing throughout the event. The race attracts families and young riders, creating an encouraging community atmosphere. The Wyoming countryside provides scenic views with rolling terrain and sustained climbing sections that challenge participants across all distance categories.

name: Eislek Gravel
tagline: Luxembourg Ardennes gravel challenge.

name: Flanders Legacy Gravel
tagline: Belgian Flanders gravel challenge.
st: The available transcript content for Flanders Legacy Gravel is extremely limited and fragmented, containing only brief mentions of heat conditions and racing intensity. The transcript appears to be incomplete or corrupted, with repeated references to heat and racing dynamics but no clear information about course features, terrain conditions, specific challenges, or actionable race intelligence. No concrete details about the 75-mile Belgian gravel course, its 4000 feet of elevation, or specific sections are provided in the available material.

name: Flint Hills Death Ride
tagline: Kansas Flint Hills gravel in Cottonwood Falls.

name: Gravel Grit 'n Grind
tagline: UCI Gravel World Series stop on the Swedish coast—lakeside forests, farm roads, and Nordic grit.
st: Gravel Grit 'n Grind is a 3-day stage race in Halmstad, Sweden featuring a time trial on Friday, UCI race on Saturday, and swap race on Sunday. The TT course is 17km with technical sections in the first 10 minutes before opening to farm roads, including a steep kicker that most riders walk and another steep climb near the finish. The UCI race begins with a mass start neutral section followed by a crucial 5-minute gravel climb where groups split. Riders emphasize the importance of starting well-positioned in the neutral zone, as being too far back affects placement on the decisive climb. The course can vary dramatically based on weather conditions - dry years produce fast racing while muddy conditions make it significantly harder. The TT stage serves as a good leg opener for the longer UCI race.

name: Hell of Hunterdon
tagline: Rolling New Jersey hills gravel in Hunterdon County.
st: The available transcripts for the Hell of Hunterdon gravel race contain minimal substantive content about the race experience. One transcript mentions reaching the top of an elevation called 'pickle' with scenic views and horses in the area, suggesting the course includes some significant climbs with rural countryside scenery. However, the transcripts lack detailed information about course conditions, terrain challenges, gear recommendations, or specific race strategies that would typically be found in race analysis videos.

name: Houffa Gravel
tagline: Belgian Ardennes gravel in Houffalize.
st: This transcript provides limited race-specific intelligence about the Houffa Gravel race in Belgium. The rider mentions it's their second World Gravel Series event and notes logistical planning required, being about an hour and a half away from the race location. The surrounding area is described as flatter than France with quiet roads and located around historical battle sites. However, the transcript primarily focuses on travel preparation and pre-race logistics rather than course details, terrain conditions, or race-specific challenges that would be useful for other riders preparing for this event.

name: Kal-Tour Dirty 100
tagline: Kal-Tour Dirty 100. 100 miles of gravel through Kalamazoo.
st: The Kalamazoo area offers excellent cycling infrastructure with separated bike lanes and bike highways along the west coast of Michigan. Riders encounter crushed limestone trail sections that can be challenging and slow going, though manageable in dry conditions. The terrain includes sections through Holland, Saugatuck, and South Haven areas. Nutrition becomes critical on longer rides, with riders reporting their bodies craving more organic options like fruit smoothies over typical gas station fare. Headwinds are a common challenge in the region. Wider tires (28mm vs 23mm) are recommended for the crushed limestone surfaces. The area provides good support infrastructure with health food stores available in Kalamazoo for better nutrition options during long training rides.

name: King of the Lake
tagline: German Lake Constance rolling gravel.
st: The Lake Constance cycling route is a 250 kilometer signposted bike path that circles the lake through Germany, Austria, and Switzerland. The eastern German shore features villages, hotels, and private properties with limited lake views, while the western Swiss side runs closer to the shore with longer stretches between settlements. The route includes gravel sections that allow for easy detours and excursions. Austria's elegant city of Bregenz sits between Germany and Switzerland, offering beautiful promenades and beaches. The Rhine River delta area provides nature park wetlands for bird watching. Villages appear every few kilometers with impressive architecture, and the route offers abundant accommodation options including hotels, guest houses, campsites, restaurants, and cafes. The terrain is described as suitable for relaxing cycling and beginner bike touring rather than high-mileage days.

name: La Drômoise Gravel
tagline: Vineyard passages and Vercors cliff views through the Drôme and Bez valleys of France.

name: Lauf Gritfest
tagline: Welsh mountain gravel in Cambrian Mountains.
st: Lauf Gritfest in the Cambrian Mountains features a challenging 65km Saturday stage with 1350 meters of climbing through incredible trails around woods and reservoirs. The course includes a brutal climb towards the end followed by a technical descent that challenges even experienced off-road riders. The prologue is particularly gnarly with hardcore single track sections. Riders report the first off-road section is quite greasy requiring careful handling. Night sections are possible with proper lighting. Long queues of 35-40 minutes form at time section starts on weekend stages. The terrain feels much smoother when tire pressure is dropped to around 14 PSI. Riders describe an overwhelming sense of freedom on the gravel tracks, though frequent stops at time sections and feed stations can disrupt rhythm for some participants.

name: Pony Express 120
tagline: 120-mile Kansas farmland/prairie gravel in Marysville.
st: The Pony Express 120 in Marysville, Kansas features challenging gravel terrain on B roads and farm roads with significant elevation changes. The signature climb is Brewsky Hill, the first major ascent that rewards finishers with a free beer ticket. Riders describe the surface as dusty with good rock on flatter sections and challenging gravel overall. Weather conditions typically include hot temperatures with headwinds on the return leg. The race offers multiple distance options from 30 miles for beginners to 120 miles, with the shorter course being mostly flat while the full distance provides fast downhills and tough climbs. The event incorporates historical elements, routing through locations like Alco Springs Park and Rock Creek Station along the original Pony Express route. Marysville offers free camping at the city park, and Casey's convenience store serves as a popular pre-race fuel stop.

name: Standard Deluxe Dirt Road Century
tagline: 100-mile Alabama dirt roads in Waverly.
st: The Standard Deluxe Dirt Road Century in Waverly, Alabama features punchy, short climbs rather than long sustained efforts that wreak havoc on the peloton and cause group separations. The terrain is hardly flat throughout this part of Alabama. When rain falls during the race, muddy conditions coat riders and bikes, though fenders aren't typically used. The race begins with a relaxed neutral rollout before aggressive racing develops, with leaders launching attacks in the final miles. The event allows any type of bicycle and maintains an informal, rule-free atmosphere typical of gravel racing. Organized by Terrafirma Cycling, the race offers both 60-mile and 100-mile distance options, attracting riders of various ages and abilities to Alabama's challenging gravel roads.

name: Texas Hell Week
tagline: Multi-day Texas gravel summer challenge in Warda.

name: Veneto Gravel
tagline: Italian Veneto hilly gravel in Asolo.

name: 114 Gravel Race
tagline: Portuguese rolling gravel challenge.
st: The provided transcript from the 114 Gravel Race in Portugal contains primarily music, foreign speech, and fragmented audio that does not yield extractable race intelligence. The video appears to be from race day coverage but lacks coherent discussion of course features, terrain conditions, or race-specific advice that would be useful for future participants. No specific information about the 70-mile course, 4000 feet of elevation, challenging sections, surface conditions, or gear recommendations can be determined from this transcript. Riders seeking actionable intelligence about this Portuguese gravel race would need additional sources with clearer commentary about the course characteristics, pacing strategies, or logistical considerations.

name: Alentejo Gravel
tagline: Portuguese Alentejo gravel adventure.

name: Back Forty Highlander
tagline: Rural Ontario gravel through farmland and forest roads.
st: No race-specific information was found in the provided transcripts for the Back Forty Highlander gravel race in Mississippi Station, Ontario. The transcripts contain content about tire testing, hunting adventures, and aviation activities, but do not include any rider experiences, course descriptions, or race-related intelligence for this 62-mile gravel event with 3,500 feet of elevation gain. To provide actionable intelligence for riders considering this race, transcripts from actual race participants or race coverage would be needed.

name: Bear Howard Gravel
tagline: Arizona mountain gravel challenge.
st: The Flagstaff area features challenging climbs including Mount Eldon Lookout Road and Mars Hill. Mount Eldon's steep section is particularly demoralizing because riders can see the full extent of climbing ahead on the wide open road. The climb gains about 3000 feet over 15 miles and is described as destroying riders even when using mountain bikes with lower gearing compared to gravel bikes. However, the descent down Mount Eldon Lookout road is considered one of the best around. Mars Hill offers another punishing climb with the worst section just past the lookout. The area requires careful pacing on climbs and riders should be prepared for mechanical issues like flats. Night riding is possible but requires proper lighting. The terrain includes gravel roads with significant elevation changes through the Flagstaff area.

name: Border Wars
tagline: Creek crossings and red-clay gravel along the Georgia-Alabama border.
st: No specific information about the Border Wars race at the GA/AL border was found in the provided transcripts. The transcripts contain content about motorcycle travel through Wyoming dirt roads, bikepacking in Patagonia, and general cycling experiences, but none mention the Border Wars gravel race, its 65-mile distance, 3500 feet of elevation, course conditions, terrain features, or rider experiences specific to this event.

name: BURGR 95
tagline: Wisconsin northwoods gravel challenge.

name: Burnt Bridge Classic
tagline: Cowichan Valley wine country gravel on Vancouver Island.
st: The Burnt Bridge Classic features mixed terrain including logging roads, gravel paths, pavement, fire roads and single track sections in the Cowichan Valley. The course is quite hilly throughout with a particularly challenging first climb that creates early gaps and requires steady pacing. At the 15K mark, riders pass through an active logging area with equipment present. The terrain is described as great for gravel riding with nothing super technical, allowing for smooth riding after the initial climbs. Riders report the course is incredibly well marked with aid stations positioned appropriately. Weather can change quickly with potential rain, and flat tires are common. Wildlife including cougars have been sighted in the area. The event offers multiple distance options and maintains a grassroots, low-key atmosphere while providing solid climbing challenges and beautiful scenery around Shawnigan Lake.

name: Castellon Gravel Race
tagline: Spanish Castellon gravel challenge.
st: The Castellon Gravel Race features intense climbing with 3,000 meters of elevation gain in under 100K, described by riders as extremely challenging. The course profile consists primarily of climbing and descending sections. Riders recommend suspension forks despite the weight penalty, noting significant time savings on descents and improved confidence on technical terrain. Mountain bike cassettes are suggested for maximum gear range on this technical race. The challenging elevation profile makes this suitable for riders who love climbing but prepare for difficulty. Tire setup recommendations include 45mm width on front for grip with slightly narrower rear tires for reduced rolling resistance. The race is part of the UCI Gravel World Series.

name: Chino Grinder
tagline: Central Arizona high desert gravel in Chino Valley.
st: The Chino Grinder is Arizona's longest running gravel race featuring one big long out and back course. The 2025 edition was shortened from 120 to 108 miles with addition of fire roads near Flagstaff. Key features include a long HC category road climb in the middle of the race and recently regraded roads with low gradient climbing of 1-2% in initial sections. Race conditions were dry and dusty with poor visibility issues, especially during sunrise descents. Start temperatures were below 40 degrees but warmed quickly. The first major descent after the high point is non-technical but straight with some kickers. Riders recommend attacking early to secure clean lines and visibility on descents. Coffee truck opens at 5:00 AM with 8:15 AM start time.

name: Dirty Sheets Gravel Grind
tagline: Rolling Georgia gravel near Atlanta in Chattahoochee Hills.
st: The Dirty Sheets Gravel Grind in Chattahoochee Hills features a mix of terrain that riders describe as challenging but not overly difficult. The course includes a technical single track section and steep, short punchy climbs through chunky rough terrain. These climbs are particularly notable because success depends heavily on line selection - riders who pick good lines can power through, while others may need to walk. The route covers approximately 20 miles around the Chattahoochee Hills area near Atlanta. Riders emphasize that while the course has challenging sections, it remains accessible to a range of skill levels, making it a popular choice for group rides and social events in the gravel cycling community.

name: Farmer's Daughter
tagline: Rocky bridges and rolling farm gravel through upstate New York countryside.
st: The Farmer's Daughter Gravel Grinder is a non-competitive 65-mile event in Chatham, New York, featuring 3,500 feet of elevation gain through Columbia County's gravel roads, paved sections, and single track trails. Riders describe the gravel roads as really dry and hard packed in good conditions, with roots on trail sections that aren't slippery when dry. Weather is a key concern, as rain can make trail sections muddy and significantly change conditions. The event attracts both beginners and veterans, with a lively atmosphere and music at the start. Fast riders sprint off early and disappear ahead, so newer riders shouldn't worry about keeping pace with them. The location is about 90 minutes from Danbury, Connecticut, making it accessible for regional riders.

name: Garmin Gravel Worlds
tagline: Nebraska rolling farmland gravel in Lincoln.
st: Gravel Worlds in Lincoln, Nebraska features continuous rollers that make the race tough and challenging, requiring good climbing ability throughout the 75-mile course. The terrain includes a mix of dirt and gravel surfaces with significant hills starting around mile 20. Riders report the heat can become brutal in later miles, leading to bonking around mile 66. The course includes secondary checkpoints in towns for refueling with water and snacks. Race logistics require check-in at the Schilling bridge building with primary times Thursday noon-7pm and Friday 10am-7pm. Race morning check-in starts at 4:30am but is discouraged due to limited resources. Riders should arrive 30 minutes before start time and line up according to estimated finish times marked on roadside for safety. Traffic congestion is expected with hundreds of participants using different neighborhood entrances.

name: Gravel des Flandres
tagline: Belgian Flanders rolling gravel.
st: Gravel des Flandres is organized by the same team behind the pro Tour of Flanders race, featuring iconic Flemish cobbles and brilliant gravel roads. The event includes famous sections from the pro race including Oude Kwaremont, Paterberg, and finishes on the brutal cobble climb of Koppenberg. Riders face multiple distance options with splits that are easy to miss if not paying attention. Morning conditions can be quite cold with temperatures around 4 degrees Celsius at the 9:00 AM start. The event village is located in the center of Oudenaarde with early sign-on recommended due to limited breakfast options. Post-race amenities include lunch vouchers and beer. The course combines the heritage and culture of classic Belgian cycling with modern gravel racing, creating a challenging mix of cobbled climbs and gravel terrain.

name: Gravel Fondo Louisiana
tagline: Louisiana rolling hills gravel in St. Francisville.
st: The Gravel Fondo Louisiana in St. Francisville features a challenging 65-mile course with 3000 feet of elevation gain described by riders as continuous rolling hills throughout. The terrain includes significant climbs with potholes deep enough to cause mechanical issues like dropped chains. Key course features include a critical turn at 9.83 miles where the 20-mile group splits from longer distances, and a particularly difficult climbing section around the 13-mile marker. Riders report the back stretch as especially challenging with hard hill after hill. The event uses pace cars and cone markings for navigation. Race speeds reach 28-30 mph on flatter sections with groups that crack the whip early in the race. The elevation profile shows 2061 feet of climbing with what goes up must come down terrain characteristics.

name: Gravel One Fifty
tagline: Dutch flat gravel in Roden, Drenthe.
st: Gravel One Fifty is a well-organized 150km Dutch gravel race with 1300 participants featuring an entertaining but demanding route. The course includes technical single track sections with ditches alongside where crashes occur during passing attempts, and lots of sand that requires lower tire pressures than full bar. The pace remains hot throughout with groups not settling as quickly as expected. Key challenges include hydration management in technical sections, with dehydrated riders seeing power drop to 200 watts in final hours. Feed stops offer free food and drinks. Riders recommend wider tires, hydration packs for water capacity, avoiding high tire pressure on sandy terrain, and conservative pacing for the 5+ hour event. The race serves as qualifying for world championships and attracts serious competition.

name: Gravelista
tagline: Victorian rolling gravel in Australia.
st: Gravelista takes place in the small farming town of Seymour, Victoria, starting and finishing at Seymour Race Course. The course features one major climb called Wickets Hill, located at the start of the main loop. The 136k riders complete the loop twice while 95k riders do it once. Key challenges include a creek crossing 500 meters into the gravel section where line choice matters, and a sketchy bridge crossing that narrows significantly as riders enter the main loop. The terrain mixes tarmac and gravel with big muddy puddles that can catch riders off guard. Early positioning is crucial as stronger riders make moves on Wickets Hill. The race starts neutralized on tarmac before hitting gravel where attacks begin. The course offers stunning scenery through farming countryside. Elite competition is fierce with the 2024 men's race decided by just 3 seconds between defending champion Brendan Johnston and Mark O'Brien.

name: Hegau Gravel Race
tagline: German rolling gravel in Singen.
st: The Hegau Gravel Race in Singen, Germany is an extremely demanding course with no flat sections - only steep climbs and steep descents according to riders who reconned it. The climbs are short but require riding above threshold power, with minimal recovery time on equally short downhills. Riders describe it as anaerobic racing where pacing strategy is critical to avoid exploding. The descents feature very steep and twisty off-camber turns. The gravel surface quality is described as pretty good overall. This inaugural race takes place in an area with rich cycling history, having hosted mountain bike Marathon World Cups for over 20 years. Weather can be a major factor with thunderstorms and rain forecasted. Riders note this is a course where you can easily blow up, making it crucial to find the right balance between staying competitive and riding within your limits.

name: KowTown Gravel
tagline: KowTown Gravel. 60 miles of gravel through Kremmling.
st: KowTown Gravel in Kremling, Colorado features three distances with the longest reaching nearly 10,000 feet elevation and significant climbing. The course runs through working cattle ranch land owned by the Shaw family, with smooth gravel roads that are better than many paved surfaces. Key challenges include double-digit grade climbs where riders struggle with gearing, high altitude effects causing very slow 2.5 mph average pace on climbs, and reaching 9,200+ feet elevation. The terrain varies from smooth valley roads along the Colorado River to chunky dirt roads in the mountains. Riders report excellent scenery with wildlife including elk, bears, and moose, plus zero car traffic for hours. The course includes Grouse Mountain as a high point with spectacular descents. Gear recommendations include wider tires around 47-50mm for better traction on varied surfaces.

name: Lone Wolf Gravel
tagline: Upper Peninsula Michigan wilderness gravel in Iron Mountain.
st: The provided transcripts do not contain any race-related content for the Lone Wolf Gravel race in Iron Mountain, Michigan. The transcripts appear to be music videos or songs rather than race reports or rider experiences. No information about course features, terrain conditions, challenges, gear recommendations, or race atmosphere is available from these sources.

name: Montana Gravel Challenge
tagline: Montana Gravel Challenge. 65 miles of gravel through Huson.
st: Based on rider accounts, gravel racing presents unique challenges around terrain conditions and race dynamics. The surface feels dramatically different at race pace compared to pre-riding - what feels manageable at 17 mph becomes rough and loose at racing speeds of 25-30 mph. Dust conditions can severely limit visibility, making it impossible to see other riders or judge gaps. Pinch points and tunnels create critical bottlenecks where positioning in the first mile can determine race outcomes. Cold weather preparation is essential, with riders using thermal jackets and gloves for 40-degree starts. Equipment trends show wider tires becoming standard, with 50mm now common where 45mm used to be considered wide. Mountain bike-width tires up to 2.2 inches allow riders to tackle rocky terrain at full speed without concern. Race preparation involves detailed nutrition planning with hundreds of grams of carbs and strategic caffeine timing.

name: Opelika Okey Dokey
tagline: Rolling Alabama gravel in east Alabama's Opelika.
st: The Opelika Okey Dokey gravel race in Alabama features a unique timed segment format with two sections - a 10-mile segment and a longer 23-26 mile segment. Riders receive ankle bracelets that automatically time them through wire checkpoints at segment boundaries. The race offers 30, 65, and 100-mile distance options on quiet Alabama roads with minimal car traffic. Race organization had some issues with people cutting timing wires on the longer segments. Post-race activities include food and beer at Red Clay Brewing Company in downtown Opelika. Results are viewable immediately on phones after finishing. The area offers camping at nearby Chewacla State Park. Weather can be variable with potential for cold, rain, and wind. The course runs through scenic Alabama countryside with good road surfaces and minimal traffic making for an enjoyable riding experience in this neat small town setting.

name: Purgatory Road Race
tagline: Texas Hill Country gravel in Fredericksburg.
st: The Purgatory Road Race in Fredericksburg, Texas features challenging Texas Hill Country terrain with limestone surfaces that demand steady momentum rather than power surges. The course includes loose gravel descents that punish late braking and embedded rock gardens requiring precise tire placement. Riders describe traction that can shift unexpectedly mid-climb. Race logistics include aid stations every 50 miles where water refills are available, but no support vehicles are allowed on course, making self-sufficiency critical. The race format uses two 100-mile loops allowing midpoint resupply. Starting and finishing in darkness requires proper lighting equipment. Cell phone signal is poor throughout the Hill Country area. Riders report significant elevation gain with just over 10,000 feet of climbing across the full distance. The limestone climbs and technical rock sections test bike handling skills and pacing strategy.

name: Serenissima Gravel
tagline: Italian flat to rolling gravel near Venice.

name: Switchgrass
tagline: Kansas Flint Hills gravel in Leonardville.

name: The GRiT Adventure
tagline: Washington Cascades gravel adventure.
st: The GRiT Adventure at Snoqualmie Pass features challenging descents that riders describe as gnarly and dangerous, especially in wet conditions. Multiple crashes were reported on the descents, with at least one rider developing a knee hematoma from wiping out. Rain significantly increases the difficulty and danger of the course, causing several riders to abandon the race. The event has aid stations where volunteers assist with nutrition needs, particularly electrolytes. Despite the challenging conditions, there's a supportive community atmosphere with riders helping each other and sharing advice about continuing after crashes. The race attracts both experienced and novice gravel riders, with some participants noting the technical difficulty of the terrain.

name: Unravel Gravel (VA State Championship)
tagline: Virginia state championship gravel race.

name: Wollombi Gravel Fest
tagline: NSW valley gravel festival in historic Wollombi.
st: The Wollombi Gravel Fest takes place in Wollombi Valley, about 150 kilometers north of Sydney, starting from Myrtle and Stone Cafe. The inaugural event featured two days of riding with the first year limited to 300 participants but expanding to 1000 spots for 2025. Day one was described as longer with warmer conditions, while day two featured a shorter out-and-back course that was at least 10 degrees cooler with overcast skies providing relief from UV exposure. Pro cyclists Val Botas and Tiffany Cromwell participated and emphasized the fun, non-competitive nature of gravel riding. The event showcases a region close to Sydney that's appropriate for gravel riding, allowing riders to enjoy off-road pleasure without the usual on-road stress of reaching gravel from the city. The weekend included evening social activities with barbecues and informal talks with pro athletes, emphasizing community building through cycling.

name: Big Sky Gravel
tagline: Beartooth Pass gateway gravel with immense Montana landscapes around Red Lodge.
st: No relevant information about Big Sky Gravel race found in the provided transcripts. The transcripts contain content about snooker, general cycling footage, and Swiss tourism, but do not include any rider commentary, course descriptions, gear recommendations, or race-specific intelligence for the Big Sky Gravel race in Red Lodge, Montana.

name: Bohemian Border Bash
tagline: Czech rolling to hilly gravel.
st: The Bohemian Border Bash is described as one of the toughest self-supported off-road races in the world, covering 1,300 km with 24,000 meters of climbing along Bohemian borderlands. The race starts fast with tarmac and light gravel sections featuring many twists and turns where riders fight for position. Key challenges include dense forest sections between checkpoints that become very difficult to navigate at night, with long hike-a-bike sections particularly around checkpoint 2, described as a rock in the middle of mountains. The Bohemian Forest area requires significant walking with riders doing more walking than riding on gravel bikes. Terrain varies from perfect forest gravel roads with fair climbing and fun descents to technical sections requiring careful navigation. Resupply opportunities are limited, especially in Germany on Sundays. Weather conditions include high humidity and cold temperatures when descending from mountains.

name: Canmore Crux
tagline: Canadian mountain gravel challenge.
st: The Canmore Crux features challenging gravel conditions that riders describe as horrible, making for tough riding throughout the course. The race takes place in bear country, with riders needing to carry bear spray for wildlife safety. One rider completed the distance in approximately seven and a half hours, indicating this is a demanding endurance event. The course includes significant climbing and descending sections, with riders mentioning both uphill and downhill segments. The challenging terrain and wildlife considerations make this a technically demanding gravel race in the Canadian wilderness around Canmore, Alberta.

name: CIRREM
tagline: Rolling Illinois farmland gravel in rural Roubaix.

name: Dirty Pecan
tagline: Red clay tunnels and pecan orchards through Florida's panhandle backcountry.
st: The Dirty Pecan in Monticello, Florida offers multiple distance options from 60 to 200 miles, with most of the route extending into Georgia. The terrain varies from hard-packed clay roads to gravel with challenging sandy sections, particularly at Gum Swamp Road 15 miles from the finish, which riders describe as completely blowing up groups. The route features rolling hills through beautiful tree-lined Kennedy roads and pecan orchards. Sandy spots cause significant problems for riders on 40mm tires or less. The race starts early for longer distances, with 150-mile riders beginning at 7am in 40-degree weather. A critical decision point at mile 75 determines whether riders continue for 38 additional miles on the 150-mile route or take the easier 100-mile option. The event draws 480+ riders on various bikes including mountain bikes and fat bikes, with proceeds benefiting local 4-H programs. Camping is available with separate RV and tent areas.

name: Dusty Bandita
tagline: Women-only Montana gravel in grizzly country — bear spray required near Ovando.

name: Essential Gravel
tagline: Self-supported gravel through unmarked Wisconsin forest roads near Ringle.
st: These transcripts provide general gravel riding advice rather than specific information about the Essential Gravel race in Ringle, Wisconsin. Riders emphasize the importance of tire selection, with Victoria Mezcal tires recommended for mixed terrain riding from road to hard pack. Tire pressure requires experimentation starting from manufacturer recommendations, adjusting by 1-2 psi increments to balance traction, comfort and rolling resistance. For steep climbs, riders should move their body weight forward to prevent front wheel wandering. Descending technique involves keeping weight back and using front and rear brakes evenly to avoid rear wheel fishtailing. Nutrition strategy calls for 250-300 calories per hour on long events. The mental approach treats bike and rider as dance partners, sometimes letting the bike lead rather than over-controlling. Essential gear includes repair supplies distributed between bike wallet and saddlebag systems. Line choice focuses on avoiding surfaces where tires will struggle and spin out. The transcripts emphasize gravel riding as a continuous learning process requiring adaptation to varied terrain conditions.

name: GFNY Miami
tagline: 100 flat miles through South Florida's wind corridors. The GFNY series' warm-weather edition where the bridges are the only climbing.

name: Ghost of the Gravel
tagline: Alberta prairie gravel challenge.
st: Ghost of the Gravel in Alberta, Canada features terrain in the foothills with nice hills and beautiful scenery. The race has a relaxed atmosphere with bacon beats and cookie stops along the course. Riders describe it as less aggressive than extreme racing, focusing more on having a good time rather than intense competition. The course takes place on gravel roads through the foothills area with awesome terrain that riders find enjoyable. The event appears to emphasize the fun and social aspects of gravel cycling over hardcore racing mentality.

name: Gravel Fever
tagline: French rolling gravel in Châtellerault.

name: Gravel Revival
tagline: Rolling Tennessee gravel in rural Hickman County.
st: Gravel Revival in Centerville, Tennessee offers multiple course options ranging from 15 to 66 miles over two days near East Hickman High School, about 35 miles southwest of Nashville. The terrain features a variety of gravel road surfaces suitable for different skill levels from beginner to race difficulty. Riders encounter unique challenges including crossing private pasture land with livestock, navigating through gates that must be closed behind you, and potential barbed wire fencing around dam areas. GPS navigation can be unreliable with some sections requiring creative routing through private property. The course includes cow-beaten paths and requires respect for landowner property. One rider used a 43x36 gear combination on challenging sections. The event showcases Hickman County's diverse gravel road network with scenic countryside views and fall foliage.

name: Gravel Roll - Pecan Shaker
tagline: Rolling Georgia gravel in central Georgia's Hawkinsville.

name: Gun Flint 44
tagline: Grand Marais, Minnesota 44-mile rolling gravel.
st: No relevant information about the Gun Flint 44 gravel race in Grand Marais, Minnesota was found in the provided transcripts. The transcripts contain content about Wu-Tang Clan music and RAGBRAI cycling event in Iowa, but do not include any rider experiences, course details, terrain conditions, or race-specific information about the Gun Flint 44 race.

name: Heathland Gravel
tagline: Belgian heathland gravel challenge.
st: Heathland Gravel features challenging terrain with about 50% unroad surfaces including sandy, dry desert sections. Key obstacles include a giant sand pit before the finish line where spectators gather, and 10 miles of technical sandy single track after a 7-mile straight climb at 2-3% grade. Riders emphasize tire pressure is crucial due to puncture risks seen in other races. Early positioning is critical at the start due to tight corners creating log jams. The technical sections require mountain bike pedals for better grip, and the sand pit should be tackled without touching brakes. Desert conditions are described as sandy and dry, requiring careful fueling strategy for the demanding terrain.

name: Holly Shelter Gravel Grinder
location: Southeast North Carolina (Holly Shelter Game Land)
tagline: Coastal plain gravel in southeast North Carolina's Holly Shelter Game Land.
st: Holly Shelter Gravel Grinder takes place in Holly Shelter Game Land near Hampstead, North Carolina. The course features miles and miles of dirt roads through the game land area, with Lodge Road being a major section that connects the main entrances from Hampstead toward Rocky Point. Riders need to be aware of hunting seasons and avoid riding during deer season or other major hunting periods in the game land. Heat can be a significant factor, with heat index potentially reaching 104 degrees, making hydration crucial for race day success. The terrain consists primarily of dirt roads with some pavement sections connecting different areas of the course.

name: Homegrown Gravel Adventure
tagline: Rolling Georgia gravel in rural west Georgia.
st: Homegrown Gravel Adventure in Franklin, Georgia is a mixed-surface race hosted by Wayne Whitesides that takes riders from Georgia into Alabama and back. The 100-mile course is mostly gravel roads with some paved sections, featuring chunky and gnarly descents typical of Alabama and Georgia terrain. The race starts cold with temperatures around 27 degrees Fahrenheit, requiring proper layering. A key challenge occurs early when the race breaks up around mile 15-20 with breakaway formation, and there's a bottleneck section that can separate riders from the main group. Riders describe it as having minimal climbing, making it appealing for those who prefer flatter gravel courses. The event offers multiple distance options including 62-mile and 100-mile routes, with the longer distance crossing state borders for added adventure.

name: Lake Country Gravel
tagline: 70 miles of kettle moraine lake-country gravel through Oconomowoc, Wisconsin.
st: Riders describe challenging gravel racing conditions with rocky, chunky terrain that pounds arms relentlessly over 4+ hours of racing. Courses typically start with smooth, fast gravel roads where groups can work together effectively, then transition to more technical terrain. Key challenges include sandy sections in forest areas that require bike handling skills and sometimes force hiking, plus technical climbs over roots. Riders emphasize avoiding starting too fast, especially when cold starts are required after standing in age pens for 45 minutes before racing begins. Equipment reliability is crucial - many races end due to wheel failures from big hits on rocky terrain. Tire pressure strategy is important, with experienced riders recommending sticking to lower pressures despite puncture risks to maintain tire deformation and suspension benefits. Sandy trails test tired legs significantly and can dramatically slow pace in later race sections.

name: Marly Grav
tagline: Short, steep, and intense gravel through Limburg hills and Château Marly, Netherlands.
st: Marly Grav features a triangular lap course in the Netherlands with riders completing multiple laps. The race presents significant challenges with gravel sections containing marbles that can cause crashes, particularly on banked areas where bikes drift down and lose traction. Mud conditions can be extremely problematic, with sticky mud accumulating heavily on tires and making riding nearly impossible. The course includes transitions between different surface types, and riders must navigate technical gravel sections where wheel slip is common. Race dynamics typically see early breakaways form, with gaps opening and closing throughout the event as riders struggle with the challenging surface conditions and equipment issues that can arise from the demanding terrain.

name: Pickerel Buck40
tagline: Wisconsin 40-mile rolling gravel.

name: Polish Gravel
tagline: Polish rolling gravel in Kraków.
st: This transcript covers casual cycling in Kraków city rather than the Polish Gravel race. The rider describes urban cycling conditions around major tourist attractions like Wawel Castle and the main square. Key features include surprisingly bike-friendly smooth surfaces throughout the city center and compact distances between attractions. The old town presents navigation challenges with confusing cycling rules - some areas are pedestrian-only, others have limited traffic, and one-way streets where bikes can travel both directions. The rider notes that cyclists are allowed in most pedestrian areas including the main square. The medieval streets of the UNESCO World Heritage old town require careful navigation due to mixed traffic rules and varying restrictions for different vehicle types.

name: Rad am Ring Gravel
tagline: Formula 1 arena start, then gravel circuits around the Nürburgring's Green Hell.
st: No actionable race intelligence could be extracted from the available transcript. The transcript appears to contain primarily music lyrics or unclear audio rather than race-specific information about the Rad am Ring Gravel event in Nürburg, Germany. Riders seeking course details, terrain conditions, gear recommendations, or race strategy information will need to reference additional sources.

name: The Crusher
tagline: Remote Upper Peninsula Michigan wilderness gravel.
st: The Crusher in Michigan's Upper Peninsula is organized by Todd Bouquet and features two versions: the mass start and the EX (extra enhanced). The mass start is supposed to be relatively easy gravel roads but recent years have included EX-style sections with unbridged rivers, mud pits, sand, overgrown rocky tracks, and steep single track. The first 16 miles start in darkness and are fast-paced, where mechanical issues commonly surface. Riders report the actual distance being longer than advertised - 203 miles instead of the stated 175 miles. The race requires significant endurance with nearly 15 hours on course. Riders emphasize that having a partner makes the experience much better, and that staying positive after setbacks is crucial. The event has been running since 2018 and is considered an adventure that tests both physical and mental limits in Michigan's challenging terrain.

name: Tor Divide
tagline: UK Peak District gravel challenge.
st: Riders describe the Peak District Tor Divide as a challenging 200k gravel route with 4,000m of climbing through 38 total climbs. Key challenging sections include Winn's Pass with extreme rocky conditions and 10-foot drops, The Broken Road requiring 6.6km of hike-a-bike terrain, and the unpleasant Stony Middleton climb. Terrain features very rocky technical descents that force walking, boggy sections especially after rain, and roads with extreme drop-offs. The unsupported format allows 32 hours to complete with options to sleep or ride through. Riders report completing 20% of climbing in first 30k. Mandatory kit includes 1.5 liters of water. Route shares space with walkers, horse riders, and motor bikers. Weather can bring consistent rain making conditions more challenging. Popular strategy includes overnight stops around Bakewell for two-day approach.

name: Tour de Gap
tagline: Mississippi rolling hills gravel in Fulton.
st: The provided transcripts do not contain content about the Tour de Gap gravel race in Fulton, Mississippi. Instead, they feature coverage of the Tour de France road cycling race, discussing professional cyclists like Jonas Vingegaard and Tadej Pogacar on mountain climbs. The transcripts cover climbing techniques, power-to-weight ratios, drafting strategies, and biomechanics adjustments used by Tour de France riders, but contain no information about the 65-mile gravel race with 3000 feet of elevation gain in Mississippi. No course features, terrain conditions, gear recommendations, or race-specific challenges for the Tour de Gap are mentioned in these video transcripts.

name: Turnhout Gravel
tagline: Belgian Turnhout gravel challenge.
st: Turnhout Gravel is described as the fastest UCI gravel race with a 144km course over two loops and only 200m of total elevation, flat as a pancake. The race features very long straight gravel sections where speeds reach 45k per hour, but the course includes many loose sandy sectors that prove decisive. The first gravel section is relatively smooth allowing for high speeds, though it becomes super crowded with limited room to maneuver around other riders. Starts are extremely fast-paced requiring immediate full gas efforts. The race serves as a UCI Worlds qualifier where finishing in the top 25% of age group qualifies riders, but only the winner receives priority start box positioning at World Championships. Navigation through hundreds of age group riders creates chaos, making it crucial to track front riders and maintain positioning from the start line.

name: Albia Holy Cow
tagline: Mormon Trail history and cattle encounters on Monroe County's pastoral Iowa gravel.
st: Albia Holy Cow is a 60-mile gravel race in Iowa featuring aggressive hills that riders describe as hard and challenging. The course maintains good road quality with no ice conditions reported. Weather plays a significant role, with wind being a major challenge and temperatures expected to warm throughout the day. Riders adopt survival strategies, aiming to reach the 30-mile mark before focusing on the return. The race offers multiple distance options including 50K and 100K variants, with some course modifications made for wet conditions including B-road alternatives and potential hike-a-bike sections. The event attracts spectators who come to support friends and enjoy the race atmosphere in the scenic Iowa countryside.

name: Bear 100
tagline: Northern Wisconsin Northwoods gravel century.
st: The Bear 100 in Laona, Wisconsin is described by riders as a tough early season race that serves as a good primer for the gravel season. The course typically features a challenging muddy two-track section at the end - an ATV trail with deep, thick, slushy mud - though this was removed in recent years due to safety concerns after heavy rain. Riders report hard-packed dirt roads with wet, sticky conditions when there's been recent precipitation, along with numerous potholes and puddles to navigate. The terrain includes beautiful sections through pines and birch trees characteristic of northern Wisconsin. The race starts easy on local ATV trails with bad blacktop, rolling out at 15-16 mph before riders establish working groups. Early sections feature uphill mud that can cause significant physical challenges around mile 9. The course was shortened from 113 to 115 miles due to course modifications, and the race attracts around 140 participants in shorter distance categories, starting from a local bar in town.

name: Cedar Blitz
tagline: West Michigan gravel in Cedar Springs near Grand Rapids.

name: Cow Pie Classic
tagline: Central Michigan farmland gravel in Ionia.

name: Fairfield Harvest Rush
tagline: Harvest-season B-road gravel through Jefferson County — more backroads than most Iowa rides.
st: The Fairfield Harvest Rush attracts competitive riders like Gabe Hobi, who placed second in the 100K distance in 2024. This Iowa gravel race is part of the regional gravel racing scene that draws riders from across the Midwest. The race represents the type of gravel event that has become popular in Iowa, where gravel cycling has emerged as the primary cycling discipline due to the terrain being better suited for gravel bikes than mountain bikes. Riders come from various backgrounds, including former runners and mountain bikers who have transitioned to gravel racing. The race appears to be well-established within the Iowa gravel racing circuit and attracts riders looking for competitive gravel racing opportunities in the region.

name: Glenwood Gravel
tagline: Iowa's original gravel series race, 30 minutes from Omaha through rolling western Iowa farmland.
st: The Glenwood Gravel course is front-loaded with elevation, featuring about half the climbing in the first 25 miles. Key challenges include pitchy 6-8% inclines on miles 3-5, the prominent Pathfield Road at mile 10 with towering sidewalls and nearly 14% grades, and a punishing Quant Road climb at mile 13 with 10% sections. The notorious Farm Road from Hell segment through cornfields can vary dramatically based on wet or dry conditions. Quick climb near mile 48 acts as the race's villain with 11% grades determining the front group. The most technical section is Rally Road descent with bike tire-sized ruts and negative 14% grade requiring careful navigation. The course features some of the best B roads in Western Iowa with dirt and possible mud conditions. Despite challenging terrain, it's one of the faster Iowa gravel series races with winners averaging over 19 mph.

name: Gravel and Granite
tagline: Creek crossings and national park granite country near Tenterfield, Australia.
st: The Gravel and Granite race in Tenterfield, NSW features challenging terrain with significant elevation including a brutal 23% incline climb that forces many riders to walk. The course combines gravel sections with corrugations, multiple water crossings, and freshly bituminized roads that organizers warn are very slippery. Key hazards include slippery causeways with water, cattle grids requiring straight crossings, and potential wildlife encounters.
