The final 18 kilometers feature serious hills with 800 meters of elevation gain at the 80k mark. Strong headwinds significantly impact race difficulty. The event accommodates all skill levels with generous cutoff times and welcomes both gravel and mountain bikes. Two feed stations provide water, fruit, and supplies on the 65k course. Roads remain open to minimal car traffic. The race attracts nearly 800 participants across multiple distance options, making it the largest mass participation cycling event on the calendar for the region.

name: Gravel Unravel - Bon Jon Pass Out
tagline: Washington mountain gravel challenge.
st: The Gravel Unravel - Bon Jon Pass Out in Quilcene, WA features extreme elevation gain starting at sea level and climbing to nearly 4,000 feet. The signature Bon Jon Pass climb hits 3,500 feet by mile 15, requiring an hour and a half of sustained climbing. Riders face 15-18 gravel climbs throughout the course, with many walking sections. Terrain includes loamy, soft ground with moss-covered roads and muddy conditions. Weather can include precipitation with temperatures starting in the 40s. The Pacific Northwest setting offers mountain views and pine forests. Riders recommend 700x44 tires in endurance casing for the sharp rocks and soft terrain, plus low gearing like 30x42 for the steep sections. Equipment security is important due to rough conditions causing mounting failures.

name: Gray Duck Grit
tagline: Southeast Minnesota gravel in Cannon Falls near Twin Cities.
st: No race-specific information available for Gray Duck Grit. The provided transcripts do not contain content related to this gravel cycling race in Cannon Falls, Minnesota. Unable to extract course details, terrain conditions, challenges, or rider experiences from the available sources.

name: Hart Hills
tagline: Hart Hills. 55 miles of gravel through Hart.

name: Lowell Classic
tagline: West Michigan gravel in Lowell near Grand Rapids.

name: Marys Mayhem
tagline: Ancient rock formations and gold-country backroads through Mudgee, New South Wales.
st: Mary's Mayhem in Mudgee is marketed as an easy, laid-back social ride across two days, but riders report the pace becomes intense immediately when tires hit gravel on day one. The 98km first day starts at Small Batch Brewery with climbs toward Gong through Barrel, featuring a challenging first climb from the TAC and a difficult final loop toward Frog Rock where cramping becomes an issue. Day two offers a more relaxed 100km route from Ralston toward Cox Creek through state forest, with some of the smoothest recently-graded gravel roads riders have experienced. Feed stations provide excellent homemade food, water, and brownies. The event finishes at the brewery with a relaxed atmosphere. Despite being called non-competitive, winning times around 3 hours suggest a strong pace throughout.

name: Melting Mann
tagline: Southwest Michigan spring thaw gravel in Vandalia.

name: Real West Gravel
tagline: Eastern Oregon high desert gravel in Pendleton wheat country.
st: Real West Gravel in Pendleton, Oregon features some of the most amazing gravel roads in the state according to participants. The 62-mile course includes pretty smooth gravel roads overall with good climbing on the back side. First-time racers describe the terrain as good gravel with smooth road conditions throughout most of the route. The event is part of the Mudslinger series and attracts the Oregon cycling community. Riders emphasize the personal challenge aspect of the race and the opportunity to explore the gravel roads that this region has to offer. The climbing sections appear to be a notable feature, particularly on the back portion of the course.

name: Ride the Gravel Trails
tagline: Trail variety and rolling countryside gravel through Dakota County, Minnesota.
st: Based on rider experiences in Dakota County Minnesota, this 60-mile gravel race features predominantly hard-packed gravel surfaces that riders describe as solid enough to tap dance on, similar to North Central Florida terrain. The course runs through extensive farmland with soybean and corn fields, featuring numerous roller hills rather than flat terrain. Riders encounter diverse surface conditions including hard-packed clay, thicker gravel sections, and some loose areas along river roads. The route includes climbs that become steeper and more technical toward the end. Farm roads dominate the course with arrow-straight sections and plenty of rollers throughout. Riders should expect dust from farm equipment and school buses. The terrain varies from super hard-packed sections to areas with chatter on climbs. Multiple small towns dot the route including Welch Minnesota, with brief pavement sections between gravel segments.

name: Spring Valley 100
tagline: 100 miles of self-supported gravel through Minnesota's Bluff Country.

name: Sunday Creek Classic
tagline: Queensland hinterland gravel through dairy country.
st: The Sunday Creek Classic is a 120km gravel race in Kenilworth, Queensland with over 3000m of climbing on mostly dirt roads, fire trails and gravel roads. Riders describe it as deliberately really hard with guaranteed hike-a-biking sections. Key challenges include Mount Buggery at 45km requiring 45 minutes of walking where only eight riders cleaned it, and a secret climb spanning the first 92km with death by a thousand tiny climbs from 100m to 840m elevation. The final major climb is Sunday Creek Road itself, described as an hour of relentless uphill. Road conditions vary - some years are brutal with rough surfaces causing arm fatigue, while perfect weather can leave courses in prime condition. The race has minimal support with only two checkpoints offering basic nutrition. Riders emphasize being self-sufficient and managing tire punctures which are common. The course traverses diverse terrain from pine forests through valleys and creeks to open plains, offering spectacular scenery throughout the challenging route.

name: Swamp Fox Gravel Fondo
location: Francis Marion National Forest, South Carolina
tagline: South Carolina coastal forest gravel in Francis Marion NF.
st: The Swamp Fox area in Francis Marion National Forest features very flat terrain with no significant elevation changes, allowing riders to cover high mileage. The course runs on straight forestry roads through native pine forests and marshy areas, with sudden changes between tall pines and swampy bays. Riders encounter three pavement sections of about half a mile each, multiple road crossings including Steed Creek Road, and recently graded roads with numerous potholes. Winter conditions bring mud and pollen in late February. Water sources east of Halfway Creek Road are not suitable for drinking due to brackish salt contamination. The area offers peaceful riding with no car traffic on forestry roads, though morning rush hour creates noise at road crossings. Temperature ranges are moderate, typically between 60-70 degrees for overnight events.

name: The Mane Event Gravel
tagline: Wyoming mountain gravel challenge.
st: Based on rider accounts, this gravel event features four distinct timed segments including a road time trial, gravel descent, gravel climb, and singletrack section, allowing riders to cruise between competitive portions. The course covers challenging terrain with significant dust conditions that create a heavy atmosphere. By the 30-mile halfway point with 3000 feet of elevation gain, riders report feeling more depleted than expected in steady 90-degree temperatures. The event provides full meal service with breakfast, lunch, and dinner included. Riders describe it as having camping options though some opt for lodge accommodations. The course travels around scenic areas with perfect singletrack sections. Tire selections vary widely from 2.1 inches on vintage 26-inch wheel setups to 29 by 2.4 inch tires on modern gravel bikes. The event attracts around 700 participants with varying approaches from serious racing to casual riding focused on the party-to-race ratio experience.

name: The Watermoo
tagline: Southeast Michigan gravel in Dexter near Ann Arbor.
st: The Watermoo in Dexter, Michigan is described as having smooth gravel roads that transition from pavement with rolling hills and some climbs throughout the 110-mile course. The elevation is described as plenty but not too much, making it non-intimidating for riders from flatter areas. The course features beautiful scenery and includes a water crossing that adds intrigue to the race. Riders report that a variety of tire types work well, from slicks to tires with light tread. The event is well-organized with ample parking available at the Jolly Pumpkin and a nearby school with a large parking lot. The route is praised for being absolutely fantastic on gravel bikes with smooth road surfaces.

name: Total Tomahawk Terrain
tagline: Northern Wisconsin Northwoods gravel in Tomahawk.
st: The provided transcripts do not contain any information about the Total Tomahawk Terrain race in Tomahawk, Wisconsin. The transcripts cover general bike maintenance topics including bike fitting techniques, cleaning procedures, and material comparisons between titanium, steel, and aluminum frames. No race-specific content about course features, terrain conditions, challenges, or rider experiences from the Total Tomahawk Terrain event appears in these video transcripts.

name: Waukon 100
tagline: 100 miles through Iowa's Driftless region with 5,000 feet of climbing and river valley gravel.
st: The Waukon 100 features challenging terrain in Iowa's Allamakee County with aggressive hills throughout the course. The opening section has the worst gravel conditions with looser surface affected by truck traffic, leading to a busy paved road with wide shoulders. An early signature feature is the Van Buren drop and steep climb that gets riders' hearts going at the beginning, previously positioned at race end. The course descends through beautiful meandering valleys with trout streams where temperatures run cooler and fishermen may be present. Around mile 20, the first aid station appears surprisingly quickly due to downhill approach, positioned before the first long climb out of the valley. Recent moisture has improved gravel conditions and reduced dust. Riders consistently describe the hills as hard and aggressive, with many adopting a survival mentality for the challenging 100-mile distance through this scenic but demanding Iowa terrain.

name: Clarkes Gambit
tagline: Bushranger-country gravel with a tidal river crossing near Nelligen, Australia.
st: Clarke's Gambit is a challenging two-day gravel event in Nelligen, NSW featuring significant climbing including a grueling 7-8K climb and a 2K climb on clean gravel. The course includes river crossings that guarantee wet feet, rolling hills with packed sand sections, and technical downhills requiring careful line selection. Riders describe mostly safe, comfortable gravel surfaces though some sections are mountain bikey. The terrain can feel jittery where light suspension would help. Weather conditions vary with potential overnight rain helping dust but creating cooler, damper conditions than expected. The event passes through areas affected by previous bushfires that have regrown. Key features include Clyde Ridge Road ridgeline section and multiple challenging climbs requiring granny gear. The two-day format is significantly more demanding than single-day events and requires proper training and fueling strategy.

name: De Ronde Van Grampian
tagline: Private terrain, Bald Mountain singletrack, and Lake Orion views through Oakland County.

name: Fast Fitty
tagline: Shaded canopy, Lacey Lake views, and fast Michigan farmland gravel near Charlotte.
st: The Fast Fitty gravel race takes place in Charlotte, Michigan and offers both 50-mile and 36-mile distance options. According to race participants, cramping can be a significant issue in the final miles of the race, with one rider experiencing severe cramping approximately three miles from the finish line. The race appears to be well-organized with proper podium ceremonies for finishers. Riders note this as a challenging event that requires proper preparation, especially for those who haven't raced recently during the summer season. The race serves as good preparation for other major gravel events later in the season.

name: Galactic Grinder
tagline: New Mexico desert gravel challenge.
st: The Galactic Grinder takes place on April 6th in Cedar Hill, New Mexico, hosted by Aztec Adventures. This is the third annual running of the event. The race offers multiple distance options referred to as orbits of 13, 37, or additional miles. Registration space is limited and riders are advised to book early. The event uses space-themed terminology, calling distance options orbits and registration booking your flight. Cedar Hill, New Mexico serves as the host location for this gravel cycling event. Limited information is available about specific course features, terrain conditions, or race logistics beyond the basic registration details and distance options provided by the organizers.

name: Grassroots Gravel
tagline: Red-rock canyons and Wet Mountain Range views on Pueblo, Colorado gravel.
st: Grassroots Gravel in Pueblo, Colorado offers four distance options: 15, 40, 75, and 110 miles. The signature challenge is a 12-mile climb gaining over 3,000 feet on one sustained ascent. Riders describe the terrain as smooth dirt with no technical sections, though washboard conditions appear immediately after the start line. The course features fast rolling sections through historic Colorado ranches on burntmill road. A highlight is the finish through stem Beach, a private natural area with gravel roads between ponds, accessible only on race day. The race starts downtown with 4 miles of neutral rollout and 6 miles neutral to the finish. Aid stations are well-stocked. The event emphasizes inclusivity, welcoming riders of all abilities from age 10 up, with strong community atmosphere and volunteer support throughout.

name: Gravel Rocks
tagline: Gravel Rocks. 55 miles of gravel through North York Moors.
st: Gravel Rocks in North York Moors features 80k of prime Yorkshire moors combining single track, gravel roads, and ancient drove roads. The course includes challenging climbs where riders report getting stuck halfway up with many walking their bikes. Downhill sections prove technical with many participants struggling on descents. The terrain feels super grippy on gravel tires, especially on proper gravelly double track sections. The route follows prehistoric medieval drove roads with bronze age barrow cemeteries. Weather can vary from grey and cloudy to clearing views. The event includes feed stations and uses a time stage format similar to mountain bike enduro racing. Riders recommend strategic positioning based on climbing versus descending strengths, noting that many competitors struggle with technical descents but can overtake on climbs.

name: Great Otway Gravel Grind
tagline: Jurassic rainforest and tall tree ferns near Great Ocean Road in Victoria, Australia.
st: The Great Otway Gravel Grind features two major climbs that define the race experience. The first is a 15-kilometer steady climb on Road Knight Creek Road at 3-4% grade starting around the 9-mile mark, followed by the Ladness Track section climbing to 540m elevation. The course includes a long technical descent to Apollo Bay with twisty turns and sharp corners on hard-packed but rough gravel. The signature challenge is the Otway Ordeal near mile 50 - a three-stage climb to Mount Sabine summit featuring steep muddy ramps, rutted sections requiring careful line choice, and a final push through rainforest. The gravel quality remains compact year-round due to limited sun exposure in the wet Otway forests, though some sections have loose material in the middle. Timeout zones at key points stop race time for regrouping and nutrition. The race starts and finishes at Forrest Football Ground with early ashphalt sections before transitioning to forest gravel roads through mountain ash and rainforest terrain.

name: Hibernator
tagline: Northern Wisconsin cooler-season Northwoods gravel.
st: The Hibernator takes place in Laona, Wisconsin with 30, 60, and 100-mile distance options starting from a local bar in town. The course features hard-packed dirt roads through thick pine and birch forest cover, creating beautiful scenery that showcases northern Wisconsin gravel riding. Riders report the roads had decent condition overall but can become wet and sticky after rain, with numerous potholes and puddles to navigate. The race starts on local walking and ATV trails with some blacktop sections, providing a flat easy beginning before hitting the forested hills. Key challenges include an aggressive pace increase around mile 19 where the front group drops many riders, and a critical decision point at mile 52 where the 60 and 100-mile courses intersect at the final aid station. Weather can be unpredictable with rain developing throughout race day, making conditions increasingly muddy and miserable in later miles. The course atmosphere is described as beautiful with thick forest cover, though riders should pace conservatively for longer distances to avoid cramping issues.

name: Iowa State Gravel TT Championship
tagline: Iowa state championship gravel challenge.

name: North Wales Gravel X
tagline: Reservoir loops and forest climbing through Clocaenog Forest in North Wales.
st: North Wales Gravel X near Ruthin features a challenging course that starts with a steep climb before transitioning to mainly forest fire tracks and flowing sections along a reservoir edge. At 5k in, riders face a demanding single track section with big puddles and twisty terrain where passing is difficult. The course includes numerous potholes that require constant attention, especially when riding in groups. Weather conditions can be misty throughout the day. The event has excellent feed stops with helpful staff and a strong community atmosphere. There's a time cut at 12:30 at the split point for route selection. The climbs aren't too long and can be rolled over with speed. Riders praise the quality of gravel in the forest sections and describe it as a hidden gem with great scenery that's easily accessible from major motorways.

name: Prairie Burn 100
tagline: Prairie Burn 100. 100 miles of gravel through Grinnell.
st: Prairie Burn 100 is a gravel cycling event in Grinnell, Iowa, offering 25, 50, and 100-mile routes on almost 100 percent gravel roads. The race utilizes Iowa's extensive 79,000-mile gravel road network, with routes chosen based on wind direction. Riders report exceptional safety on gravel roads, often traveling 2-3 hours seeing only one car. The event routes all participants back through Grinnell's renovated Central Park, featuring food trucks and family activities from 9 AM to noon. Organizers recommend road bikes with slightly bigger tires for comfort on gravel surfaces. The event avoids busy gravel roads and selects less-traveled routes. Ride times range from a couple hours to 10 hours depending on distance. This is the fourth year of the event, typically attracting 150-200 riders and supporting local community initiatives.

name: Spring Thaw
tagline: Early-season Michigan gravel when the roads are still thawing near Muskegon.
st: No race-related content found in the provided transcripts. The transcripts contain content about retail crime prevention technology, flooding along the Muskegon River in Michigan, and a hospital recovery story, but do not include any information about the Spring Thaw gravel race in Muskegon, Michigan or cycling-related content that would be useful for race preparation or intelligence.

name: Takelma Gravel Grinder
tagline: Oregon mountain gravel challenge.
st: The Takelma Gravel Grinder is a 55-mile race with over 6,000 feet of climbing, making it the most climbing per mile of any gravel race in Oregon according to organizers. The unique race starts inside Seven Feathers Casino in Canyonville. Riders follow the South Fork Umpqua River for 10 miles before turning onto steep gravel roads that climb 3,000 feet over the next 10 miles. The course features narrow, remote gravel roads with no car traffic and grades reaching 20%. Key challenges include sustained steep climbs that cause experienced riders to cramp, and a final climb with two false summits. The Gailsville Reservoir section provides flat, paved relief between climbs. Heat becomes a major factor as the day progresses, with morning sections offering shade but afternoon sun exposure being brutal. The race attracts 75-80 riders and includes post-race food and drink tickets.

name: West Coast Gravel
tagline: Oregon coast gravel challenge.
st: The West Coast Gravel race in Yachats, Oregon features a challenging 55-mile course starting with a remote start format. The main challenge is a relentless 1,900-foot climb over the first 10 miles that strings riders across the mountainside. A dangerous left turn at mile 2.8 onto Forest Service Road 55 requires caution due to traffic, with fire department stationed for safety. After the initial climb, the course follows an up-and-down pattern gaining and losing elevation until the second aid station, then transitions to primarily downhill. The route includes Forest Service roads with giant potholes before finishing on Yachats River Road, where riders face a stiff headwind for the final flat paved miles. About 200 racers typically start this timed event, which uses sensors rather than finish line placement for results. The scenic Oregon coast setting provides beautiful views despite the challenging terrain and weather conditions.

name: Beaver Dam Gravel Grinder
tagline: Nevada desert gravel challenge.
st: No relevant race intelligence available for the Beaver Dam Gravel Grinder from the provided transcript. The transcript discusses a different event (the Gravel Grind in Fredericton, New Brunswick) which is a recreational fundraising ride for Special Olympics New Brunswick with over 100 participants and routes of varying surfaces, distances and difficulties.

name: Camp Michaux Gravel Grinder
tagline: State forest singletrack and technical gravel through Michaux near Gardners, Pennsylvania.

name: Cowboy Crusher
tagline: Oregon Trail backcountry and open prairie gravel through remote western Wyoming.

name: Dirty Dino
tagline: Ashley National Forest gravel climbing to 10,100 feet in Utah's dinosaur country.

name: Forbidden Gravel
tagline: Canadian coastal gravel challenge.
st: Forbidden Gravel takes riders into the mountains around Cumberland, British Columbia, utilizing logging roads that provide access to remote areas. The course features challenging washboardy descents, particularly on the downhill to Comox Lake. Riders report encountering snow during practice runs, which forced some to drop from longer distances to the piccolo category. A key challenge is route navigation, as practice routes downloaded beforehand may not match the actual race day course. The race organizers encouraged female participation by holding additional spots and offering discounts. Cumberland serves as the race hub and is known as one of the most popular mountain biking destinations on Vancouver Island, with Rider's Pizza serving as a popular stop for cyclists.

name: Hardwood Hustle
tagline: Remote Wisconsin hardwood forest gravel in tiny Nelma.

name: Heywood Ride
tagline: Heywood Ride. 50 miles of gravel through Northfield.
st: The Heywood is organized by Heath Creek Cycles in Northfield, Minnesota, offering both 30-mile and longer distance options. Riders emphasize being prepared for cold weather conditions that can feel much colder than expected, with temperatures in the 40s feeling like the 30s. The most challenging section is the final seven miles which features a brutal headwind that riders describe as the hardest part of the event. The race has a strong community atmosphere, with riders noting the event is more about the people and connections than just the riding itself. The course includes bridge crossings and sections through state park areas. Cold weather preparation is essential, with local tips including using buck bags for feet warmth. Wind conditions can be significant, particularly affecting the final portions of the ride.

name: JUST.GRAVEL
tagline: UK Lake District gravel challenge.
st: Lake District gravel riding features challenging terrain with significant elevation gain from the start. Griesdale Forest offers 200 meters of uphill climbing in the first 20 minutes on well-maintained gravel fire tracks. The terrain includes purpose-built tracks, gravel roads, and narrow roads with steep ascents and descents. During wet conditions, forest trails become waterlogged with river-like crossings that soak through regular shoes. Routes typically accommodate 200 riders with intermediate beginner difficulty suitable for gravel bikes. Weather can significantly impact events, with long routes potentially canceled for thundershowers and modified medium routes offered instead. The area features multiple route options including color-coded trails and various distance options. Social atmosphere is strong with friendly riders and community support. Feed stops provide sandwiches, coffee, and crisps around the 30-35 mile mark.

name: Mad Gravel
tagline: Colorado plains gravel challenge.
st: Mad Gravel takes place at Peaceful Valley Scout Ranch in Elbert County, Colorado, situated at 6,500-7,000 feet elevation on the Eastern Slope of the Front Range between Colorado Springs and Denver. The 55-mile course features fast-rolling gravel with some challenging corrugations that can sap speed and cause bikes to drift sideways. Riders report sections where the surface becomes more sand than gravel, which significantly slows progress. The course includes some single track sections described as loose and spicy. The altitude at just under 2,000 meters is noticeable and affects performance. The event is part of a 3-day Memorial Day weekend festival with camping, food vendors, live music, and family activities. Weather can impact the race, with heavy rain sometimes forcing course modifications. The venue offers spectacular views of the Colorado Front Range and high peaks.

name: Raid Rockingham
tagline: New Hampshire local gravel challenge.
st: Raid Rockingham is a 50-mile gravel race in SE New Hampshire with 3,500 feet of climbing. Riders describe premium road conditions throughout the course. Key challenges include a steep 13 percent grade climb out of Craftsbury and a 10-mile pavement stretch near Green River Reservoir where riders can stretch their legs before returning to gravel. The race features a flexible start format with a window from 7:45 to 8:15 AM rather than a mass start. The event is mostly self-supported, requiring riders to navigate using cue sheets. Terrain includes mixed surfaces with rocks and roots typical of New England gravel racing. The course passes through beautiful Northeast Kingdom scenery with an after party starting around 11 AM for finishers.

name: Salty Lizard
tagline: Moondust and volcanic terrain through the Silver Island Mountains near Wendover, Utah.
st: The Salty Lizard features a challenging early profile with a short but very steep road climb out of Wendover leading to the first gravel section. The course includes a notorious fast downhill section where gravel bikes slide out, followed by terrain that feels bumpy and fast. Aid station 1 sits at mile 17 near Silver Island pass, which features a fast and rowdy descent where riders nearly lose control. The middle section allows riders to fly at 17-19 mph on dirt through spectacular desert scenery with mountains on the left and Salt Flats on the right. The race splits at 65 and 100 mile options after the Silver Island descent. A major climbing challenge hits around mile 90 in the fourth section. Heart rate management proves critical as the early pace can spike unsustainably high. The course travels through the Silver Island Scenic back way offering beautiful desert mountain views.

name: Silver City Century
tagline: Century ride featuring the Wabash Trace Trail through Silver City, Iowa.
st: The Silver City Century features challenging rollers in the first five miles that riders should pace conservatively. The course includes multiple Class B road sections described as some of the smoothest terrain, even smoother than pavement, with one grass-covered section being particularly scenic. A long south-heading section offers opportunities for riders to use aero bars effectively. Key aid stations are located near Macedonia (mile ~20) with pit toilets and at Willow Slew (mile 45). The course becomes more challenging after the second aid station where gravel turns soft and requires extra energy. The final section includes three hills on soft gravel that non-competitive riders can bypass using the trace. Traffic crossings require caution during faster northern sections. The race has strong community connections with local establishments like Silver Spokes bike shop.

name: Soldier Cutoff Hillduro
tagline: Loess Hills gravel along the Soldier River Cutoff in western Iowa.
st: The transcripts contain limited specific information about the Soldier Cutoff Hillduro race itself. One rider mentions that course changes can affect finishing times even when placement improves, noting their time was slower despite moving from third to second place. The discussions focus primarily on rider backgrounds, with mentions of Nebraska off-road racing experience and participation in various gravel events including Gravel Worlds in Spearfish, South Dakota. Riders discuss cross-training between mountain biking and gravel racing, with technical mountain bike skills helping with gravel racing while gravel endurance training supports mountain biking performance. The conversations include references to Prairie Bloom cycling team and participation in multiple racing formats throughout the season.

name: Trough Creek Gravel Grinder
tagline: Three distances and family festival vibes through Trough Creek State Park gravel.
st: This gravel route in Pennsylvania covers 56 miles with almost 6,000 feet of climbing through the heavily dissected Alageney Plateau. Starting at Cross Creek Park in West Middletown, the course follows old Native American trails along ridges, featuring a patchwork of gravel and lightly traveled pavement. Key sections include the legendary Shades of Death Road and gravel segments in West Virginia along Amspoker Ridge. The route crosses the Pennsylvania-West Virginia border multiple times and passes through historical areas including former coal mining communities. Located 43 minutes southwest of Pittsburgh, this lesser-known gravel area sits between Hillman State Park and the Whiskey Rebellion routes. Riders describe it as a roller coaster route with relatively forgotten gravel segments that only see a handful of cyclists, making it an alternative to Pittsburgh's more popular gravel destinations.

name: Uncle John's Gravel Race
tagline: Uncle John's Gravel Race. 50 miles of gravel through St Johns.
st: Uncle John's Gravel Race in St Johns, Michigan features a challenging finish with a super steep, wet grassy hill that forces many riders to dismount and walk. The race starts with a neutral rollout through an apple orchard with tricky sand sections before hitting gravel roads. Key challenges include flying rocks around mile 32.5 that can damage equipment and hit riders, and a brutal final climb that catches riders off guard. The race distance runs longer than advertised, with the 24-mile option actually closer to 26 miles. Weather conditions include overcast skies with light drizzle. The event draws large fields of 450+ riders and features excellent organization with good support throughout. Post-race festivities include hard cider, fresh donuts, and food trucks at Uncle John's Cider Mill, creating a welcoming community atmosphere for both new and experienced gravel racers.

name: Bald Eagle Gravel Grinder
tagline: Clear streams and mountain ridges through central Pennsylvania's Bald Eagle Valley.
st: No race-related content was found in the provided transcripts for the Bald Eagle Gravel Grinder in Lock Haven, Pennsylvania. The transcripts appear to contain unrelated content about fictional stories rather than cycling race analysis or rider experiences from this gravel event.

name: Glengarry Games Gravel Run
tagline: Rolling gravel through Highland Games country in Maxville, Ontario.
st: The Glengarry Games Gravel Run features a mix of normal gravel roads, significant pavement sections, and trail sections including old logging roads and unmaintained areas. Key highlights include McDonald's Side Road, a favorite shaded section that's narrow and sheltered from wind with minimal traffic. The course incorporates maintained gravel roads through municipally designated conservation forests. Some unmaintained sections may need time to dry out, though riders found them solid even when wet. The race finishes dramatically on the oval track at the Highland Games fairgrounds in front of grand stands with crowds cheering. Heat is a significant factor requiring lots of water and cooling strategies. The event features a neutral start through downtown Maxville before the competitive portion begins. Aid stations provide water support throughout the course.

name: Gravel Unravel - Why-Not-Chee
tagline: Washington coastal gravel challenge.

name: Kalona Horseshoe
tagline: Kalona Horseshoe. 50 miles of gravel through Kalona.
st: The Kalona Horseshoe is described as a mixed surface race featuring big chunky gravel sections that have historically been tackled by riders using road bikes with 25mm tires. The race includes feed zones for mechanical support and nutrition. Riders discuss pacing strategies, with early solo breakaways being considered risky but potentially rewarding if gaps can be established. The event attracts riders from across the Midwest, with some traveling from Wisconsin and other neighboring states. Race morning logistics include packet pickup the night before. The course appears to have some climbing despite initial expectations of flat terrain. Weather conditions have been described as beautiful with great turnout from the cycling community.

name: Mid-State Gravel Grinder
tagline: Gates, switchbacks, and quiet farm country gravel in central Pennsylvania.
st: The Mid-State Gravel Grinder features fantastic roads and nice trails through Pennsylvania terrain. Riders describe a key challenge as a five-mile drag that climbs only 1000 feet over five miles, feeling like a false flat forever. The race can start fast with large groups maintaining 20-24 mph for the first 20 miles, involving tactical racing and drafting. Weather conditions are typically excellent. The event emphasizes community with camping, campfires, guitar playing, and s'mores after racing. Riders praise the camaraderie and group dynamics, with many forming lasting friendships. The multi-day format creates strong bonds between participants who work together throughout the race. Many riders describe it as an immersive gravel experience they want to repeat.

name: Muck n Mac Fest
tagline: Muck n Mac Fest. 55 miles of gravel through Traquair House.
st: The Muck n Mac Festival takes place at Traquair House in Althan, Scotland in the Scottish Borders. According to industry participants, the event has a very nice and lovely setting. The transcript primarily focuses on behind-the-scenes setup the night before the event rather than race-specific details. The location appears to be suitable for camping, with participants setting up tents on site. While this transcript doesn't provide specific course details, terrain conditions, or racing challenges, it confirms the scenic Scottish Borders location and suggests the event has an attractive natural setting that appeals to both industry professionals and riders.

name: Nicolet Hot Days
tagline: Waist-deep river ford and dispersed forest camping in Wisconsin's Nicolet National Forest.

name: Old Fashioned Gravel
tagline: Old Fashioned Gravel. 55 miles of gravel through Hokah.
st: No race-specific information available. The provided transcripts contain content about car driving techniques and running shoe reviews, but do not contain any information about the Old Fashioned Gravel race in Hokah, Minnesota. No course features, terrain conditions, challenges, or race atmosphere details are available from these sources.

name: Ottawa Valley Gravel Experience
tagline: Logging roads and K&P Rail Trail through Ontario's rural backcountry near Calabogie.
st: The Ottawa Valley Gravel Experience near Calabogie features challenging mixed terrain including snowmobile trail sections with leaves hiding rocks, and roads like English Road with banking on inside turns but dangerous off-camber sections on the outside. Riders report packed-down right tire tracks versus loose middle sections that cause tire spin and gravel rooster tails. Key challenges include the final 5 kilometers with gusty wind conditions. The area offers twisty roads with natural scenery and is located 1.75 hours from Ottawa. Riders mention using gravel-specific bikes like the VCI Hatchet with SRAM 1x drivetrains. The event attracts both experienced racers and newcomers attempting their first century distances, with emphasis on having fun and community atmosphere over pure competition.

name: Parker Dam Gravel Grinder
tagline: State park forest roads through rural Clearfield County, Pennsylvania.

name: The Divide
tagline: Manistee National Forest gravel and two-track through northern Michigan.

name: The Insayner
tagline: Snowmobile trails and logging roads through northern Wisconsin's state forest near Sayner.

name: Thunder Bay Thriller
tagline: Two-track and singletrack near Lake Huron's Thunder Bay in northern Michigan.
st: The Thunder Bay Thriller in Alpena, Michigan features surprisingly varied terrain that goes beyond typical gravel racing. Riders describe the course as having much more diversity than expected, with two-track sections that feel like obstacle courses with more challenges than anticipated. The race offers 62-mile, 27-mile, and 16-mile distance options, with the longer course adding a southern loop near Hubbard Lake. Parking opens at 6 a.m. at Thunder Bay Junior High School via Third Avenue, with early arrival recommended for closer spots to the start/finish line. Volunteers are stationed at busier intersections for rider safety. The race attracts around 170 participants and features strong community support, with riders helping each other on course when mechanical issues arise. The event benefits the Rock CF Foundation, a cystic fibrosis nonprofit, with 100% of proceeds supporting patient programs.

name: Wild Gravel
tagline: Australian remote gravel challenge.
st: The provided transcript does not contain content related to the Wild Gravel race in Gnowangerup, Western Australia. The transcript is from a Boulder, Colorado Thursday night cruiser ride video by Ryan Van Duzer, featuring casual urban cycling rather than gravel racing. No information about the 60-mile Wild Gravel race course, terrain conditions, elevation challenges, or racing strategies is available in this transcript. The content focuses on a recreational community bike ride with no connection to the gravel racing event in Western Australia.

name: Dalby Grit
location: Dalby Forest, North York Moors, UK
tagline: Dalby Grit. 50 miles of gravel through Dalby Forest.
st: Dalby Grit is a new 50-mile gravel race in Dalby Forest near York featuring 3500 feet of elevation over three laps. Riders describe steeper climbs than comparable events, with a particularly challenging steep descent followed by a steep ascent midcourse. The course includes chunky gravel sections, twisty 90-degree turns, single track designed to string out the field, and some road sections ideal for nutrition. Weather conditions include 20mph winds with potential drizzle but temperatures above 20 degrees. The race attracts around 400 riders total with 40-50 per age group, creating an intimate atmosphere. The neutralized start is described as very hard with opportunities for strong riders to gain early gaps. Course features both forest roads and sections extending into the North Yorkshire Moors, with generally less severe gravel than other major UK gravel events.

name: Dunoon Dirt Dash
tagline: Dunoon Dirt Dash. 45 miles of gravel through Dunoon.
st: The Dunoon Dirt Dash is a two-day overnight bikepacking event in Scotland with extensive hiker bike sections requiring significant pushing and carrying over long boggy steep hillsides and slippery footpaths. The course features forest roads, single track, coastal roads, and includes stops at Benmore Botanical Gardens cafe. Key challenges include the first day's excessive hiker bike section and six climbs on day 2. Riders can bail out at main roads partway through day 2. The event provides evening meals including venison burgers, eliminating need for camping stoves. The atmosphere emphasizes it's not a race with a don't be a dick philosophy. Second day features some excellent gravel descents through valleys despite the challenging climbs.

name: Dusty Gravel - Lacs de l'Eau d'Heure
tagline: Belgian local gravel challenge.

name: Dusty Gravel Series - Andenne
tagline: Belgian Ardennes singletrack and Mosane valley terrain near Andenne.
st: The Dusty Gravel Series race in Andenne features an extremely challenging start that riders describe as really hard, where you're just trying to hang on before the pace eventually settles. The course includes technical sections with small grass pots that can catch your front wheel and cause crashes. Mechanical issues are common, with riders experiencing problems like pedals falling out and chains dropping multiple times during the race. The race is long enough that group dynamics become crucial - riders emphasize the importance of finding a good group since doing a lot of the race alone is not ideal. After the intense opening, the race typically slows down and groups form, which is when the real racing begins. The level of competition is described as high even at the amateur level, with riders using this race as qualification attempts for World Championships.

name: Haldon Heroic
tagline: Technical bridleways and forest trails through Devon's Haldon Forest, UK.
st: The Haldon Heroic takes riders through Haldon Forest and surrounding Devon countryside on a challenging 85k course with 1282 meters of elevation. The terrain features thick, treacherous mud that riders describe as like cycling through treacle, with bridleways so muddy that many participants walk sections. Key challenges include an extremely steep climb that scatters the field and muddy bog sections that follow tarmac portions. The course offers a mix of forest trails, gravel roads, and racecourse sections, but lacks the fast-flowing gravel roads found in other regions. Navigation can be tricky with missed turns and unclear signage. Gravel bikes struggle in the thick mud conditions where debris gets packed between wheels and frames. Feed stations offer bacon and egg rolls. The forest park allows free riding access for cyclists, with only parking fees for drivers. Riders should expect significantly longer completion times than typical gravel events due to the challenging English terrain and muddy conditions.

name: Nanaimo Unpaved
tagline: Vancouver Island coastal forest gravel and mountain terrain near Nanaimo, BC.
st: The Nanaimo Unpaved takes place near the motocross track and Mount Benson area, featuring gravel roads and trails. The race starts with a challenging uphill section that helps riders warm up in cold January conditions. Key features include a creek crossing where riders drop down after hanging a right. The course was described as running behind Mount Benson with some riders wanting more mud and hills for additional challenge. Weather can be cold and rainy in January, making proper clothing important. Race organizers mention fire risk concerns in hot weather. The event offers multiple distance options and has a welcoming community atmosphere. Riders recommend bringing electrolytes to avoid feeling euphoric then tired after about 45 minutes. The race starts near Dumont Trails and attracts riders from across Vancouver Island including Courtenay.

name: OMG (Oxford MS Gravel)
tagline: Holly Springs National Forest gravel through rolling Mississippi hill country.
st: Oxford Mississippi Gravel is a challenging 50-mile course with nearly 3000 feet of elevation gain and very few flat sections. The race features constant undulation with fast downhills leading straight into uphills. Key challenges include two substantial climbs immediately after the start that catch unprepared riders, requiring a good warm-up. Additional difficult climbs occur around miles 20-22 on the back side of the course. The course includes a 3-mile paved relief section from mile 28-31, but finishes with a steep climb. Riders describe narrow transitions from paved to gravel sections, particularly near a Baptist church in a low-lying muddy area. The race typically starts in freezing conditions but can warm to 50+ degrees. With 200 riders on the start line, the race atmosphere is energetic despite cold Mississippi weather conditions that feel particularly harsh due to humidity.

name: Yorkshire Coast Dirt Dash
tagline: UK Yorkshire coast gravel challenge.
st: The transcripts do not contain information about the Yorkshire Coast Dirt Dash race. Instead, they discuss other cycling routes including the Cantey Way in East Kent. The Cantey Way is described as a 145-mile loop that is mostly paved and traffic-free with some off-road sections that can become muddy in wet conditions. The terrain is relatively flat with few hills that are short and not steep. Off-road sections consist of well-maintained gravel tracks with some flowing single track bridleways. Riders recommend bikes that can accommodate 30mm plus tires, with gravel bikes, hybrids, or touring bikes being ideal. GPS navigation is recommended due to the varied route types. The route offers coastal views, cafe stops, and follows bridleways, cycle tracks, and quiet country lanes around Kent's well-populated areas.

name: Rose City Rampage Gravel Race
tagline: Southern Georgia backroad gravel through the Rose City of Thomasville.

name: Rust Buster Gravel Fondo
tagline: Faulkner-country delta gravel and spring floods through Memphis, Tennessee backroads.
st: No race-specific content available in the provided transcript. The transcript appears to be focused on bicycle restoration and maintenance rather than race coverage or rider experiences from the Rust Buster Gravel Fondo in Memphis, TN.

name: Scratch Ankle Gravel
tagline: Longleaf pine ecosystem gravel through Blackwater and Conecuh National Forests.
st: Scratch Ankle is an annual gravel race in Milton, Florida, organized by Chain Busters and held in the Blackwater River State Forest area. The race offers multiple distance options including 30-mile, 60-mile, and 200-mile routes. The course features a mixture of gravel and asphalt with chunky sandy surface conditions. Riders describe encountering punchy hills throughout the course, particularly on the northern loop above Highway Four. Key landmarks include Paul Barnes Road, which marks the return from the northern section with about 5 miles to the finish. Race conditions can include significant water on course due to rain, and sandy sections appear near the finish line. The event has been running for many years and attracts riders of various skill levels, with separate start times for short and long course groups.
